1: # Lakou — Claude Code Handoff Prompt
2: 
3: Paste this entire block as your **first message** to Claude Code in the Android Studio terminal. It primes Claude with everything it needs to pick up where the planning session left off and start writing real code against the existing scaffold.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: You are picking up work on **Lakou** — a sideloaded Android-only Flutter project management app for Tom Reynolds, sole architect at Green Stories Architecture. The project was scaffolded in a previous Claude Code session and is partially built. Your job is to continue implementation, not re-plan it.
8: 
9: ## What Lakou is, why it exists, and who it's for
10: **Lakou** ("courtyard" in Haitian Kreyòl) is a personal daily-driver mobile-first project management app for one architect running a small practice. The lakou is the courtyard around which a Haitian household lives — the well at its center is where everything in the household converges. That's the metaphor for the app: every text, every receipt, every hour of work converges in one place.
11: 
12: **Tagline:** *Your practice. Your pace. Your app.*
13: 
14: ### The user
15: Tom Reynolds — licensed architect, owner of Green Stories Architecture, runs the firm solo (occasionally with consultants). **Has AuDHD**, which shapes every design decision: the app must be motivating without being aggressive, gentle without being condescending, visual without being noisy. Tom is not deeply technical — he wants buttons and screens, not config files. He uses a Pixel phone, an RTX 5090 desktop, makes TikToks
<truncated 17286 bytes>
Run tests after touching shared code.** From `app/`: `flutter test test/filename_builder_test.dart` should always pass.
150: 
151: ## What to do first
152: 1. `Read` the files in the "Read these first" list above.
153: 2. `Glob` `app/lib/**/*.dart` to see the actual current file tree.
154: 3. Run `flutter analyze` from `app/` and report any errors/warnings to the user.
155: 4. Run `flutter test` and confirm all tests pass.
156: 5. Then ask the user: **"What would you like me to work on first?"** — give them 3-4 concrete options pulled from the open work in `docs/android-studio-handoff.md` § "First 5 tasks for Gemini" (e.g. native `SmsReceiver.kt`, `TimerForegroundService.kt`, end-to-end Dropbox auth test, Project Importer, etc.).
157: 
158: ## Useful commands (run from `app/`)
159: ```powershell
160: flutter pub get
161: flutter analyze
162: flutter test
163: flutter test test/filename_builder_test.dart
164: flutter run                     # against connected Pixel
165: flutter build apk --release     # APK lands in build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/
166: ..\scripts\build-apk.ps1        # also copies to releases/
167: ```
168:

173: - **Dropbox sync target for files the app writes:** `C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Green Stories\<year>\...`
174: - **Sample folder template (don't modify):** `C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Green Stories\Architecture\Sample Folder Structure\`

1: # Lakou — Android Studio Handoff for Gemini
2: 
3: This doc gives Gemini in Android Studio everything it needs to take over native-side work on Lakou. It assumes no prior context. Read it top to bottom before touching code.
4: 
5: ## 1. Project overview
6: 
7: > **Lakou — Your practice. Your pace. Your app.**
8: >
9: > Most project management tools were built for someone else — and then handed to architects. Lakou wasn't. It was built from the actual frustrations of running a small practice: client texts that disappear, fee burn you don't catch until it's too late, folder structures that only make sense if you invented them. Lakou captures the way your work actually moves — from the first client text to the milestone nudge — without forcing you into someone else's system.
10: 
11: **Lakou** ("courtyard" in Haitian Kreyòl) is a sideloaded Android-only project management app for **Tom Reynolds**, sole architect at Green Stories Architecture. It is a personal daily-driver tool, not a Play Store app — it intentionally uses `READ_SMS`, `READ_CONTACTS`, foreground services, and exact alarms that would fail Play review.
12: 
13: **Stack:** Flutter 3.22+ / Dart 3.4+ / Material 3 / Riverpod / go_router. Native side is Kotlin via plugin channels.
14: 
15: **Design intent:** AuDHD-friendly. Light theme. Gentle gamification (XP, streaks, quests). No corporate productivity-theater language. Mobile-first, pocket-sized.
16: 
17: ## 2. Locked decisions
18: 
19: | Area | Choice |
20: |---|---|
21: | Mobile stack | Flutter (Dart) |
22: | Distribution
<truncated 14523 bytes>
1. **Run `flutter create .` inside `app/`** with the org/project flags above. Verify the existing `lib/`, `pubspec.yaml`, `android/build.gradle.kts`, `android/app/build.gradle.kts`, and `AndroidManifest.xml` survive. Run `flutter pub get` and confirm the dependency tree resolves under AGP 9.1.0.
218: 2. **Implement `SmsReceiver.kt`** as described in §7.1, plus its `MethodChannel` on `lakou/sms`. Wire a Dart listener in `lib/features/capture/sms_listener.dart` (new file) that reads watched senders from `settings` and routes incoming texts into `CaptureReviewScreen` via `go_router`.
219: 3. **Implement `TimerForegroundService.kt`** with persistent notification + start/stop channel. Wire `lib/features/timetrack/timer_service.dart` (new file) to call it from `TimetrackScreen`. Persist `time_entries` rows on stop.
220: 4. **Wire Dropbox OAuth.** Build a tiny in-app sign-in screen that runs the PKCE flow, captures the refresh token, and calls `DropboxClient.saveRefreshToken`. Test by uploading a dummy `.txt` to `/Programs/Lakou/data/test.txt`.
221: 5. **End-to-end capture test.** Share a fake SMS like `"site visit Friday foundation pour 23AC-08"` from any text app → Lakou's Capture Review opens → Claude returns a structured note → user confirms `23AC-08` → file lands in `Green Stories/2023/Ascension Capital/23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark - Multifamily/04 Meeting Agendas and Reports/2026-MMDD_23AC-08_…_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`. Verify `captures` table has the row.
222: 
223: After those 5 are green, the remaining roadmap is in `lively-growing-alpaca.md` (the approved plan): New Project wizard, importer, billing/expenses, intake form, dock mode, gamification, Gmail/Calendar.
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: **Questions for Tom (not Gemini):** if anything in this doc is unclear, the source of truth is the approved plan at `C:\Users\thoma\.claude\plans\lively-growing-alpaca.md`. The plan and this handoff doc must stay in sync.

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # AGP 9.1.0 Compliance — baked in from day one
2: 
3: Android Studio kept trying to upgrade Tom's previous projects to AGP 9.1.0 mid-stream and breaking things. Lakou is **born on AGP 9.1.0** so the upgrade prompt is a no-op.
4: 
5: ## What AGP 9 changed that bites Flutter projects
6: 
7: | Change | Old way | Lakou way |
8: |---|---|---|
9: | Manifest `package` attr | `<manifest package="com.example">` | **Removed.** Namespace lives in `app/build.gradle.kts` as `namespace = "com.greenstories.lakou"`. |
10: | Java target | Java 11 | **Java 17** in `compileOptions` and Kotlin `jvmTarget`. |
11: | Gradle | 7.x / 8.0 | **Gradle 8.11.1** via `gradle-wrapper.properties`. |
12: | AGP | 7.x / 8.x | **9.1.0** pinned in `settings.gradle.kts` `plugins {}` block. |
13: | Kotlin | 1.8.x / 1.9.x | **Kotlin 2.0.21**. |
14: | `compileSdk` / `targetSdk` | 33–34 | **35** (Android 15). |
15: | `BuildConfig` generation | on by default | off by default → **explicitly enabled** via `buildFeatures { buildConfig = true }`. |
16: | `buildDir` property | mutable | deprecated → **uses `layout.buildDirectory`** in root `build.gradle.kts`. |
17: | `aaptOptions` / `lintOptions` / `dexOptions` | allowed | **removed** — use `androidResources`, `lint`, packaging blocks instead. |
18: | Foreground services | optional `foregroundServiceType` | **required**. Manifest declares `specialUse` for `TimerForegroundService` with the `PROPERTY_SPECIAL_USE_FGS_SUBTYPE` rationale. |
19: | Non-transitive R classes | optional | **enabled** in `gradle.properties`. |
20: | Configuration cache 
<truncated 49 bytes>
21: | Resource shrinking with code shrinking | implicit | must be **explicit** — `isMinifyEnabled` + `isShrinkResources` set together in `release` buildType. |
22: 
23: ## Files that lock this in
24: - `app/android/settings.gradle.kts` — pins AGP 9.1.0 + Kotlin 2.0.21.
25: - `app/android/build.gradle.kts` — uses `layout.buildDirectory`, no `buildDir`.
26: - `app/android/app/build.gradle.kts` — declares `namespace`, Java 17, `compileSdk` 35, `buildConfig = true`, core library desugaring.
27: - `app/android/gradle.properties` — `nonTransitiveRClass=true`, `nonFinalResIds=true`, configuration cache on, Jetifier off.
28: - `app/android/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties` — Gradle 8.11.1.
29: - `app/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` — **no `package` attribute**, foreground service types declared.
30: 
31: ## Plugin gotcha
32: Some Flutter plugins still hardcode `package` in their own manifests and break AGP 9 builds. If `flutter pub get` brings in a plugin that fails, the fix is one of:
33: 1. Upgrade the plugin to a version published after Aug 2024 (most are AGP-9-ready by now).
34: 2. Add a `pluginManagement` resolver in `settings.gradle.kts` that overrides the namespace (already structured to allow this).
35: 3. Fork the plugin and add `namespace` to its `build.gradle`.
36: 
37: The packages chosen in `pubspec.yaml` (riverpod, go_router, sqflite, dio, receive_sharing_intent, another_telephony, flutter_contacts, image_picker, battery_plus, sensors_plus, wakelock_plus, flutter_local_notifications, audioplayers, confetti, googleapis, google_sign_in) all have AGP-9-compatible releases as of late 2025 — but if any one of them regresses, the workaround above applies.
38: 
39: ## When Gemini in Android Studio offers to "Upgrade AGP"
40: **Decline.** The project is already on the latest. If Studio nags, it's because the wrapper hasn't downloaded yet — run `./gradlew wrapper` once and the prompt goes away.
41:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Lakou
2: ### Your practice. Your pace. Your app.
3: 
4: > Most project management tools were built for someone else — and then handed to architects. Lakou wasn't. It was built from the actual frustrations of running a small practice: client texts that disappear, fee burn you don't catch until it's too late, folder structures that only make sense if you invented them. Lakou captures the way your work actually moves — from the first client text to the milestone nudge — without forcing you into someone else's system.
5: 
6: Sideloaded Android app for the Green Stories architecture firm. Mobile-first project management with SMS-to-project-plan capture, time tracking, billing/expenses, gamification, dock mode, Gmail/Calendar sync, and Dropbox-backed storage.
7: 
8: **Owner:** Tom Reynolds. **Distribution:** sideload only (uses `READ_SMS`, `READ_CONTACTS`, foreground services — will not pass Play Store review, by design).
9: 
10: ## Repository layout
11: 
12: ```
13: TextProjectManagement/
14:   app/        Flutter project (lib/, android/, test/, pubspec.yaml)
15:   data/       SQLite live db (tpm.db) — synced via Dropbox
16:   docs/       README, permissions, filename spec, Android Studio handoff
17:   scripts/    build-apk.ps1
18:   inbox/      Webhook drop folder for client intake form submissions
19: ```
20: 
21: ## First-time setup
22: 
23: 1. Install Flutter SDK (stable channel) and Android Studio with Android SDK 34+.
24: 2. From `app/`, run `flutter create .` once to generate the platform glue (the lib/, pubspec.yaml, android/AndroidManifest.xml in this repo will be preserved/merged).
25: 3. `flutter pub get`
26: 4. Copy `app/lib/core/secrets.example.dart` → `secrets.dart` and fill in:
27:    - Anthropic API key
28:    - Dropbox app key + secret
29:    - Google OAuth client ID
30: 5. `flutter run` against a connected Pixel device (USB debugging on).
31: 
32: ## Build a sideload APK
33: 
34: ```powershell
35: .\scripts\build-apk.ps1
36: ```
37: 
38: Produces `app/build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-release.apk` and copies it to `…\Programs\TextProjectManagement\releases\`.
39: 
40: ## Hand-off to Gemini in Android Studio
41: 
42: See [`android-studio-handoff.md`](android-studio-handoff.md) once it's generated.
43: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Canonical filename + folder rules
2: 
3: ## Project folder names
4: - Developer client: `Green Stories\<year>\<DeveloperFolder>\<jobno>_<project name>\`
5:   - Example: `Green Stories\2023\Ascension Capital\23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark - Multifamily\`
6: - Non-developer: `Green Stories\<year>\<jobno>_<project name>\`
7:   - Example: `Green Stories\2026\26001_Some Project Name\`
8: 
9: ## Job number conventions
10: - **Developer:** `YY` + developer code + `-NN` (e.g. `23AC-08`). Developer code stored on the client record.
11: - **Non-developer:** `YY###` (e.g. `26001`).
12: - Regex (developer): `^\d{2}[A-Z]{2,4}-\d{2,3}$`
13: - Regex (non-developer): `^\d{2}\d{3}$`
14: 
15: ## Standard subfolder template
16: Copied from `Green Stories\Architecture\Sample Folder Structure\` on every new project create:
17: 
18: ```
19: 01 Accounting\
20:   01 Agreements\
21:   02 Invoices\
22:   03 Expenses\
23:   04 Insurance\
24: 02 Marketing\
25: 03 Correspondence\
26: 04 Meeting Agendas and Reports\          <- SMS captures land here
27: 05 Memos and List\
28: 06 Project Data\
29:   01 Project Summary\
30:   02 Project Directory\                  <- project-directory.md
31:   03 Codes\
32:   04 Estimates\
33:   05 Bids and addenda\
34:   06 Schedules\
35:   07 Site data\
36:   08 Reference\
37:   09 Photographs\
38:   XX Other\
39: 07 Drawings\
40: 08 Specifications\
41: 09 Construction Phase\
42:   01 Permits\
43:   02 ASIs, CCDs\
44:   03 Observation reports\
45:   04 Submittals\
46:   05 RFIs\
47:   06 Shop Drawings\
48:   07 Logs\
49:   08 Schedule\
50:   09 Certifications and payments\
51:   10 Change Orders\
52:   11 Closeout\
53:   XX Other\
54: XX Other\
55: ```
56: 
57: ## File naming
58: 
59: ### SMS capture (Text Project Plan)
60: `YYYY-MMDD_<jobno>_<project name>_Text-Projectplan-NN.txt`
61: 
62: Example: `2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`
63: 
64: Rules:
65: - `YYYY-MMDD` is the **capture date** (not message date), with no separator between MM and DD.
66: - `<project name>` is the project's display name (no folder safety stripping beyond filesystem-illegal chars `\\/:*?"<>|`).
67: - `NN` is auto-incremented by listing existing files in the target folder matching the same prefix.
68: - Lands in `04 Meeting Agendas and Reports\`.
69: 
70: ### Expense receipt
71: `YYYY-MMDD_<jobno>_Expense_<vendor>_<amount>.jpg` (+ sibling `.json` metadata)
72: 
73: Example: `2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg`
74: 
75: Lands in `01 Accounting\03 Expenses\`.
76: 
77: ### Project Directory
78: `project-directory.md` and `project-directory.json` in `06 Project Data\02 Project Directory\`. One per project. Updated in place; never date-stamped.
79: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

1: import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
2: import 'package:lakou/features/capture/filename_builder.dart';
3: 
4: void main() {
5:   test('canonical text capture filename matches the firm spec', () {
6:     final name = FilenameBuilder.textCapture(
7:       captureDate: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
8:       jobNo: '23AC-08',
9:       projectName: '521 Springfield Ave, Newark',
10:       iteration: 1,
11:     );
12:     expect(
13:       name,
14:       '2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt',
15:     );
16:   });
17: 
18:   test('iteration zero-pads above 9', () {
19:     final name = FilenameBuilder.textCapture(
20:       captureDate: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
21:       jobNo: '26001',
22:       projectName: 'Test Project',
23:       iteration: 12,
24:     );
25:     expect(name.endsWith('_Text-Projectplan-12.txt'), isTrue);
26:   });
27: 
28:   test('expense receipt filename', () {
29:     final name = FilenameBuilder.expenseReceipt(
30:       spentAt: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
31:       jobNo: '23AC-08',
32:       vendor: 'Home Depot',
33:       amount: 47.83,
34:     );
35:     expect(name, '2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg');
36:   });
37: 
38:   test('illegal filesystem chars are stripped', () {
39:     final name = FilenameBuilder.textCapture(
40:       captureDate: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
41:       jobNo: '23AC-08',
42:       projectName: 'Bad/Name:With*Chars?',
43:       iteration: 1,
44:     );
45:     expect(name.contains('/'), isFalse);
46:     expect(name.contains(':'), isFalse);
47:     expect(name.contains('*'), isFalse);

Total Lines: 49

Showing lines 1 to 49
The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: /// Builds canonical filenames per the firm's standard.
2: /// See `docs/filename-spec.md` for the source of truth.
3: class FilenameBuilder {
4:   /// Strips characters illegal in NTFS/FAT filenames but keeps spaces, commas,
5:   /// and hyphens (Tom's existing folder names depend on those).
6:   static String _safe(String s) =>
7:       s.replaceAll(RegExp(r'[\\/:*?"<>|]'), '').trim();
8: 
9:   static String _yyyymmdd(DateTime d) {
10:     final y = d.year.toString().padLeft(4, '0');
11:     final m = d.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
12:     final dd = d.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
13:     return '$y-$m$dd';
14:   }
15: 
16:   /// Helper for folder listing prefix
17:   static String yyyymmddForPrefix(DateTime d) => _yyyymmdd(d);
18: 
19:   /// Returns the SMS capture filename, e.g.
20:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`.
21:   ///
22:   /// [projectName] is whatever short label the user wants between job number and
23:   /// the suffix — typically the project's display name without the job number.
24:   /// [iteration] is 1-based; pass the next available NN after listing the folder.
25:   static String textCapture({
26:     required DateTime captureDate,
27:     required String jobNo,
28:     required String projectName,
29:     required int iteration,
30:   }) {
31:     final n = iteration.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
32:     return '${_yyyymmdd(captureDate)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_${_safe(projectName)}_Text-Projectplan-$n.txt';
33:   }
34: 
35:   /// Returns the receipt filename, e.g.
36:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg`.
37:   static String expenseReceipt({
38:     required DateTime spentAt,
39:     required String jobNo,
40:     required String vendor,
41:     required double amount,
42:     String extension = 'jpg',
43:   }) {
44:     final amt = amount.toStringAsFixed(2);
45:     final v = _safe(vendor).replaceAll(' ', '');
46:     return '${_yyyymmdd(spentAt)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_Expense_${v}_$amt.$extension';
47:   }
48: }
49:

4: 
5: /// SQLite schema and accessor for Lakou.
6: ///
7: /// The live db file is intended to live inside the user's Dropbox folder
8: /// (`/Programs/Lakou/data/tpm.db`) so the desktop sees it automatically.
9: /// On Android the runtime file is opened from the app's documents dir and
10: /// synced up/down via [DropboxClient]; we never write to the Dropbox path
11: /// directly because Android sandboxing makes that unreliable.
12: class LakouDb {
13:   LakouDb._(this._db);
14:   final Database _db;
15:   Database get raw => _db;
16: 
17:   static Future<LakouDb> open() async {
18:     final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
19:     final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');
20:     final db = await openDatabase(
21:       path,
22:       version: 2,
23:       onCreate: (db, version) async {
24:         await _migrateV1(db);
25:         await _migrateV2(db);
26:       },
27:       onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {
28:         if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);
29:       },
30:     );
31:     return LakouDb._(db);
32:   }
33: 
34:   /// v2: captures get a status column ('pending' | 'assigned' | 'archived').
35:   /// Archived captures are kept as local-only pointers so Tom can resurrect
36:   /// them later from the original SMS/MMS thread.
37:   static Future<void> _migrateV2(Database db) async {
38:     await db.execute(
39:       "ALTER TABLE captures ADD COLUMN status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'",
4

205:         received_at INTEGER,

278:   }
279: 
280:   /// Computes the next iteration NN for a SMS-capture filename in [folder]
281:   /// matching the pattern `<datePrefix>_<jobNo>_..._Text-Projectplan-NN.txt`.
282:   Future<int> nextCaptureIteration({
283:     required String folder,
284:     required String datePrefix, // e.g. 2026-0406
285:     required String jobNo,
286:   }) async {
287:     try {
288:       final names = await listFolder(folder);
289:       final re = RegExp(
290:         '^${RegExp.escape(datePrefix)}_${RegExp.escape(jobNo)}_.*_Text-Projectplan-(\\d{2,})\\.txt\$',
291:       );
292:       var max = 0;
293:       for (final n in names) {
294:         final m = re.firstMatch(n);
295:         if (m != null) {
296:           final v = int.tryParse(m.group(1) ?? '') ?? 0;
297:           if (v > max) max = v;
298:         }
299:       }
300:       return max + 1;
301:     } on DioException {
302:       return 1;
303:     }
304:   }
305: 
306:   Future<void> createFolder(String path) async {
307:     final token = await _accessToken();
308:     await _dio.post(
309:       '$_api/files/create_folder_v2',
310:       options: Options(headers: {'Authorization': 'Bearer $token'}),
311:       data: {'path': path, 'autorename': false},
312:     );
313:   }
314: 
315:   /// Recursively copies [from] to [to]. Used to clone the Sample Folder
316:   /// Structure into a new project folder on creation.
317:   Future<void> copyFolder(String from, String to) async {
318:     final token = await _accessToken();
319:     await _dio.post(
320:       '$_api/files/copy_v2',
321:       options: Options(headers: {'Authorization': 'Bearer $token'}),
322:       data: {'from_path': from, 'to_path': to, 'autorename': false},
323:     );
324:   }
325: }
326:

7: class SmsListener {
8:   static const _channel = MethodChannel('lakou/sms');
9:   final Ref ref;
10: 
11:   SmsListener(this.ref);
12: 
13:   void init() {
14:     Trace.log('sms', 'listener init');
15:     // Ask the host for SMS + contacts permissions on first launch.
16:     // No-op if already granted.
17:     _channel.invokeMethod('requestPermissions').catchError((e) {
18:       Trace.log('sms', 'requestPermissions error', data: e);
19:       return null;
20:     });
21: 
22:     _channel.setMethodCallHandler((call) async {
23:       Trace.log('sms', 'channel call ${call.method}');
24:       if (call.method == 'onSmsReceived') {
25:         final Map<dynamic, dynamic> args = call.arguments;
26:         final String sender = args['sender'] ?? 'Unknown';
27:         final String? displayName = args['displayName'] as String?;
28:         final String body = args['body'] ?? '';
29:         final int timestamp = args['timestamp'] ?? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch;
30: 
31:         Trace.log('sms', 'onSmsReceived',
32:             data: 'from=${displayName ?? sender} len=${body.length}');
33: 
34:         // Watched-senders gate. Empty list = capture all (default during
35:         // shake-out). Once Tom curates a list, only matching senders flow
36:         // into the capture screen — others stay logged in the inbox.
37:         final watched = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
38:         if (!senderMatches(watched: watched, sender: sender, displayName: displayName)) {
39:           Trace.log('sms', 'filtered (not watched)', data: displayName ?? sender);
40:           return;
41:         }
42: 
43:         ref.read(incomingCaptureProvider.notifier).state = IncomingCapture(
44:           rawText: body,
45:           sender: sender,
46:           displayName: displayName,
47:           capturedAt: DateTime.fromMillisecondsSinceEpoch(timestamp),
48:         );
49:       }
50:     });
51:   }
52: }

174:             // MMS provider can be flaky on some OEMs — never let it kill the SMS list.
175:         }
176: 
177:         // Sort merged list newest-first and trim.
178:         merged.sortByDescending { it["timestamp"] as Long }
179:         return if (merged.size > limit) merged.subList(0, limit) else merged
180:     }
181: 
182:     private fun isNotificationAccessGranted(): Boolean {
183:         val enabled = NotificationManagerCompat.getEnabledListenerPackages(this)
184:         return enabled.contains(packageName)
185:     }
186: 
187:     @Suppress("unused")
188:     private fun rcsListenerComponent() =
189:         ComponentName(this, RcsNotificationListener::class.java)
190: 
191:     private fun requestRuntimePermissions() {
192:         val needed = mutableListOf<String>()
193:         val wanted = listOf(
194:             Manifest.permission.READ_SMS,
195:             Manifest.permission.RECEIVE_SMS,
196:             Manifest.permission.READ_CONTACTS
197:         )
198:         for (p in wanted) {
199:             if (ActivityCompat.checkSelfPermission(this, p) != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED) {
200:                 needed.add(p)
201:             }
202:         }
203:         if (needed.isNotEmpty()) {
204:             ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(this, needed.toTypedArray(), PERMISSION_REQUEST_CODE)
205:         }
206:     }
207: }

1: package com.greenstories.lakou
2: 
3: import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
4: import android.content.Context
5: import android.content.Intent
6: import android.provider.Telephony
7: import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodChannel
8: 
9: class SmsReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
10:     companion object {
11:         var methodChannel: MethodChannel? = null
12:     }
13: 
14:     override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
15:         if (intent.action != Telephony.Sms.Intents.SMS_RECEIVED_ACTION) return
16: 
17:         // Multi-part SMS arrive as multiple PDUs from the same sender; concatenate
18:         // their bodies so the Dart side sees one capture instead of N fragments.
19:         val messages = Telephony.Sms.Intents.getMessagesFromIntent(intent) ?: return
20:         if (messages.isEmpty()) return
21: 
22:         val sender = messages.first().displayOriginatingAddress
23:         val body = messages.joinToString(separator = "") { it.displayMessageBody ?: "" }
24:         // Use the message's own timestamp (UTC ms), not wall-clock now — broadcasts
25:         // can be delivered late after a doze wake-up.
26:         val timestamp = messages.first().timestampMillis
27:         val displayName = ContactResolver.displayNameFor(context, sender)
28: 
29:         methodChannel?.invokeMethod(
30:             "onSmsReceived",
31:             mapOf(
32:                 "sender" to sender,
33:                 "displayName" to displayName,
34:                 "body" to body,
35:                 "timestamp" to timestamp,
36:                 "type" to "sms"
37:             )
38:         )
39:     }
40: }

6: import android.content.pm.PackageManager
7: import android.net.Uri
8: import android.provider.ContactsContract
9: import androidx.core.content.ContextCompat
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Resolves a phone number to a contact display name via ContactsContract.PhoneLookup.
13:  *
14:  * Returns null if:
15:  *   - READ_CONTACTS is not granted
16:  *   - the number is null/blank
17:  *   - no contact matches
18:  *
19:  * PhoneLookup handles number normalization (country code, formatting) for us, so a
20:  * raw "+1 973 555 0123" from an SMS will match a contact saved as "(973) 555-0123".
21:  */
22: object ContactResolver {
23:     fun displayNameFor(context: Context, number: String?): String? {
24:         if (number.isNullOrBlank()) return null
25:         if (ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(context, Manifest.permission.READ_CONTACTS)
26:             != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED
27:         ) return null
28: 
29:         val uri = Uri.withAppendedPath(
30:             ContactsContract.PhoneLookup.CONTENT_FILTER_URI,
31:             Uri.encode(number)
32:         )
33:         val resolver: ContentResolver = context.contentResolver
34:         return runCatching {
35:             resolver.query(
36:                 uri,
37:                 arrayOf(ContactsContract.PhoneLookup.DISPLAY_NAME),
38:                 null, null, null
39:             )?.use { c ->
40:                 if (c.moveToFirst()) c.getString(0) else null
41:             }
42:         }.getOrNull()
43:     }
44: }

10: /**

15:  *   2. `content://mms/part`       — one row per MIME part; the text body lives
16:  *                                   in a part with content_type "text/plain"
17:  *   3. `content://mms/<id>/addr`  — one row per address; sender has type=137
18:  *                                   (PduHeaders.FROM)

20:  * The DATE column on MMS rows is in **seconds**, unlike SMS which uses
21:  * milliseconds. We normalize to ms here so the Dart side never has to think
22:  * about it.
23:  */

25:     private const val FROM_TYPE = 137 // PduHeaders.FROM
26:     private const val TYPE_PLAIN = "text/plain"
27: 
28:     data class MmsMessage(val id: Long, val sender: String?, val body: String, val timestampMs: Long)
29:

31:         val resolver = context.contentResolver
32:         val out = mutableListOf<MmsMessage>()
33: 
34:         val projection = arrayOf(Telephony.Mms._ID, Telephony.Mms.DATE)
35:         val cursor = if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
36:             val arg

55:             }
56:         }
57:         return out
58:     }
59: 
60:     private fun bodyFor(resolver: ContentResolver, mmsId: Long): String? {
61:         val uri = Uri.parse("content://mms/part")
62:         val sb = StringBuilder()
63:         resolver.query(
64:             uri,
65:             arrayOf("_id", "ct", "_data", "text"),
66:             "mid=?",
67:             arrayOf(mmsId.toString()),
68:             null
69:         )?.use { c ->
70:             while (c.moveToNext()) {
71:                 val ct = c.getString(1) ?: continue
72:                 if (ct != TYPE_PLAIN) continue
73:                 val data = c.getString(2)
74:                 val text = if (data != null) {
75:                     // Body is stored as a file blob — read it.
76:                     runCatching {
77:                         resolver.openInputStream(Uri.parse("content://mms/part/${c.getLong(0)}"))
78:                             ?.bufferedReader()?.use { it.readText() }
79:                     }.getOrNull() ?: ""
80:                 } else {
81:                     c.getString(3) ?: ""
82:                 }
83:                 if (sb.isNotEmpty()) sb.append('\n')
84:                 sb.append(text)
85:             }
86:         }
87:         return if (sb.isEmpty()) null else sb.toString()
88:     }
89: 
90:     private fun senderFor(resolver: ContentResolver, mmsId: Long): String? {
91:         val uri = Uri.parse("content://mms/$mmsId/addr")

93:             uri,
94:             arrayOf("address"),
95:             "type=$FROM_TYPE",
96:             null, null
97:         )?.use { c ->
98:             if (c.moveToFirst()) c.getString(0) else null

1: package com.greenstories.lakou
2: 
3: import android.app.Notification
4: import android.service.notification.NotificationListenerService
5: import android.service.notification.StatusBarNotification
6: 
7: /**
8:  * Best-effort RCS capture via NotificationListenerService.
9:  *
10:  * RCS messages live in Google Messages' private app sandbox and have NO public
11:  * API on Android. The only way a third-party app can see them is by scraping
12:  * the **notification text** that Google Messages posts to the system tray.
13:  *
14:  * Caveats Tom should know about:
15:  *  - Only catches messages that fire a notification (silenced threads = invisible).
16:  *  - Long messages can be truncated by the notification body.
17:  *  - Group chats post one notification per sender turn — works, but the "sender"
18:  *    is the contact name as Google Messages displays it, not a phone number.
19:  *  - Notification access is a separate user grant: Settings → Notifications →
20:  *    Special access → Notification access → Lakou. The MainActivity exposes a
21:  *    method to open that page directly.
22:  *  - This is fragile: if Google changes the notification template, this breaks.
23:  */
24: class RcsNotificationListener : NotificationListenerService() {
25: 
26:     override fun onNotificationPosted(sbn: StatusBarNotification) {
27:         if (sbn.packageName != GOOGLE_MESSAGES_PKG) return
28: 
29:         val extras = sbn.notification.extras
30:         val title = extras.getCharSequence(Notification.EXTRA_TITLE)?.toString()
31:         // Prefer BIG_TEXT (full body) over TEXT (single-line preview).
32:         val body = (extras.getCharSequence(Notification.EXTRA_BIG_TEXT)
33:             ?: extras.getCharSequence(Notification.EXTRA_TEXT))?.toString()
34: 
35:         if (title.isNullOrBlank() || body.isNullOrBlank()) return
36: 
37:         // Skip the persistent "X new messages" summary notifications.
38:         if (body.matches(Regex("^\\d+ new messages?$"))) return
39:         if (title == "Messages") return
40: 
41:         SmsReceiver.methodChannel?.invokeMethod(
42:             "onSmsReceived",
43:             mapOf(
44:                 "sender" to title, // Notification title is the contact name
45:                 "displayName" to title,
46:                 "body" to body,
47:                 "timestamp" to sbn.postTime,
48:                 "type" to "rcs"
49:             )
50:         )
51:     }
52: 
53:     companion object {
54:         private const val GOOGLE_MESSAGES_PKG = "com.google.android.apps.messaging"
55:     }
56: }
57:

11:

13:     this.isActive = false,
14:     this.projectName,
15:     this.projectId,
16:     this.startTime,
17:   });
18: 
19:   TimerState copyWith({
20:     bool? isActive,
21:     String? projectName,
22:     int? projectId,
23:     DateTime? startTime,
24:   }) {
25:     return TimerState(
26:       isActive: isActive ?? this.isActive,
27:       projectName: projectName ?? this.projectName,
28:       projectId: projectId ?? this.projectId,
29:       startTime: startTime ?? this.startTime,
30:     );
31:   }
32: }
33: 
34: class TimerService extends StateNotifier<TimerState> {
35:   final Ref _ref;
36:   TimerService(this._ref) : super(TimerState());
37: 
38:   static const _channel = MethodChannel('lakou/timer');
39: 
40:   Future<void> startTimer({required int projectId, required String projectName}) async {
41:     try {
42:       await _channel.invokeMethod('startTimer', {'projectName': projectName});
43:       state = TimerState(
44:         isActive: true,
45:         projectName: projectName,
46:         projectId: projectId,
47:         startTime: DateTime.now(),
48:       );
49:     } on PlatformException catch (e) {
50:       debugPrint("Failed to start timer: '${e.message}'.");
51:     }
52:   }
53: 
54:   Future<void> stopTimer() async {
55:     try {
56:       final endTime = DateTime.now();
57:       final startTime = state.startTime;
58:       final projectId = state.projectId;
59: 
60:       await _channel.invokeMethod('stopTimer');
61: 
62:       if (startTime != null && projectId != null) {
63:         final seconds = endTime.difference(startTime).inSeconds;
64:         if (seconds > 0) {
65:           final db = await _ref.read(dbProvider.future);
66:           await db.raw.insert('time_entries', {
67:             'project_id': projectId,
68:             'category': 'design', // Default for now
69:             'started_at': startTime.millisecondsSinceEpoch,
70:             'ended_at': endTime.millisecondsSinceEpoch,
71:             'seconds': seconds,
72:             'note': '',
73:           });
74:         }
75:       }
76: 
77:       state = TimerState(isActive: false);
78:     } on PlatformException catch (e) {
79:       debugPrint("Failed to stop timer: '${e.message}'.");
80:     }
81:   }
82: }
83: 
84: final timerServiceProvider = StateNotifierProvider<TimerService, TimerState>((ref) => TimerService(ref));
85:

6: /// One-shot importer that scans `Green Stories\<year>\**` and seeds the

9: /// Folder layout we expect (per `docs/filename-spec.md`):

13: ///
14: /// Job number formats:
15: ///   developer:     ^\d{2}[A-Z]{2,4}-\d{2,3}$    (e.g. 23AC-08)
16: ///   non-developer: ^\d{5}$                       (e.g. 26001)
17: ///
18: /// We never overwrite an existing `projects` row — `job_no` is UNIQUE, so a
19: /// re-run is idempotent (`INSERT OR IGNORE`). Skips the special
20: /// `Architecture/` folder under any year (that's where the Sample Folder
21: /// Structure lives) and skips anything that doesn't look like a project.
22: class ProjectImporter {

28:   // Captures the job-number prefix, optional separator, then the rest as name.
29:   static final _folderName = RegExp(r'^([0-9A-Z\-]+)[_\s]+(.+)$');
30: 
31:   /// Scans [year] under `/Green St

120: 
121:   /// Inserts every project from [scan]ned results that isn't already in the DB.
122:   /// Returns the number of new rows inserted.
123:   Future<int> importInto(LakouDb db, List<ImportedProject> projects) async {
124:     var inserted = 0;
125:     for (final p in projects) {
126:       // Dedupe by either job_no OR dropbox_path — same folder shouldn't end
127:       // up in the table twice even if the synthesized job number differs.
128:       final existing = await db.raw.query(

131:         whereArgs: [p.jobNo, p.dropboxPath],
132:         limit: 1,
133:       );
134:       if (existing.isNotEmpty) continue;
135:       await db.raw.insert('projects', {
136:         'job_no': p.jobNo,
137:         'name': p.name,
138:         'start_year': p.year,
139:         'dropbox_path': p.dropboxPath,
140:         'phase': 'SD',
141:         'status': 'active',
142:         'created_at': DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch,
143:       });
144:       inserted++;
145:     }
146:     return inserted;
147:   }
148: }

10: class DropboxAuthNotifier extends StateNotifier<AsyncValue<void>> {
11:   DropboxAuthNotifier(this.client) : super(const AsyncValue.data(null));
12: 
13:   final DropboxClient client;
14:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
15:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
16:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
17:       resetOnError: true,
18:     ),
19:   );
20:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
21: 
22:   Future<void> signIn() async {
23:     state = const AsyncValue.loading();
24:     try {
25:       final codeVerifier = _generateCodeVerifier();
26:       await _storage.write(key: _verifierKey, value: codeVerifier);
27: 
28:       final codeChallenge = _generateCodeChallenge(codeVerifier);
29: 
30:       final url = Uri.https('www.dropbox.com', '/oauth2/authorize', {
31:         'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
32:         'response_type': 'code',
33:         'code_challenge': codeChallenge,
34:         'code_challenge_method': 'S256',
35:         'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
36:         'token_access_type': 'offline',
37:       });
38: 
39:       if (await canLaunchUrl(url)) {
40:         await launchUrl(url, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);
41:       } else {
42:         throw Exception('Could not launch Dropbox auth URL');
43:       }
44:       
45:       // Note: Token exchange happens via deep link handler (to be implemented)
46:       state = const AsyncValue.data(null);
47:     } catch (e, st) {
48:       state = AsyncValue.error(e, st);
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   String _generateCodeVerifier() {
53:     final random = Random.secure();
54:     final values = List<int>.generate(32, (i) => random.nextInt(256));
55:     return base64UrlEncode(values).replaceAll('=', '');
56:   }
57: 
58:   String _generateCodeChallenge(String verifier) {
59:     final bytes = utf8.encode(verifier);
60:     final digest = sha256.convert(bytes);
61:     return base64UrlEncode(digest.bytes).replaceAll('=', '');
62:   }
63: }
64:

14:   final Ref ref;
15:   late final _appLinks = AppLinks();
16:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
17:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
18:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
19:       resetOnError: true,
20:     ),
21:   );
22:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
23: 
24:   void init() {
25:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Initializing...');
26:     
27:     // Handle deep links when the app is already running
28:     _appLinks.uriLinkStream.listen((uri) {
29:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Stream URI: $uri');
30:       _processUri(uri);
31:     });
32: 
33:     // Handle deep link that launched the app
34:     _appLinks.getInitialLink().then((uri) {
35:       if (uri != null) {
36:         debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Initial URI: $uri');
37:         _processUri(uri);
38:       }
39:     });
40:   }
41: 
42:   void _processUri(Uri uri) {
43:     if (uri.scheme == 'lakou' && uri.host == 'oauth') {
44:       final code = uri.queryParameters['code'];
45:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code found: ${code != null}');
46:       if (code != null) {
47:         _handleDropboxCode(code);
48:       }
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> _handleDropboxCode(String code) async {
53:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Exchanging code for token...');
54:     try {
55:       final codeVerifier = await _storage.read(key: _verifierKey);
56:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code Verifier found: ${codeVerifier != null}');
57:       final dio = Dio();
58:       final res = await dio.post(
59:         'https://api.dropbox.com/oauth2/token',
60:         options: Options(contentType: Headers.formUrlEncodedContentType),
61:         data: {
62:           'code': code,
63:           'grant_type': 'authorization_code',
64:           'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
65:           'client_secret': Secrets.dropboxAppSecret,
66:           'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
67:           'code_verifier': codeVerifier,
68:         },
69:       );
70: 
71:       final refreshToken = res.data['refresh_token'] as String;
72:       final client = ref.read(dropboxClientProvider);
73:       await client.saveRefreshToken(refreshToken);
74:       
75:       // Invalidate the auth state so the UI updates
76:       ref.invalidate(dropboxAuthStateProvider);
77:       
78:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Success: Access Token Acquired');
79:     } catch (e) {
80:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error: $e');
81:     }
82:   }
83: }
84:

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import '../../core/secrets.dart';
3: import '../../core/trace.dart';
4: import 'claude_extractor.dart';
5: import 'capture_provider.dart';
6: 
7: final claudeExtractorProvider = Provider((ref) => ClaudeExtractor(Secrets.anthropicApiKey));
8: 
9: final extractionProvider = FutureProvider.autoDispose<ExtractedCapture>((ref) async {
10:   final capture = ref.watch(incomingCaptureProvider);
11:   if (capture == null) {
12:     Trace.log('claude', 'extraction skipped: no capture');
13:     throw Exception('No capture to extract');
14:   }
15: 
16:   Trace.log('claude', 'extract start',
17:       data: 'from=${capture.senderLabel} len=${capture.rawText.length}');
18:   final extractor = ref.read(claudeExtractorProvider);
19:   try {
20:     final result = await extractor.extract(capture.rawText, sender: capture.sender);
21:     Trace.log('claude', 'extract ok',
22:         data: 'job=${result.jobNoGuess} actions=${result.actionItems.length}');
23:     return result;
24:   } catch (e) {
25:     Trace.log('claude', 'extract err', data: e);
26:     rethrow;
27:   }
28: });
29:

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-04 04:11:28 UTC)

The full audit is in the artifact. Here's the headline summary:

## What I found

**The app compiles and all tests pass**, but there are **6 critical bugs** that will cause wrong behavior or crashes in daily use:

1. **Intake files save to the wrong Dropbox folder** — `config.dart` says `03 Correspondence` but the spec says `06 Project Data/02 Project Directory`
2. **Expense filenames completely ignore the locked `FilenameBuilder`** — wrong date format, missing job number, missing `_Expense_` keyword, no JSON sidecar
3. **New project folder paths use a space instead of underscore** and skip developer-client nesting
4. **Timer will crash on Android 14+** — missing `foregroundServiceType` in `startForeground()` call
5. **`setState()` called during build** in the capture review screen
6. **Claude extractor crashes on malformed AI responses** — no guard against missing JSON

Beyond those, there are **9 significant issues** (hardcoded timer category, `dbProvider` in wrong file, stale docs everywhere, 3 native services never implemented) and **12 minor code quality items**.

The audit also maps every feature in the handoff plan to its implementation status — SMS capture and the project list are solid, but dock mode auto-trigger, break alarms, Gmail/Calendar, and gamification are still unimplemented shells.

**Want me to start fixing the 6 critical bugs (Phase 1)?** They're all straightforward fixes that won't change the app's architecture.

---

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-04 04:11:28 UTC)

The full audit is in the artifact. Here's the headline summary:

## What I found

**The app compiles and all tests pass**, but there are **6 critical bugs** that will cause wrong behavior or crashes in daily use:

1. **Intake files save to the wrong Dropbox folder** — `config.dart` says `03 Correspondence` but the spec says `06 Project Data/02 Project Directory`
2. **Expense filenames completely ignore the locked `FilenameBuilder`** — wrong date format, missing job number, missing `_Expense_` keyword, no JSON sidecar
3. **New project folder paths use a space instead of underscore** and skip developer-client nesting
4. **Timer will crash on Android 14+** — missing `foregroundServiceType` in `startForeground()` call
5. **`setState()` called during build** in the capture review screen
6. **Claude extractor crashes on malformed AI responses** — no guard against missing JSON

Beyond those, there are **9 significant issues** (hardcoded timer category, `dbProvider` in wrong file, stale docs everywhere, 3 native services never implemented) and **12 minor code quality items**.

The audit also maps every feature in the handoff plan to its implementation status — SMS capture and the project list are solid, but dock mode auto-trigger, break alarms, Gmail/Calendar, and gamification are still unimplemented shells.

**Want me to start fixing the 6 critical bugs (Phase 1)?** They're all straightforward fixes that won't change the app's architecture.

1: import 'dart:convert';
2: import 'package:dio/dio.dart';
3: import '../../core/config.dart';
4: 
5: /// Structured result from Claude after parsing an SMS / shared text.
6: class ExtractedCapture {
7:   ExtractedCapture({
8:     required this.jobNoGuess,
9:     required this.summary,
10:     required this.actionItems,
11:     required this.dates,
12:     required this.markdown,
13:   });
14:   final String? jobNoGuess;
15:   final String summary;
16:   final List<String> actionItems;
17:   final List<String> dates;
18:   final String markdown;
19: 
20:   factory ExtractedCapture.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> j) => ExtractedCapture(
21:         jobNoGuess: j['job_no'] as String?,
22:         summary: (j['summary'] as String?) ?? '',
23:         actionItems: ((j['action_items'] as List?) ?? const []).cast<String>(),
24:         dates: ((j['dates'] as List?) ?? const []).cast<String>(),
25:         markdown: (j['markdown'] as String?) ?? '',
26:       );
27: }
28: 
29: /// Calls the Anthropic API to turn raw SMS / shared text into a structured
30: /// project plan note. Stays minimal — the prompt does the heavy lifting.
31: class ClaudeExtractor {
32:   ClaudeExtractor(this.apiKey);
33:   final String apiKey;
34: 
35:   static const _endpoint = 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages';
36:   final _dio = Dio();
37: 
38:   static const _systemPrompt = '''
39: You extract structured project notes for an architecture firm from raw text messages.
40: 
41: The firm uses job numbers like "23AC-08" (year + developer code + sequence) or "26001" (year + sequence). Always look for one in the input.
42: 
43: Return ONLY valid JSON matching this schema:
44: {
45:   "job_no": "string or null",
46:   "summary": "one-line summary",
47:   "action_items": ["bullet", "bullet"],
48:   "dates": ["any dates mentioned in ISO form"],
49:   "markdown": "a clean markdown body to save as the note file"
50: }
51: 
52: The markdown body should have sections: ## Summary, ## Action Items, ## Dates, ## Original Message.
53: Be concise. Do not invent details.
54: ''';
55: 
56:   Future<ExtractedCapture> extract(String rawText, {String? sender}) async {
57:     final res = await _dio.post(
58:       _endpoint,
59:       options: Options(headers: {
60:         'x-api-key': apiKey,
61:         'anthropic-version': '2023-06-01',
62:         'content-type': 'application/json',
63:       }),
64:       data: {
65:         'model': LakouConfig.claudeModel,
66:         'max_tokens': 1024,
67:         'system': _systemPrompt,
68:         'messages': [
69:           {
70:             'role': 'user',
71:             'content': 'Sender: ${sender ?? "unknown"}\n\nMessage:\n$rawText',
72:           }
73:         ],
74:       },
75:     );
76:     final content = (res.data['content'] as List).first['text'] as String;
77:     final jsonStart = content.indexOf('{');
78:     final jsonEnd = content.lastIndexOf('}');
79:     final parsed = jsonDecode(content.substring(jsonStart, jsonEnd + 1)) as Map<String, dynamic>;
80:     return ExtractedCapture.fromJson(parsed);
81:   }
82: }
83:

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-04 04:13:33 UTC)

Created At: 2026-06-04T04:13:33Z
Completed At: 2026-06-04T04:13:33Z
File Path: `file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/capture/filename_builder.dart`
Total Lines: 49
Total Bytes: 1848
Showing lines 1 to 49
The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: /// Builds canonical filenames per the firm's standard.
2: /// See `docs/filename-spec.md` for the source of truth.
3: class FilenameBuilder {
4:   /// Strips characters illegal in NTFS/FAT filenames but keeps spaces, commas,
5:   /// and hyphens (Tom's existing folder names depend on those).
6:   static String _safe(String s) =>
7:       s.replaceAll(RegExp(r'[\\/:*?"<>|]'), '').trim();
8: 
9:   static String _yyyymmdd(DateTime d) {
10:     final y = d.year.toString().padLeft(4, '0');
11:     final m = d.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
12:     final dd = d.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
13:     return '$y-$m$dd';
14:   }
15: 
16:   /// Helper for folder listing prefix
17:   static String yyyymmddForPrefix(DateTime d) => _yyyymmdd(d);
18: 
19:   /// Returns the SMS capture filename, e.g.
20:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`.
21:   ///
22:   /// [projectName] is whatever short label the user wants between job number and
23:   /// the suffix — typically the project's display name without the job number.
24:   /// [iteration] is 1-based; pass the next available NN after listing the folder.
25:   static String textCapture({
26:     required DateTime captureDate,
27:     required String jobNo,
28:     required String projectName,
29:     required int iteration,
30:   }) {
31:     final n = iteration.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
32:     return '${_yyyymmdd(captureDate)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_${_safe(projectName)}_Text-Projectplan-$n.txt';
33:   }
34: 
35:   /// Returns the receipt filename, e.g.
36:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg`.
37:   static String expenseReceipt({
38:     required DateTime spentAt,
39:     required String jobNo,
40:     required String vendor,
41:     required double amount,
42:     String extension = 'jpg',
43:   }) {
44:     final amt = amount.toStringAsFixed(2);
45:     final v = _safe(vendor).replaceAll(' ', '');
46:     return '${_yyyymmdd(spentAt)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_Expense_${v}_$amt.$extension';
47:   }
48: }
49: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

Showing lines 1 to 61
The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Launch Claude Code in the Lakou Flutter project with the handoff prompt
2: # pre-loaded so the new session has full context immediately.
3: #
4: # Usage (from anywhere):
5: #   .\scripts\start-claude.ps1
6: #
7: # Or from inside Android Studio's terminal at the repo root:
8: #   .\scripts\start-claude.ps1
9: 
10: $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
11: 
12: $repoRoot   = Split-Path -Parent $PSScriptRoot
13: $appDir     = Join-Path $repoRoot 'app'
14: $promptPath = Join-Path $repoRoot 'HANDOFF_PROMPT.md'
15: 
16: if (-not (Test-Path $promptPath)) {
17:     Write-Host "HANDOFF_PROMPT.md not found at $promptPath" -ForegroundColor Red
18:     exit 1
19: }
20: 
21: # Verify claude CLI is on PATH; install instructions if not.
22: $claude = Get-Command claude -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
23: if (-not $claude) {
24:     Write-Host ""
25:     Write-Host "Claude Code CLI is not installed." -ForegroundColor Yellow
26:     Write-Host "Install it with:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
27:     Write-Host "  npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code" -ForegroundColor Cyan
28:     Write-Host "Then close and reopen this terminal so PATH refreshes, and re-run this script." -ForegroundColor Yellow
29:     exit 1
30: }
31: 
32: # Sanity check: Flutter SDK on PATH (or known install location).
33: $flutter = Get-Command flutter -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
34: if (-not $flutter) {
35:     $localFlutter = 'C:\Users\thoma\dev\flutter\bin\flutter.bat'
36:     if (Test-Path $localFlutter) {
37:         Write-Host "Note: Flutter not on PATH for this terminal, but installed at $localFlutter" -ForegroundColor DarkYellow
38:         Write-Host "If commands fail, open a fresh PowerShell so PATH picks it up." -ForegroundColor DarkYellow
39:     } else {
40:         Write-Host "Flutter SDK not found. Install or add to PATH before running Claude tasks." -ForegroundColor Yellow
41:     }
42: }
43: 
44: Write-Host ""
45: Write-Host "Lakou — launching Claude Code with handoff prompt..." -ForegroundColor Green
46: Write-Host "  Working dir: $appDir"
47: Write-Host "  Prompt file: $promptPath"
48: Write-Host ""
49: 
50: # cd into the Flutter project so all relative paths in the prompt resolve.
51: Set-Location $appDir
52: 
53: # Load the prompt and pipe it as the first message. The trailing newline keeps
54: # the CLI happy. Use -p (print) to send a one-shot first prompt, OR use
55: # interactive mode by piping. We use interactive mode so the user can keep
56: # chatting after the initial primer.
57: $prompt = Get-Content $promptPath -Raw
58: 
59: # claude reads from stdin in interactive mode; echo the prompt then hand off.
60: Write-Output $prompt | claude
61: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

  "CodeContent": "\"# Sync conversation logs and artifacts from the .gemini folder to the docs/system_generated_logs folder.\\n# This ensures conversations are tracked inside the project repository.\\n\\n$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'\\n\\n$repoRoot = Split-Path -Parent $PSScriptRoot\\n$docsLogsDir = Join-Path $repoRoot \\\"docs\\\\system_generated_logs\\\"\\n\\n# Ensure destination directory exists\\nif (-not (Test-Path $docsLogsDir)) {\\n    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $docsLogsDir | Out-Null\\n    Write-Host \\\"Created directory: $docsLogsDir\\\" -ForegroundColor Green\\n}\\n\\n# Locate local .gemini brain directory\\n$userProfile = [System.Environment]::GetFolderPath('UserProfile')\\n$brainDir = Join-Path $userProfile \\\".gemini\\\\antigravity\\\\brain\\\"\\n\\nif (-not (Test-Path $brainDir)) {\\n    Write-Host \\\"Antigravity brain directory not found at $brainDir\\\" -ForegroundColor Yellow\\n    exit 0\\n}\\n\\nWrite-Host \\\"Syncing conversations from $brainDir to $docsLogsDir...\\\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\\n\\n# Find all conversation directories\\n$conversations = Get-ChildItem -Path $brainDir -Directory\\n\\nforeach ($conv in $conversations) {\\n    $convId = $conv.Name\\n    $destConvDir = Join-Path $docsLogsDir $convId\\n\\n    $filesToSync = @()\\n\\n    # Check for artifacts at the root of the conversation\\n    $artifactFiles = @(\\\"implementation_plan.md\\\", \\\"task.md\\\", \\\"walkthrough.md\\\")\\n    foreach ($file in $artifactFiles) {\\n        $filePath = Join-Path $conv.FullName $file\\n        if (Test-Path $filePath) {\\n            $filesToSync += [PSCustomObject]@{\\n                SourcePath = $filePath\\n                DestPath   = Join-Path $destConvDir $file\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    # Check for transcript.jsonl\\n    $transcriptPath = Join-Path $conv.FullName \\\".system_generated\\\\logs\\\\transcript.jsonl\\\"\\n    if (Test-Path $transcriptPath) {\\n        $filesToSync += [PSCustomObject]@{\\n            SourcePath = $transcriptPath\\n            DestPath   = Join-Path $destConvDir \\\"transcript.jsonl\\\"\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    if ($filesToSync.Count -gt 0) {\\n\n<truncated 503 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Create the sync-logs script to copy conversations and artifacts into the docs folder\"",
  "IsArtifact": "false",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"c:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\Dropbox\\\\My Documents\\\\Programs\\\\Lakou\\\\scripts\\\\sync-logs.ps1\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Writing sync-logs script\"",

}

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Lakou
2: ### Your practice. Your pace. Your app.
3: 
4: > Most project management tools were built for someone else — and then handed to architects. Lakou wasn't. It was built from the actual frustrations of running a small practice: client texts that disappear, fee burn you don't catch until it's too late, folder structures that only make sense if you invented them. Lakou captures the way your work actually moves — from the first client text to the milestone nudge — without forcing you into someone else's system.
5: 
6: Sideloaded Android app for the Green Stories architecture firm. Mobile-first project management with SMS-to-project-plan capture, time tracking, billing/expenses, gamification, dock mode, Gmail/Calendar sync, and Dropbox-backed storage.
7: 
8: **Owner:** Tom Reynolds. **Distribution:** sideload only (uses `READ_SMS`, `READ_CONTACTS`, foreground services — will not pass Play Store review, by design).
9: 
10: ## Repository layout
11: 
12: ```
13: TextProjectManagement/
14:   app/        Flutter project (lib/, android/, test/, pubspec.yaml)
15:   data/       SQLite live db (tpm.db) — synced via Dropbox
16:   docs/       README, permissions, filename spec, Android Studio handoff
17:   scripts/    build-apk.ps1
18:   inbox/      Webhook drop folder for client intake form submissions
19: ```
20: 
21: ## First-time setup
22: 
23: 1. Install Flutter SDK (stable channel) and Android Studio with Android SDK 34+.
24: 2. From `app/`, run `flutter create .` once to generate the platform glue (the lib/, pubspec.yaml, android/AndroidManifest.xml in this repo will be preserved/merged).
25: 3. `flutter pub get`
26: 4. Copy `app/lib/core/secrets.example.dart` → `secrets.dart` and fill in:
27:    - Anthropic API key
28:    - Dropbox app key + secret
29:    - Google OAuth client ID
30: 5. `flutter run` against a connected Pixel device (USB debugging on).
31: 
32: ## Build a sideload APK
33: 
34: ```powershell
35: .\scripts\build-apk.ps1
36: ```
37: 
38: Produces `app/build/app/outputs/flutter-apk/app-release.apk` and copies it to `…\Programs\TextProjectManagement\releases\`.
39: 
40: ## Hand-off to Gemini in Android Studio
41: 
42: See [`android-studio-handoff.md`](android-studio-handoff.md) once it's generated.
43: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

 ## Hand-off to Gemini in Android Studio
 
 See [`android-studio-handoff.md`](android-studio-handoff.md) once it's generated.
+
+## Syncing Agent Conversation Logs
+
+To capture agent conversations and implementation artifacts directly within the project repository under `docs/system_generated_logs/`, run:
+
+```powershell
+.\scripts\sync-logs.ps1
+```
+
+This script copies the conversation histories (`transcript.jsonl`) and planning artifacts (`implementation_plan.md`, `task.md`, `walkthrough.md`) from your local `.gemini` folder to `docs/system_generated_logs/<conversation-id>/`. This sync is also automatically triggered when exiting a session started via `.\scripts\start-claude.ps1`.
+

1: # Phase 1 — Fix Critical Bugs
2:

1: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
2: import 'package:path/path.dart' as p;
3: import 'package:path_provider/path_provider.dart';
4: 
5: /// SQLite schema and accessor for Lakou.
6: ///
7: /// The live db file is intended to live inside the user's Dropbox folder
8: /// (`/Programs/Lakou/data/tpm.db`) so the desktop sees it automatically.
9: /// On Android the runtime file is opened from the app's documents dir and
10: /// synced up/down via [DropboxClient]; we never write to the Dropbox path
11: /// directly because Android sandboxing makes that unreliable.
12: class LakouDb {
13:   LakouDb._(this._db);
14:   final Database _db;
15:   Database get raw => _db;
16: 
17:   static Future<LakouDb> open() async {
18:     final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
19:     final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');
20:     final db = await openDatabase(
21:       path,
22:       version: 2,
23:       onCreate: (db, version) async {
24:         await _migrateV1(db);
25:         await _migrateV2(db);
26:       },
27:       onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {
28:         if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);
29:       },
30:     );
31:     return LakouDb._(db);
32:   }
33: 
34:   /// v2: captures get a status column ('pending' | 'assigned' | 'archived').
35:   /// Archived captures are kept as local-only pointers so Tom can resurrect
36:   /// them later from the original SMS/MMS thread.
37:   static Future<void> _migrateV2(Database db) async {
38:     await db.execute(
39:       "ALTER TABLE captures ADD COLUMN status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'",
40:     );
41:     // Anything already on disk with a dropbox_path was saved to a project.
42:     await db.execute(
43:       "UPDATE captures SET status = 'assigned' WHERE dropbox_path IS NOT NULL",
44:     );
45:   }
46: 
47:   static Future<void> _migrateV1(Database db) async {
48:     final batch = db.batch();
49: 
50:     // Clients — developers get is_developer=1 and a dev_code (e.g. "AC").
51:     batch.execute('''
52:       CREATE TABLE clients (
53:         id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
54:         name         TEXT NOT NULL,
55:         email        TEXT,
56:         phone        TEXT,
57:         business     TEXT,
58:         biz_address  TEXT,
59:         biz_phone    TEXT,
60:         is_developer INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 03:45:54 UTC)

Yes, the app was partially updated per the audit. Specifically, **Phase 1 (Fixing Critical Bugs)** was completely implemented and verified:

### Completed (Phase 1 - Critical Bugs)
- **Bug 1:** Corrected the path in `config.dart` so project directories save to `'06 Project Data/02 Project Directory'` rather than `03 Correspondence`.
- **Bug 2:** Swapped out the manual filename logic in `expense_capture_screen.dart` with `FilenameBuilder.expenseReceipt()` and added sibling `.json` metadata sidecar uploads.
- **Bug 3:** Fixed the project path in `new_project_screen.dart` to use an underscore (`_`) separator between the job number and name instead of a space.
- **Bug 4:** Fixed a crash on Android 14+ by adding the required `foregroundServiceType` to `startForeground()` inside `TimerForegroundService.kt`.
- **Bug 5:** Fixed the `setState()` during build crash in `capture_review_screen.dart` by deferring the execution via a post-frame callback.
- **Bug 6:** Added null-safety and parsing guards in `claude_extractor.dart` to prevent app crashes on malformed AI responses.
- **Verification:** Ran `flutter analyze` and `flutter test` (all tests passed with 0 errors/warnings).

---

### Remaining (Phase 2 & Phase 3)
The following tasks from the audit are still pending:
* **Phase 2 (Architecture & UI Corrections):**
  * Move `dbProvider` from `projects_provider.dart` to a central location in `core/db.dart` to clean up cross-feature imports.
  * Add a time entry category picker (design, meeting, site visit, coordination, admin) on the timer screen instead of hardcoding `'design'`.
  * Fix the SMS inbox search clear button (currently clears state but doesn't clear text in the field).
  * Delete the stale `app/` directory (leftover empty build artifacts).
* **Phase 3 (Documentation Updates):**
  * Update `android-studio-handoff.md` and `agp-9-compliance.md` files to match actual dependencies (Gradle 9.3.1, Kotlin 2.1.0, AGP 8.9.1, targetSdk 36).
  * Fix stale comments in Gradle config files.

Would you like me to begin implementing the **Phase 2** architectural and UI corrections now?

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import '../projects/projects_provider.dart';
3: 
4: /// Projects whose tracked-time-against-fee ratio has crossed 1.0 — i.e.
5: /// where Tom is genuinely working for free. Threshold raised from 0.8 to 1.0
6: /// per the AuDHD synthesis: less pre-emptive anxiety, same useful signal.
7: final overFeeProjectsProvider = FutureProvider<List<Map<String, dynamic>>>((ref) async {
8:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
9:   final results = await db.raw.rawQuery('''
10:     SELECT
11:       p.id,
12:       p.job_no,
13:       p.name,
14:       p.contract_fee,
15:       p.blended_rate,
16:       SUM(t.seconds) / 3600.0 as hours,
17:       (SUM(t.seconds) / 3600.0 * p.blended_rate) / p.contract_fee as burn_ratio
18:     FROM projects p
19:     LEFT JOIN time_entries t ON p.id = t.project_id
20:     WHERE p.status = 'active' AND p.contract_fee > 0 AND p.blended_rate > 0
21:     GROUP BY p.id
22:     HAVING burn_ratio > 1.0
23:     ORDER BY burn_ratio DESC
24:   ''');
25:   return results;
26: });
27:

90: - **Light theme only.** Cream `#FAF8F3` background, Lakou green `#6B8E5A` seed color, white cards with 16px rounded corners.
91: - **AuDHD-friendly tone.** Gentle, considered, drafting-table-feel. The app talks to *one architect*, never to "users" or "teams".
92: - **No invoicing.** QuickBooks does invoicing. Lakou only *reminds* Tom to invoice. Do not propose adding invoice generation.
93: - **No team features.** Single user. No collaboration, no permissions, no sharing.
94: - **No iOS.** Android only.
95: - **Time vs. fee, not time vs. budget.** Tom bills flat-fee. Hours are tracked to detect *under-billing* (working for free), not to bill by the hour.
96: 
97: If you find yourself about to build something that contradicts any of these rules, **stop and ask Tom first**.

8: 
9: /// Billing — flat-fee + milestones, fee burn ratio, reimbursables.
10: ///
11: /// Pulls real data from the local DB rather than the prior wireframe:
12: ///  - "Worth a look" lists projects whose burn_ratio > 1.0 (already provided
13: ///    by overFeeProjectsProvider; clicking a row jumps to project detail).
14: ///  - "Reimbursables" lists unbilled expenses with reimbursable=1.
15: final _reimbursablesProvider =
16:     FutureProvider<List<Map<String, dynamic>>>((ref) async {
17:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
18:   return db.raw.rawQuery('''
19:     SELECT e.id, e.amount, e.vendor, e.spent_at, e.project_id,
20:            p.job_no, p.name AS project_name
21:     FROM expenses e
22:     LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = e.project_id
23:     WHERE e.reimbursable = 1 AND e.billed_back = 0
24:     ORDER BY e.spent_at DESC
25:   ''');

Showing lines 1 to 41
The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # AGP 9.1.0 Compliance — baked in from day one
2: 
3: Android Studio kept trying to upgrade Tom's previous projects to AGP 9.1.0 mid-stream and breaking things. Lakou is **born on AGP 9.1.0** so the upgrade prompt is a no-op.
4: 
5: ## What AGP 9 changed that bites Flutter projects
6: 
7: | Change | Old way | Lakou way |
8: |---|---|---|
9: | Manifest `package` attr | `<manifest package="com.example">` | **Removed.** Namespace lives in `app/build.gradle.kts` as `namespace = "com.greenstories.lakou"`. |
10: | Java target | Java 11 | **Java 17** in `compileOptions` and Kotlin `jvmTarget`. |
11: | Gradle | 7.x / 8.0 | **Gradle 8.11.1** via `gradle-wrapper.properties`. |
12: | AGP | 7.x / 8.x | **9.1.0** pinned in `settings.gradle.kts` `plugins {}` block. |
13: | Kotlin | 1.8.x / 1.9.x | **Kotlin 2.0.21**. |
14: | `compileSdk` / `targetSdk` | 33–34 | **35** (Android 15). |
15: | `BuildConfig` generation | on by default | off by default → **explicitly enabled** via `buildFeatures { buildConfig = true }`. |
16: | `buildDir` property | mutable | deprecated → **uses `layout.buildDirectory`** in root `build.gradle.kts`. |
17: | `aaptOptions` / `lintOptions` / `dexOptions` | allowed | **removed** — use `androidResources`, `lint`, packaging blocks instead. |
18: | Foreground services | optional `foregroundServiceType` | **required**. Manifest declares `specialUse` for `TimerForegroundService` with the `PROPERTY_SPECIAL_USE_FGS_SUBTYPE` rationale. |
19: | Non-transitive R classes | optional | **enabled** in `gradle.properties`. |
20: | Configuration cache 
<truncated 49 bytes>
21: | Resource shrinking with code shrinking | implicit | must be **explicit** — `isMinifyEnabled` + `isShrinkResources` set together in `release` buildType. |
22: 
23: ## Files that lock this in
24: - `app/android/settings.gradle.kts` — pins AGP 9.1.0 + Kotlin 2.0.21.
25: - `app/android/build.gradle.kts` — uses `layout.buildDirectory`, no `buildDir`.
26: - `app/android/app/build.gradle.kts` — declares `namespace`, Java 17, `compileSdk` 35, `buildConfig = true`, core library desugaring.
27: - `app/android/gradle.properties` — `nonTransitiveRClass=true`, `nonFinalResIds=true`, configuration cache on, Jetifier off.
28: - `app/android/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties` — Gradle 8.11.1.
29: - `app/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` — **no `package` attribute**, foreground service types declared.
30: 
31: ## Plugin gotcha
32: Some Flutter plugins still hardcode `package` in their own manifests and break AGP 9 builds. If `flutter pub get` brings in a plugin that fails, the fix is one of:
33: 1. Upgrade the plugin to a version published after Aug 2024 (most are AGP-9-ready by now).
34: 2. Add a `pluginManagement` resolver in `settings.gradle.kts` that overrides the namespace (already structured to allow this).
35: 3. Fork the plugin and add `namespace` to its `build.gradle`.
36: 
37: The packages chosen in `pubspec.yaml` (riverpod, go_router, sqflite, dio, receive_sharing_intent, another_telephony, flutter_contacts, image_picker, battery_plus, sensors_plus, wakelock_plus, flutter_local_notifications, audioplayers, confetti, googleapis, google_sign_in) all have AGP-9-compatible releases as of late 2025 — but if any one of them regresses, the workaround above applies.
38: 
39: ## When Gemini in Android Studio offers to "Upgrade AGP"
40: **Decline.** The project is already on the latest. If Studio nags, it's because the wrapper hasn't downloaded yet — run `./gradlew wrapper` once and the prompt goes away.
41: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

201: Produces `releases/lakou-<timestamp>.apk`. Install on Pixel via `adb install` or by opening from Dropbox on the phone.
202: 
203: For `flutter run` during development, plug Pixel in with USB debugging and run from Android Studio's run button or the terminal.
204: 
205: ## 10. Open questions / known gotchas
206: 
207: - **`flutter create .` first.** The repo doesn't yet contain Flutter's platform glue (no `MainActivity.kt`, no `local.properties`, no resources). Run `flutter create . --org com.greenstories --project-name lakou --platforms=android` once inside `app/` — it will preserve the `lib/`, `pubspec.yaml`, and our Gradle/manifest files because Flutter only fills in missing scaffolding.
208: - **Plugin AGP 9 regressions.** If `flutter pub get` brings in a plugin that fails to build under AGP 9, see `agp-9-compliance.md` § "Plugin gotcha" for the three fixes.
209: - **Dropbox app key.** Tom needs to register a Dropbox app at https://www.dropbox.com/developers/apps with PKCE redirect URI `lakou://oauth`. Put the key in `secrets.dart`.
210: - **Anthropic API key in client.** Currently the key sits in `secrets.dart` and is sent direct from the device. Acceptable for a personal sideload — never do this in a public app.
211: - **Time vs. fee report.** Tom specifically wants this front-and-center. It's a SQL query: `SUM(time_entries.seconds) / 3600 * blended_rate / contract_fee` per project. Expose as a dashboard widget.
212: - **Boot persistence.** SMS receiver and timer must come back after reboot — wire a `BootReceiver` to re-
<truncated 344 bytes>
, `pubspec.yaml`, `android/build.gradle.kts`, `android/app/build.gradle.kts`, and `AndroidManifest.xml` survive. Run `flutter pub get` and confirm the dependency tree resolves under AGP 9.1.0.
218: 2. **Implement `SmsReceiver.kt`** as described in §7.1, plus its `MethodChannel` on `lakou/sms`. Wire a Dart listener in `lib/features/capture/sms_listener.dart` (new file) that reads watched senders from `settings` and routes incoming texts into `CaptureReviewScreen` via `go_router`.
219: 3. **Implement `TimerForegroundService.kt`** with persistent notification + start/stop channel. Wire `lib/features/timetrack/timer_service.dart` (new file) to call it from `TimetrackScreen`. Persist `time_entries` rows on stop.
220: 4. **Wire Dropbox OAuth.** Build a tiny in-app sign-in screen that runs the PKCE flow, captures the refresh token, and calls `DropboxClient.saveRefreshToken`. Test by uploading a dummy `.txt` to `/Programs/Lakou/data/test.txt`.
221: 5. **End-to-end capture test.** Share a fake SMS like `"site visit Friday foundation pour 23AC-08"` from any text app → Lakou's Capture Review opens → Claude returns a structured note → user confirms `23AC-08` → file lands in `Green Stories/2023/Ascension Capital/23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark - Multifamily/04 Meeting Agendas and Reports/2026-MMDD_23AC-08_…_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`. Verify `captures` table has the row.
222: 
223: After those 5 are green, the remaining roadmap is in `lively-growing-alpaca.md` (the approved plan): New Project wizard, importer, billing/expenses, intake form, dock mode, gamification, Gmail/Calendar.
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: **Questions for Tom (not Gemini):** if anything in this doc is unclear, the source of truth is the approved plan at `C:\Users\thoma\.claude\plans\lively-growing-alpaca.md`. The plan and this handoff doc must stay in sync.
