10: class DropboxAuthNotifier extends StateNotifier<AsyncValue<void>> {
11:   DropboxAuthNotifier(this.client) : super(const AsyncValue.data(null));
12: 
13:   final DropboxClient client;
14:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
15:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
16:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
17:       resetOnError: true,
18:     ),
19:   );
20:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
21: 
22:   Future<void> signIn() async {
23:     state = const AsyncValue.loading();
24:     try {
25:       final codeVerifier = _generateCodeVerifier();
26:       await _storage.write(key: _verifierKey, value: codeVerifier);
27: 
28:       final codeChallenge = _generateCodeChallenge(codeVerifier);
29: 
30:       final url = Uri.https('www.dropbox.com', '/oauth2/authorize', {
31:         'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
32:         'response_type': 'code',
33:         'code_challenge': codeChallenge,
34:         'code_challenge_method': 'S256',
35:         'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
36:         'token_access_type': 'offline',
37:       });
38: 
39:       if (await canLaunchUrl(url)) {
40:         await launchUrl(url, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);
41:       } else {
42:         throw Exception('Could not launch Dropbox auth URL');
43:       }
44:       
45:       // Note: Token exchange happens via deep link handler (to be implemented)
46:       state = const AsyncValue.data(null);
47:     } catch (e, st) {
48:       state = AsyncValue.error(e, st);
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   String _generateCodeVerifier() {
53:     final random = Random.secure();
54:     final values = List<int>.generate(32, (i) => random.nextInt(256));
55:     return base64UrlEncode(values).replaceAll('=', '');
56:   }
57: 
58:   String _generateCodeChallenge(String verifier) {
59:     final bytes = utf8.encode(verifier);
60:     final digest = sha256.convert(bytes);
61:     return base64UrlEncode(digest.bytes).replaceAll('=', '');
62:   }
63: }
64: 
65: final dropboxAuthProvider = StateNotifierProvider<DropboxAuthNotifier, AsyncValue<void>>((ref) {
66:   return DropboxAuthNotifier(ref.watch(dropboxClientProvider));
67: });
68: 
69: final dropboxAuthStateProvider = FutureProvider<bool>((ref) async {
70:   final client = ref.watch(dropboxClientProvider);
71:   return client.hasRefreshToken();
72: });

12: class AppLinksHandler {
13:   AppLinksHandler(this.ref);
14:   final Ref ref;
15:   late final _appLinks = AppLinks();
16:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
17:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
18:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
19:       resetOnError: true,
20:     ),

23: 
24:   void init() {
25:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Initializing...');
26:     
27:     // Handle deep links when the app is already running
28:     _appLinks.uriLinkStream.listen((uri) {
29:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Stream URI: $uri');
30:       _processUri(uri);
31:     });
32: 
33:     // Handle deep link that launched the app
34:     _appLinks.getInitialLink().then((uri) {
35:       if (uri != null) {
36:         debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Initial URI: $uri');
37:         _processUri(uri);
38:       }
39:     });
40:   }
41: 
42:   void _processUri(Uri uri) {
43:     if (uri.scheme == 'lakou' && uri.host == 'oauth') {
44:       final code = uri.queryParameters['code'];
45:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code found: ${code != null}');
46:       if (code != null) {
47:         _handleDropboxCode(code);
48:       }
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> _handleDropboxCode(String code) async {
53:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Exchanging code for token...');
54:     try {
55:       final codeVerifier = await _storage.read(key: _verifierKey);
56:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code Verifier found: ${codeVerifier != null}');
57:       final dio = Dio();
58:       final res = await dio.post(
59:         'https://api.dropbox.com/oauth2/token',
60:         options: Options(contentType: Headers.formUrlEncodedContentType),
61:         data: {
62:           'code': code,
63:           'grant_type': 'authorization_code',
64:           'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
65:           'client_secret': Secrets.dropboxAppSecret,
66:           'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
67:           'code_verifier': codeVerifier,
68:         },
69:       );
70: 
71:       final refreshToken = res.data['refresh_token'] as String;
72:       final client = ref.read(dropboxClientProvider);
73:       await client.saveRefreshToken(refreshToken);
74:       
75:       // Invalidate the auth state so the UI updates
76:       ref.invalidate(dropboxAuthStateProvider);
77:       
78:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Success: Access Token Acquired');
79:     } on DioException catch (e) {
80:       final responseData = e.response?.data;
81:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error (DioException): ${e.message}\nResponse: $responseData');
82:     } catch (e) {
83:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error: $e');
84:     }
85:   }
86: }

280:   /// Computes the next iteration NN for a SMS-capture filename in [folder]
281:   /// matching the pattern `<datePrefix>_<jobNo>_..._Text-Projectplan-NN.txt`.
282:   Future<int> nextCaptureIteration({
283:     required String folder,
284:     required String datePrefix, // e.g. 2026-0406
285:     required String jobNo,
286:   }) async {
287:     try {
288:       final names = await listFolder(folder);
289:       final re = RegExp(
290:         '^${RegExp.escape(datePrefix)}_${RegExp.escape(jobNo)}_.*_Text-Projectplan-(\\d{2,})\\.txt\$',
291:       );
292:       var max = 0;
293:       for (final n in names) {
294:         final m = re.firstMatch(n);
295:         if (m != null) {
296:           final v = int.tryParse(m.group(1) ?? '') ?? 0;
297:           if (v > max) max = v;
298:         }
299:       }
300:       return max + 1;
301:     } on DioException {
302:       return 1;
303:     }
304:   }

14: /// Job number formats:
15: ///   developer:     ^\d{2}[A-Z]{2,4}-\d{2,3}$    (e.g. 23AC-08)

17: ///
18: /// We never overwrite an existing `projects` row — `job_no` is UNIQUE, so a
19: /// re-run is idempotent (`INSERT OR IGNORE`). Skips the special
20: /// `Architecture/` folder under any year (that's where the Sample Folder
21: /// Structure lives) and skips anything that doesn't look like a project.

28:   // Captures the job-number prefix, optional separator, then the rest as name.
29:   static final _folderName = RegExp(r'^([0-9A-Z\-]+)[_\s]+(.+)$');
30:

121:   /// Inserts every project from [scan]ned results that isn't already in the DB.
122:   /// Returns the number of new rows inserted.
123:   Future<int> importInto(LakouDb db, List<ImportedProject> projects) async {
124:     var inserted = 0;
125:     for (final p in projects) {
126:       // Dedupe by either job_no OR dropbox_path — same folder shouldn't end
127:       // up in the table twice even if the synthesized job number differs.
128:       final existing = await db.raw.query(
129:         'projects',
130:         where: 'job_no = ? OR dropbox_path = ?',
131:         whereArgs: [p.jobNo, p.dropboxPath],
132:         limit: 1,
133:       );
134:       if (existing.isNotEmpty) continue;
135:       await db.raw.insert('projects', {
136:         'job_no': p.jobNo,
137:         'name': p.name,
138:         'start_year': p.year,
139:         'dropbox_path': p.dropboxPath,
140:         'phase': 'SD',
141:         'status': 'active',
142:         'created_at': DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch,
143:       });
144:       inserted++;
145:     }
146:     return inserted;
147:   }

1: # Lakou — Claude Code Handoff Prompt
2: 
3: Paste this entire block as your **first message** to Claude Code in the Android Studio terminal. It primes Claude with everything it needs to pick up where the planning session left off and start writing real code against the existing scaffold.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: You are picking up work on **Lakou** — a sideloaded Android-only Flutter project management app for Tom Reynolds, sole architect at Green Stories Architecture. The project was scaffolded in a previous Claude Code session and is partially built. Your job is to continue implementation, not re-plan it.
8: 
9: ## What Lakou is, why it exists, and who it's for
10: **Lakou** ("courtyard" in Haitian Kreyòl) is a personal daily-driver mobile-first project management app for one architect running a small practice. The lakou is the courtyard around which a Haitian household lives — the well at its center is where everything in the household converges. That's the metaphor for the app: every text, every receipt, every hour of work converges in one place.
11: 
12: **Tagline:** *Your practice. Your pace. Your app.*
13: 
14: ### The user
15: Tom Reynolds — licensed architect, owner of Green Stories Architecture, runs the firm solo (occasionally with consultants). **Has AuDHD**, which shapes every design decision: the app must be motivating without being aggressive, gentle without being condescending, visual without being noisy. Tom is not deeply technical — he wants buttons and screens, not config files. He uses a Pixel phone, an RTX 5090 desktop, makes TikToks
<truncated 8061 bytes>
 to fit on a phone. Every screen must work one-handed on a Pixel.
86: - **Sideload only.** Will never ship to Play Store. SMS, contacts, exact alarms, foreground services, full-screen intents are all fair game.
87: - **Dropbox is the source of truth for files.** SQLite on the device tracks app state; the actual files (notes, receipts, project directories) live in `Green Stories\` and the app writes them via the Dropbox API. Tom's desktop sees them automatically through Dropbox sync.
88: - **The firm's folder taxonomy is sacred.** The 10 standard subfolders from `Green Stories\Architecture\Sample Folder Structure\` are the canonical layout. Never invent new folder names. Match the **exact** spelling, including `04 Meeting Agendas and Reports` (singular "Meeting").
89: - **Filename spec is locked.** `app/lib/features/capture/filename_builder.dart` and `app/test/filename_builder_test.dart` are the source of truth. New patterns get new methods + new tests; existing ones never change.
90: - **Light theme only.** Cream `#FAF8F3` background, Lakou green `#6B8E5A` seed color, white cards with 16px rounded corners.
91: - **AuDHD-friendly tone.** Gentle, considered, drafting-table-feel. The app talks to *one architect*, never to "users" or "teams".
92: - **No invoicing.** QuickBooks does invoicing. Lakou only *reminds* Tom to invoice. Do not propose adding invoice generation.
93: - **No team features.** Single user. No collaboration, no permissions, no sharing.
94: - **No iOS.** Android only.
95: - **Time vs. fee, not time vs. budget.** Tom bills flat-fee. Hours are tracked to detect *under-billing* (working for free), not to bill by the hour.
96: 
97: If you find yourself about to build something that contradicts any of these rules, **stop and ask Tom first**.
98: 
99: ## Session state (last verified)

{
  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"A comprehensive handoff prompt to take the Lakou project from its current Phase 4 state to a world-class individual project management app, tailored for Tom.\",\"UserFacing\":true}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Handoff Prompt: Taking Lakou to World-Class\\n\\nCopy and paste the text block below into a fresh session with Claude. It provides the exact context of what has been built, the toolchain in place, and a step-by-step roadmap to implement the remaining features.\\n\\n***\\n\\n```markdown\\nYou are an expert Flutter and Android developer. We are building **Lakou**, a highly tailored, mobile-first project management app designed specifically for a single user: Tom, an AuDHD architect running a solo firm (Green Stories).\\n\\nLakou is designed to feel like a premium drafting table\u2014using a warm cream/sage color palette, monospace styling, and gentle phrasing. It is offline-first, backing all data into a local SQLite database (`tpm.db`) which is synced up and down via Dropbox. It uses a sideloaded Android deployment due to its deep integration with native permissions (`READ_SMS`, notification access, foreground services).\\n\\n### 1. Current Repository Structure\\nThe project has been cleaned up and is structured as follows:\\n```\\nLakou/ (repository root)\\n  Lakou/                                Flutter project root\\n    lib/\\n      main.dart                         ProviderScope + App Links initialization\\n      app.dart                          GoRouter routing and Material 3 theme configuration\\n      core/\\n        config.dart                     Global constants, paths, and default settings\\n        db.dart                         SQLite open accessor and global dbProvider\\n        dropbox_client.dart             Dropbox REST client (files, metadata, OAuth)\\n        gentle_phrases.dart             Custom AuDHD-friendly response greetings\\n        lakou_theme.dart                Cream (#FAF8F3) and green (#6B8E5A) visual styling\\n      features/\\n        auth/                           Dropbox OAuth handlers and deep linking\\n        billing/                        Milestone billing and fee burn analytics\\n        capture/                        SMS scraping (MethodChannel), review screens, Claude extraction\\n        dashb\n<truncated 8409 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Write the updated world-class Claude handoff prompt to docs/claude_handoff_prompt.md.\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"c:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\Dropbox\\\\My Documents\\\\Programs\\\\Lakou\\\\docs\\\\claude_handoff_prompt.md\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Creating world-class handoff prompt file in docs\"",
  "toolSummary": "\"Creating handoff prompt\""
}
```
</details>

1: // Lakou — Android settings (AGP 8.9.1 compatible).
2: // Uses the Flutter loader plugin from FLUTTER_SDK + the AGP/Kotlin plugins
3: // declared up front so the whole project shares one resolver.
4: 
5: pluginManagement {
6:     val flutterSdkPath = run {
7:         val properties = java.util.Properties()
8:         file("local.properties").inputStream().use { properties.load(it) }
9:         val flutterSdkPath = properties.getProperty("flutter.sdk")
10:         require(flutterSdkPath != null) { "flutter.sdk not set in local.properties" }
11:         flutterSdkPath
12:     }
13: 
14:     includeBuild("$flutterSdkPath/packages/flutter_tools/gradle")
15: 
16:     repositories {
17:         google()
18:         mavenCentral()
19:         gradlePluginPortal()
20:     }
21: }
22: 
23: plugins {
24:     id("dev.flutter.flutter-plugin-loader") version "1.0.0"
25:     // Downgraded for plugin compatibility

27:     id("com.android.library")     version "8.9.1" apply false
28:     id("org.jetbrains.kotlin.android") version "2.1.0" apply false
29: }

31: include(":app")

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: # Build a release APK for Lakou and copy it to a releases\ folder for sideload.
2: # Run from the repo root: .\scripts\build-apk.ps1
3: 
4: $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
5: 
6: $root = Split-Path -Parent $PSScriptRoot
7: $appDir = Join-Path $root 'Lakou'
8: $releasesDir = Join-Path $root 'releases'
9: New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $releasesDir | Out-Null
10: 
11: Push-Location $appDir
12: try {
13:     Write-Host 'flutter pub get...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
14:     flutter pub get
15: 
16:     Write-Host 'flutter build apk --release...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
17:     flutter build apk --release
18: 
19:     $apk = Join-Path $appDir 'build\app\outputs\flutter-apk\app-release.apk'
20:     if (-not (Test-Path $apk)) { throw "APK not found at $apk" }
21: 
22:     $stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmm'
23:     $dest  = Join-Path $releasesDir "lakou-$stamp.apk"
24:     Copy-Item $apk $dest -Force
25:     Write-Host "Wrote $dest" -ForegroundColor Green
26: }
27: finally {
28:     Pop-Location
29: }
30: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

  "CodeContent": "\"# Lakou Project Walkthrough \u2014 Codebase Hygiene & Handoff Prompt\\n\\nThis walkthrough summarizes the cleanup of static analysis issues in Lakou and the generation of a comprehensive handoff prompt to take the app to the next level (world-class individual project management).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Verification Results\\n\\n| Check | Result | Details |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `flutter analyze` | \u2705 **0 issues found** | Cleaned up all 21 warnings (unused imports, deprecations, async gaps, const constructors) |\\n| `flutter test` | \u2705 **5/5 tests passed** | All widget smoke tests and filename builders run and pass |\\n| `.\\\\scripts\\\\build-apk.ps1` | \u2705 **Build Succeeded** | Created release APK under [releases/](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/releases/) folder |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Changes Implemented\\n\\n### 1. Codebase Hygiene (Lint Fixes)\\nWe resolved all compiler and code-hygiene warnings across the following files:\\n* **Unused/Unnecessary Imports:** Removed unused imports (`dart:typed_data`, `package:go_router/go_router.dart`, and `../../core/db.dart`) in `share_routing_screen.dart`, `planner_screen.dart`, `expense_capture_screen.dart`, `gmail_sync_service.dart`, and `intake_screen.dart`.\\n* **Deprecated Value Property:** Swapped deprecated `value` property with `initialValue` in `DropdownButtonFormField` widgets inside `share_routing_screen.dart`, `planner_screen.dart`, and `timetrack_screen.dart`.\\n* **Deprecated Opacity Method:** Replaced deprecated `color.withOpacity(...)` with the modern `color.withValues(alpha: ...)` in `planner_screen.dart` and `timetrack_screen.dart`.\\n* **Unused Result warning:** Changed `ref.refresh(calendarEventsProvider)` to `ref.invalidate(calendarEventsProvider)` in `planner_screen.dart` (line 594) to cleanly invalidate state without triggering unused return value warnings.\\n* **BuildContext across async gaps:** Guarded async callbacks in `trace_viewer_screen.dart` and `timetrack_screen.dart` using `context.mounted`.\\n* **Const Constructors:** Added `const` to `_SectionHeader\n<truncated 1337 bytes>",

1: ---
2: name: android-cli
3: description: Orchestrates Android development tasks including project creation, deployment, SDK management, and environment diagnostics using the `android` command-line tool.
4: ---
5: # Android CLI Specialist
6: 
7: This skill provides instructions for using the `android` CLI tool. The tool includes various commands for creating projects, running applications, interacting with devices, and managing the CLI environment.
8: 
9: ## Installation
10: 
11: If the android tool is not in the path install it. To install run the following command:
12: Linux: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/linux_x86_64/install.sh | bash
13: Mac: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/darwin_arm64/install.sh | bash
14: Windows: curl.exe -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/windows_x86_64/install.cmd -o "%TEMP%\i.cmd" && "%TEMP%\i.cmd"
15: 
16: ## SDK management
17: To manage the installation of Android SDKs and tools, use the `sdk` command. For example:
18: 
19: - `android sdk install <package>[@<version>]...`: Install specific packages. Multiple packages can be specified, separated by spaces. `<version>` defaults to latest. For example: `android sdk install platforms/android-30@2 platforms/android-34`
20: - `android sdk update [<pkg-name>]`: Update a specific package or all packages to the latest version.
21: - `android sdk remove <pkg-name>`: Remove a package from the local SDK.
22: - `android sdk list --all`: List installed and available SDK packages.
23: 
24: ## Project creation
25: Create projects from templa

7: final claudeExtractorProvider = Provider((ref) => ClaudeExtractor(Secrets.anthropicApiKey));
8: 
9: final forceClaudeProvider = StateProvider.autoDispose<bool>((ref) => false);
10: 
11: final extractionProvider = FutureProvider.autoDispose<ExtractedCapture>((ref) async {
12:   final capture = ref.watch(incomingCaptureProvider);
13:   if (capture == null) {
14:     Trace.log('claude', 'extraction skipped: no capture');
15:     throw Exception('No capture to extract');
16:   }
17: 
18:   final forceClaude = ref.watch(forceClaudeProvider);
19: 
20:   Trace.log('claude', 'extract start',
21:       data: 'from=${capture.senderLabel} len=${capture.rawText.length} forceClaude=$forceClaude');
22:   final extractor = ref.read(claudeExtractorProvider);
23:   try {
24:     final result = await extractor.extract(capture.rawText, sender: capture.sender, forceClaude: forceClaude);
25:     Trace.log('claude', 'extract ok',
26:         data: 'job=${result.jobNoGuess} actions=${result.actionItems.length} tier=${result.extractionTier}');
27:     return result;
28:   } catch (e) {
29:     Trace.log('claude', 'extract err', data: e);

4: import android.content.Context
5: import android.content.Intent
6: import android.util.Log
7: 
8: class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
9:     override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
10:         if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) {
11:             Log.d("LakouBootReceiver", "Boot completed broadcast received.")
12:             
13:             // Check for running timer
14:             val prefs = context.getSharedPreferences("LakouTimerPrefs", Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
15:             val isActive = prefs.getBoolean("isActive", false)
16:             if (isActive) {
17:                 Log.d("LakouBootReceiver", "Active timer found at boot, marking as interrupted.")
18:                 prefs.edit().apply {
19:                     putBoolean("isActive", false)
20:                     putBoolean("interrupted", true)
21:                     putLong("interruptedEndTime", System.currentTimeMillis())
22:                     apply()
23:                 }
24:             }
25:             
26:             // Re-schedule alarms
27:             AlarmReceiver.scheduleDefaultAlarms(context)
28:         }
29:     }
30: }

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 15:08:37 UTC)

I did a detailed audit comparing the completed codebase against the original `lakou-antigravity-plan.md` brief and identified **two gaps** where the implementation didn't fully match the conceptual plan:

1. **Tier 3 On-Device LLM (Gemini Nano / MediaPipe Gemma):**
   * *What was planned:* Text extraction using Gemini Nano or a downloaded Gemma model when offline.
   * *Current reality:* The app currently falls back directly to **Tier 1 (Local Heuristic Regex)** if Claude (Tier 4) is not explicitly triggered. There is no active MediaPipe or AICore code in the codebase (due to the difficulty of testing/compiling Google Pixel-exclusive neural hardware APIs in a sandboxed developer environment).
2. **SQLite Database Auto-Sync (`tpm.db`):**
   * *What was planned:* The README references the `tpm.db` file in `/data/` being synced via Dropbox.
   * *Current reality:* While the app uploads photos, receipt JSONs, and Markdown files to Dropbox, it does **not** automatically upload or download the SQLite database itself on launch/exit. It runs entirely on the local device documents directory.

### How this is handled:
I have **proactively incorporated both of these missing elements** as the primary focus areas in the new [docs/claude_handoff_prompt.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/docs/claude_handoff_prompt.md):
* **Stage 1** of the roadmap instructs Claude on how to implement the **SQLite Auto-Sync & Backup to Dropbox** (metadata-based conflict management).
* **Stage 2** instructs Claude on how to wire up the **Offline Voice Capturing & Local LLM (Gemini Nano / MediaPipe)**.

When you copy-paste the handoff prompt into Claude, it will pick up these specific items immediately and implement them for you.

---

31: final projectsProvider = FutureProvider<List<Project>>((ref) async {
32:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
33:   final maps = await db.raw.query(

36:     whereArgs: ['archived'],
37:     orderBy: 'job_no DESC',
38:   );
39:   return maps.map((m) => Project.fromMap(m)).toList();
40: });

42: /// Mutations for project lifecycle (archive/delete).
43: class ProjectActions {
44:   ProjectActions(this.ref);
45:   final Ref ref;
46: 
47:   Future<void> archive(int id) async {
48:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
49:     await db.raw.update(
50:       'projects',
51:       {'status': 'archived'},
52:       where: 'id = ?',
53:       whereArgs: [id],
54:     );
55:     ref.invalidate(projectsProvider);
56:   }
57: 
58:   Future<void> unarchive(int id) async {
59:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
60:     await db.raw.update(
61:       'projects',
62:       {'status': 'active'},
63:       where: 'id = ?',
64:       whereArgs: [id],
65:     );
66:     ref.invalidate(projectsProvider);
67:   }
68: 
69:   Future<void> delete(int id) async {
70:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
71:     await db.raw.delete('projects', where: 'id = ?', whereArgs: [id]);
72:     ref.invalidate(projectsProvider);
73:   }
74: }
75: 
76: final projectActionsProvider = Provider<ProjectActions>((ref) => ProjectActions(ref));

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 15:14:13 UTC)

The build is complete and has been successfully pushed to your phone!

### Updates Implemented:
1. **Direct Past Time Logging:** Exposed the `ManualTimeEntryDialog` publicly and updated it to support an optional `initialProjectId`.
2. **Project Detail integration:** Added a **Log manual time (past)** action item in the project details screen's action card. It automatically pre-populates with the active project, lets you pick any date in the past, enter decimal hours, add notes, and refreshes the project's tracked fee burn immediately upon saving.
3. **Pristine Build & Deploy:** Cleaned and compiled the APK natively with the updated feature and successfully pushed the release build (`lakou-20260612-1113.apk`) to your phone via wireless ADB at `192.168.50.67:42483`.
4. **Synced Logs:** Ran `sync-logs.ps1` to update the repository's log directories with this final state.

The app is now fully up to date on your phone with clean code hygiene and direct past time logging!

232: 
233:   /// Lists immediate children of [folder] with full metadata. Handles pagination
234:   /// via list_folder/continue so callers see every entry, not just the first page.
235:   Future<List<DropboxEntry>> listFolderEntries(String folder) async {
236:     final token = await _accessToken();
237:     final out = <DropboxEntry>[];
238:     try {
239:       var res = await _dio.post(
240:         '$_api/files/list_folder',
241:         options: Options(
242:           headers: {
243:             'Authorization': 'Bearer $token',
244:             'Content-Type': 'application/json',
245:           },
246:         ),
247:         // Dropbox v2 wants the root listed as the empty string. Pagination
248:         // returns subsequent pages via list_folder/continue + a cursor.
249:         data: {'path': folder, 'recursive': false},
250:       );
251:       while (true) {
252:         final entries = (res.data['entries'] as List).cast<Map<String, dynamic>>();
253:         for (final e in entries) {
254:           out.add(DropboxEntry(
255:             name: e['name'] as String,
256:             pathDisplay: (e['path_display'] as String?) ?? '',
257:             isFolder: (e['.tag'] as String?) == 'folder',
258:           ));
259:         }
260:         final hasMore = res.data['has_more'] == true;
261:         if (!hasMore) break;
262:         final cursor = res.data['cursor'] as String;
263:         res = await _dio.post(
264:           '$_api/files/list_folder/continue',
265:           options: Options(
266:             headers: {
267:               'Authorization': 'Bearer $token',
268:               'Content-Type': 'application/json',
269:             },
270:           ),
271:           data: {'cursor': cursor},
272:         );
273:       }
274:     } on DioException catch (e) {
275:       throw DropboxException(_dropboxErrorSummary(e));
276:     }
277:     return out;
278:   }

11: /// Hands-on Dropbox folder browser. Tom navigates to whatever folder he wants

17: ///    and inserts one row.

20: /// This is the escape hatch for when the year-folder convention doesn't match
21: /// reality (e.g. archived projects, projects under a non-year folder, projects
22: /// whose folder name doesn't start with a job number).

232: 
233:   /// Lists immediate children of [folder] with full metadata. Handles pagination
234:   /// via list_folder/continue so callers see every entry, not just the first page.
235:   Future<List<DropboxEntry>> listFolderEntries(String folder) async {
236:     final token = await _accessToken();
237:     final out = <DropboxEntry>[];
238:     try {
239:       var res = await _dio.post(
240:         '$_api/files/list_folder',
241:         options: Options(
242:           headers: {
243:             'Authorization': 'Bearer $token',
244:             'Content-Type': 'application/json',
245:           },
246:         ),
247:         // Dropbox v2 wants the root listed as the empty string. Pagination
248:         // returns subsequent pages via list_folder/continue + a cursor.
249:         data: {'path': folder, 'recursive': false},
250:       );
251:       while (true) {
252:         final entries = (res.data['entries'] as List).cast<Map<String, dynamic>>();
253:         for (final e in entries) {
254:           out.add(DropboxEntry(
255:             name: e['name'] as String,
256:             pathDisplay: (e['path_display'] as String?) ?? '',
257:             isFolder: (e['.tag'] as String?) == 'folder',
258:           ));
259:         }
260:         final hasMore = res.data['has_more'] == true;
261:         if (!hasMore) break;
262:         final cursor = res.data['cursor'] as String;
263:         res = await _dio.post(
264:           '$_api/files/list_folder/continue',
265:           options: Options(
266:             headers: {
267:               'Authorization': 'Bearer $token',
268:               'Content-Type': 'application/json',
269:             },
270:           ),
271:           data: {'cursor': cursor},
272:         );
273:       }
274:     } on DioException catch (e) {
275:       throw DropboxException(_dropboxErrorSummary(e));
276:     }
277:     return out;
278:   }

  "CodeContent": "\"# Implementation Plan: Project Card Time & Dropbox Drawing Viewer\\n\\nThis plan outlines the changes required to display project tracked hours on the project cards, link to the drawings subfolder (`07 Drawings/Plots`) directly from the project detail view, sort drawings recent-first, and support direct PDF file opening from the Dropbox browser.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## User Review Required\\n\\n> [!IMPORTANT]\\n> **Dropbox Drawing Folder Mapping:**\\n> We will add a direct navigation option from the Project Detail screen to browse `07 Drawings/Plots`. If the folder does not exist on Dropbox when clicked, the Dropbox browser will automatically fall back to the project root directory so Tom can navigate easily.\\n>\\n> **Drawing PDF Viewer:**\\n> Tapping a PDF drawing (or any non-folder file) inside the Dropbox browser will now query a temporary download link from Dropbox and launch it in the device's system viewer/browser.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Open Questions\\n\\n* No open questions. We will proceed with this plan once approved.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### Projects Feature & Models\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [projects_provider.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/projects_provider.dart)\\n- Update `Project` model to include a `secondsTracked` field, populated from SQLite query.\\n- Update `projectsProvider` SQL query to run a `LEFT JOIN` with `time_entries` and select the sum of tracked seconds grouped by project.\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [projects_list_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/projects_list_screen.dart)\\n- Update the project card's subtitle to display: `[job_no] \u00b7 [hours]h` (e.g. `23AC-08 \u00b7 12.5h`).\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [project_detail_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/project_detail_screen.dart)\\n- Update `_ActionsCard` to add a new action list tile: **Browse Drawings (Plots)**.\\n- Wire this tile to push `/projects/browse?path=[project_path]/07 Drawings/\n<truncated 2156 bytes>",

'active',\\n      secondsTracked: (map['seconds_tracked'] as num?)?.toInt() ?? 0,\\n    );\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n\\nfinal projectsProvider = FutureProvider<List<Project>>((ref) async {\\n  final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);\\n  final maps = await db.raw.rawQuery('''\\n    SELECT p.*, COALESCE(SUM(t.seconds), 0) AS seconds_tracked\\n    FROM projects p\\n    LEFT JOIN time_entries t ON p.id = t.project_id\\n    WHERE p.status != 'archived'\\n    GROUP BY p.id\\n    ORDER BY p.job_no DESC\\n  ''');\\n  return maps.map((m) => Project.fromMap(m)).toList();\\n});\"",

+  Future<void> _openFile(String path) async {
+    setState(() {
+      _busy = true;
+      _error = null;
+    });
+    try {
+      final dropbox = ref.read(dropboxClientProvider);
+      final link = await dropbox.getTemporaryLink(path);
+      final uri = Uri.parse(link);
+      if (await canLaunchUrl(uri)) {
+        await launchUrl(uri, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);
+      } else {
+        throw Exception('Could not launch viewer for this drawing.');
+      }
+    } catch (e) {
+      setState(() => _error = _humanError(e));
+    } finally {
+      if (mounted) setState(() => _busy = false);
+    }
+  }

1: # Build a release APK for Lakou and copy it to a releases\ folder for sideload.
2: # Run from the repo root: .\scripts\build-apk.ps1
3: 
4: $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
5: 
6: $root = Split-Path -Parent $PSScriptRoot
7: $appDir = Join-Path $root 'Lakou'
8: $releasesDir = Join-Path $root 'releases'
9: New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $releasesDir | Out-Null
10: 
11: Push-Location $appDir
12: try {
13:     Write-Host 'flutter pub get...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
14:     flutter pub get
15: 
16:     Write-Host 'flutter build apk --release...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
17:     flutter build apk --release
18: 
19:     $apk = Join-Path $appDir 'build\app\outputs\flutter-apk\app-release.apk'
20:     if (-not (Test-Path $apk)) {
21:         # Fallback to Gradle's default output path when AGP/Gradle 9.x outputs there
22:         $apk = Join-Path $appDir 'android\app\build\outputs\apk\release\app-release.apk'
23:     }
24:     if (-not (Test-Path $apk)) { throw "APK not found at expected paths" }
25: 
26:     $stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd-HHmm'
27:     $dest  = Join-Path $releasesDir "lakou-$stamp.apk"
28:     Copy-Item $apk $dest -Force
29:     Write-Host "Wrote $dest" -ForegroundColor Green
30: }
31: finally {
32:     Pop-Location
33: }
34:

1: # Implementation Plan: Project Card Time & Dropbox Drawing Viewer
2: 
3: This plan outlines the changes required to display project tracked hours on the project cards, link to the drawings subfolder (`07 Drawings/Plots`) directly from the project detail view, sort drawings recent-first, and support direct PDF file opening from the Dropbox browser.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## User Review Required
8: 
9: > [!IMPORTANT]
10: > **Dropbox Drawing Folder Mapping:**
11: > We will add a direct navigation option from the Project Detail screen to browse `07 Drawings/Plots`. If the folder does not exist on Dropbox when clicked, the Dropbox browser will automatically fall back to the project root directory so Tom can navigate easily.
12: >
13: > **Drawing PDF Viewer:**
14: > Tapping a PDF drawing (or any non-folder file) inside the Dropbox browser will now query a temporary download link from Dropbox and launch it in the device's system viewer/browser.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## Open Questions
19: 
20: * No open questions. We will proceed with this plan once approved.
21: 
22: ---
23: 
24: ## Proposed Changes

26: ### Projects Feature & Models

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import '../../core/db.dart';
3: 
4: class Project {
5:   final int id;
6:   final String jobNo;
7:   final String name;
8:   final String dropboxPath;
9:   final String status;
10:   final int secondsTracked;
11: 
12:   Project({
13:     required this.id,
14:     required this.jobNo,
15:     required this.name,
16:     required this.dropboxPath,
17:     required this.status,
18:     this.secondsTracked = 0,
19:   });
20: 
21:   factory Project.fromMap(Map<String, dynamic> map) {
22:     return Project(
23:       id: map['id'] as int,
24:       jobNo: map['job_no'] as String,
25:       name: map['name'] as String,
26:       dropboxPath: map['dropbox_path'] as String,
27:       status: (map['status'] as String?) ?? 'active',
28:       secondsTracked: (map['seconds_tracked'] as num?)?.toInt() ?? 0,
29:     );
30:   }
31: }
32: 
33: 
34: final projectsProvider = FutureProvider<List<Project>>((ref) async {
35:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
36:   final maps = await db.raw.rawQuery('''
37:     SELECT p.*, COALESCE(SUM(t.seconds), 0) AS seconds_tracked
38:     FROM projects p
39:     LEFT JOIN time_entries t ON p.id = t.project_id
40:     WHERE p.status != 'archived'
41:     GROUP BY p.id
42:     ORDER BY p.job_no DESC
43:   ''');
44:   return maps.map((m) => Project.fromMap(m)).toList();
45: });
46: 
47: /// Mutations for project lifecycle (archive/delete).
48: class ProjectActions {
49:   ProjectActions(this.ref);
50:   final Ref ref;
51: 
52:   Future<void> archive(int id) async {
53:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
54:     await db.raw.update(
55:       'projects',
56:       {'status': 'archived'},
57:       where: 'id = ?',
58:       whereArgs: [id],
59:     );
60:     ref.invalidate(projectsProvider);
61:   }
62: 
63:   Future<void> unarchive(int id) async {
64:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
65:     await db.raw.update(
66:       'projects',
67:       {'status': 'active'},
68:       where: 'id = ?',
69:       whereArgs: [id],
70:     );
71:     ref.invalidate(projectsProvider);
72:   }
73: 
74:   Future<void> delete(int id) async {
75:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
76:     await db.raw.delete('projects', where: 'id = ?', whereArgs: [id]);
77:     ref.invalidate(projectsProvider);
78:   }
79: }
80: 
81: final projectActionsProvider = Provider<ProjectActions>((ref) => ProjectActions(ref));
82: 
83: final projectByJobNoProvider = FutureProvider.family<Project?, String>((ref, jobNo) async {
84:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
85:   final maps = await db.raw.query(
86:     'projects',
87:     where: 'job_no = ?',
88:     whereArgs: [jobNo],
89:     limit: 1,
90:   );
91:   if (maps.isEmpty) return null;
92:   return Project.fromMap(maps.first);
93: });

33: /// Minimal Dropbox v2 REST wrapper.
34: ///
35: /// Auth uses OAuth 2 PKCE; refresh token is stored in secure storage.
36: /// All paths are absolute Dropbox paths starting with `/` (e.g. `/Green Stories/2026/...`).
37: ///
38: /// Only the methods Lakou actually needs are implemented:

40: ///   - [listFolder] for filename collision checking

290:     required String jobNo,
291:   }) async {
292:     try {
293:       final names = await listFolder(folder);
294:       final re = RegExp(
295:         '^${RegExp.escape(datePrefix)}_${RegExp.escape(jobNo)}_.*_Text-Projectplan-(\\d{2,})\\.txt\$',
296:       );
297:       var max = 0;
298:       for (final n in names) {
299:         final m = re.firstMatch(n);
300:         if (m != null) {
301:           final v = int.tryParse(m.group(1) ?? '') ?? 0;
302:           if (v > max) max = v;
303:         }
304:       }
305:       return max + 1;
306:     } on DioException {
307:       return 1;
308:     }

12: /// Hands-on Dropbox folder browser. Tom navigates to whatever folder he wants
13: /// and chooses how to bring it in:
14: ///
15: ///  - **Scan this folder** runs the existing project importer over the current
16: ///    folder's children, treating it as a year-or-developer-style container.
17: ///  - **Add as project** treats the *currently open* folder as a single project
18: ///    and inserts one row.
19: ///  - Tap any subfolder to descend into it.
20: ///
21: /// This is the escape hatch for when the year-folder convention doesn't match
22: /// reality (e.g. archived projects, projects under a non-year folder, projects
23: /// whose folder name doesn't start with a job number).

1: import 'package:flutter_test/flutter_test.dart';
2: import 'package:lakou/features/capture/filename_builder.dart';
3: 
4: void main() {
5:   test('canonical text capture filename matches the firm spec', () {
6:     final name = FilenameBuilder.textCapture(
7:       captureDate: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
8:       jobNo: '23AC-08',
9:       projectName: '521 Springfield Ave, Newark',
10:       iteration: 1,
11:     );
12:     expect(
13:       name,
14:       '2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt',
15:     );
16:   });
17: 
18:   test('iteration zero-pads above 9', () {
19:     final name = FilenameBuilder.textCapture(
20:       captureDate: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
21:       jobNo: '26001',
22:       projectName: 'Test Project',
23:       iteration: 12,
24:     );
25:     expect(name.endsWith('_Text-Projectplan-12.txt'), isTrue);
26:   });
27: 
28:   test('expense receipt filename', () {
29:     final name = FilenameBuilder.expenseReceipt(
30:       spentAt: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
31:       jobNo: '23AC-08',
32:       vendor: 'Home Depot',
33:       amount: 47.83,
34:     );
35:     expect(name, '2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg');
36:   });
37: 
38:   test('illegal filesystem chars are stripped', () {
39:     final name = FilenameBuilder.textCapture(
40:       captureDate: DateTime(2026, 4, 6),
41:       jobNo: '23AC-08',
42:       projectName: 'Bad/Name:With*Chars?',
43:       iteration: 1,
44:     );
45:     expect(name.contains('/'), isFalse);
46:     expect(name.contains(':'), isFalse);
47:     expect(name.contains('*'), isFalse);
48:     expect(name.contains('?'), isFalse);
49:   });

1: ---
2: name: android-cli
3: description: Orchestrates Android development tasks including project creation, deployment, SDK management, and environment diagnostics using the `android` command-line tool.
4: ---
5: # Android CLI Specialist
6: 
7: This skill provides instructions for using the `android` CLI tool. The tool includes various commands for creating projects, running applications, interacting with devices, and managing the CLI environment.
8: 
9: ## Installation
10: 
11: If the android tool is not in the path install it. To install run the following command:
12: Linux: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/linux_x86_64/install.sh | bash
13: Mac: curl -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/darwin_arm64/install.sh | bash
14: Windows: curl.exe -fsSL https://dl.google.com/android/cli/latest/windows_x86_64/install.cmd -o "%TEMP%\i.cmd" && "%TEMP%\i.cmd"
15: 
16: ## SDK management
17: To manage the installation of Android SDKs and tools, use the `sdk` command. For example:
18: 
19: - `android sdk install <package>[@<version>]...`: Install specific packages. Multiple packages can be specified, separated by spaces. `<version>` defaults to latest. For example: `android sdk install platforms/android-30@2 platforms/android-34`
20: - `android sdk update [<pkg-name>]`: Update a specific package or all packages to the latest version.
21: - `android sdk remove <pkg-name>`: Remove a package from the local SDK.
22: - `android sdk list --all`: List installed and available SDK packages.
23: 
24: ## Project creation
25: Create projects from templa

1: # Lakou Project Walkthrough — Codebase Hygiene & Handoff Prompt
2: 
3: This walkthrough summarizes the cleanup of static analysis issues in Lakou and the generation of a comprehensive handoff prompt to take the app to the next level (world-class individual project management).
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## Verification Results
8: 
9: | Check | Result | Details |
10: |---|---|---|
11: | `flutter analyze` | ✅ **0 issues found** | Cleaned up all 21 warnings (unused imports, deprecations, async gaps, const constructors) |
12: | `flutter test` | ✅ **5/5 tests passed** | All widget smoke tests and filename builders run and pass |
13: | `.\scripts\build-apk.ps1` | ✅ **Build Succeeded** | Created release APK under [releases/](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/releases/) folder |
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## Changes Implemented
18: 
19: ### 1. Codebase Hygiene (Lint Fixes)
20: We resolved all compiler and code-hygiene warnings across the following files:
21: * **Unused/Unnecessary Imports:** Removed unused imports (`dart:typed_data`, `package:go_router/go_router.dart`, and `../../core/db.dart`) in `share_routing_screen.dart`, `planner_screen.dart`, `expense_capture_screen.dart`, `gmail_sync_service.dart`, and `intake_screen.dart`.
22: * **Deprecated Value Property:** Swapped deprecated `value` property with `initialValue` in `DropdownButtonFormField` widgets inside `share_routing_screen.dart`, `planner_screen.dart`, and `timetrack_screen.dart`.
23: * **Deprecated Opacity Method:** Replaced deprecated `color.withOpacity(...)` with the modern `color.withValues(alpha: ...)` in `planner_screen.dart` and `timetrack_screen.dart`.
24: * **Unused Result warning:** Changed `ref.refresh(calendarEventsProvider)` to `ref.invalidate(calendarEventsProvider)` in `planner_screen.dart` (line 594) to cleanly invalidate state without triggering unused return value warnings.
25: * **BuildContext across async gaps:** Guarded async callbacks in `trace_viewer_screen.dart` and `timetrack_screen.dart` using `context.mounted`.
26: * **Const Constructors:** Added `const` to `_SectionHeader` widgets in `billing_screen.dart` and declared `const focusSecs = ...` in `timetrack_screen.dart`.
27: 
28: ### 2. Build Script Robustness
29: * **Fallback Pathing:** Updated [build-apk.ps1](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/scripts/build-apk.ps1) to add a fallback check to `android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk`. When newer AGP 8.9.1 / Gradle 9.x versions skip the Flutter-specific build redirection, the script now cleanly copies the compiled release APK into Tom's local `releases/` directory.
30: 
31: ### 3. World-Class Claude Handoff Prompt
32: * Generated a detailed, comprehensive prompt in [claude_handoff_prompt.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/docs/claude_handoff_prompt.md) (also synced to the local artifacts folder).
33: * The prompt maps out the current repository structure, toolchain, and finished Phase 4 features, and proposes a 5-Stage Roadmap to take Lakou to a world-class level:
34:   1. **SQLite Database Auto-Sync & Backup to Dropbox** (metadata-based conflict management).
35:   2. **Enhanced Capture (Offline Voice Memos & Call log matching)**.
36:   3. **Visual Timeline Ledger & Site Visit Geotagging Map logs**.
37:   4. **ADHD Focus Shield & Ambient Drafting Audio mixer**.
38:   5. **Project Fee Estimator Worksheet & actual vs. estimated burn visualization**.
39:

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 16:03:04 UTC)

I have successfully completed the tasks requested, built the release APK, and installed it on your phone.

Here is a summary of what has been accomplished:

### Key Features Implemented

1. **Tracked Project Time on Project Cards:**
   * Updated the `Project` model and query in [projects_provider.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/projects_provider.dart) to calculate the sum of tracked time from the SQLite database.
   * Modified [projects_list_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/projects_list_screen.dart) to show total hours on each project's card subtitle (e.g. `23AC-08 · 12.5h`).

2. **Direct "Browse Drawings (Plots)" Access:**
   * Added a new list action in [project_detail_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/project_detail_screen.dart) named **Browse Drawings (Plots)**.
   * This button takes you directly to the `07 Drawings/Plots` subdirectory inside the project's Dropbox folder.
   * If the `07 Drawings/Plots` directory does not exist on Dropbox yet, it falls back to the project root directory.

3. **Recent-First Sorting & PDF Viewer Integration:**
   * Modified [dropbox_client.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/core/dropbox_client.dart) to parse the `client_modified` timestamp and added a method to request temporary download URLs for documents.
   * Updated [app.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/app.dart) and [dropbox_browser_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/dropbox_browser_screen.dart) to extract the sort query parameter and sort files descending by their modification time (most recent first) while keeping subfolders alphabetically grouped at the top.
   * Configured file taps inside the browser to fetch a temporary secure URL and open drawings using the system's default PDF viewer (e.g., Drive PDF Viewer or Chrome).

---

### Verification and Deployment

* **Compilation & Code Hygiene:** Ran `flutter analyze` (0 warnings/errors found) and verified all tests passed successfully (5/5 tests).
* **Build APK:** Ran `.\scripts\build-apk.ps1` which successfully compiled the release APK: [lakou-20260612-1202.apk](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/releases/lakou-20260612-1202.apk).
* **Push to Device:** Automatically deployed the APK to your Pixel 10 Pro XL (`192.168.50.67:42483`) via wireless ADB.

For a detailed breakdown of the verification and implemented changes, you can refer to the [walkthrough.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/.gemini/antigravity/brain/b8ee263d-5d80-47c7-b33e-d8fde3b41dd5/walkthrough.md) artifact.

Please open Lakou on your phone and test the new drawings button and time tracking display!

1: ---
2: file: lakou-task-f-fieldy.md
3: project: Lakou
4: chat: Antigravity Handoff Plan
5: date: 2026-0612
6: ---
7: 
8: # Task F: Fieldy Meeting Capture Integration
9: 
10: **Addendum to `lakou-antigravity-plan.md`. All Section 4 constraints and the Section 7 working style from that document apply here.** This task describes a new feature by outcome. Implementation approach is yours to propose before building.
11: 
12: ---
13: 
14: ## 1. The Goal in One Paragraph
15: 
16: Tom wears a Fieldy AI capture device that records and transcribes his real-world conversations to Fieldy's cloud. Site meetings, client calls, and consultant coordination all pass through it. Right now those transcripts live only in Fieldy. The goal: from inside Lakou, Tom reviews recent Fieldy conversations, assigns the relevant ones to a project, tags who was in the meeting (people from his phone contacts AND people who are not contacts), and Lakou pulls the transcript and Fieldy's analysis into that project so every meeting record lives with the project, in Dropbox, forever.
17: 
18: ---
19: 
20: ## 2. Verified Facts About the Fieldy API
21: 
22: These were confirmed by live calls against Tom's account on 2026-06-12. Build on them, but verify the authentication story (Section 2.4) before designing.
23: 
24: ### 2.1 Endpoints exist for exactly what this feature needs
25: 
26: - **List recent conversations** (reverse chronological, paginated, page size up to 50)
27: - **List conversations in a time range** (ISO 8601 bounds, range capped at 30 days per call, paginated)
28: - **Ge
<truncated 7060 bytes>
o crawl history; recent-first with manual "load more" matches how Tom will actually use it.
118: 3. **Offline behavior:** the inbox requires connectivity to list and pull, and should fail gently (gentle phrasing, retry affordance) when offline. Already-pulled meeting records are local files and database rows; viewing them must work fully offline.
119: 4. **Token storage** in `flutter_secure_storage`, consistent with existing secrets handling.
120: 5. **Do not store Fieldy credentials, raw API responses, or unassigned conversation content in Dropbox or `tpm.db`.** Only assigned meetings produce persistent artifacts.
121: 
122: ---
123: 
124: ## 5. Acceptance
125: 
126: - Open the Fieldy inbox, see recent conversations with summaries, dismiss two, assign one to a project, tag one phone contact and one free-text non-contact with a role, in under two minutes.
127: - The assigned meeting appears as a markdown record in the correct project Dropbox subfolder with a compliant filename, containing metadata, attendees, Fieldy's summary and analysis, and the raw transcript as the clearly separated backup section.
128: - The meeting appears in the project detail meeting list and updates Project Glance's last-activity surface.
129: - Assigning the same conversation again does not create a duplicate.
130: - A previously tagged non-contact autocompletes on the next tagging.
131: - Airplane mode: previously pulled meetings remain fully readable; the inbox declines gracefully.
132: - Dismissed conversations do not reappear in the inbox.
133: 
134: ---
135: 
136: ## 6. Where This Sits in the Roadmap
137: 
138: Priority relative to the main plan: after Task E, alongside or ahead of Task A at Tom's discretion. It compounds with Task E (meetings feed glance and the backlog) and shares the attendee/people groundwork that Task B's Gmail correspondence matching will eventually want. Propose your recommended sequencing when you propose your approach.
139:

1: /// App-wide constants and config for Lakou.
2: class LakouConfig {
3:   /// Root of the firm's project tree inside Dropbox.
4:   /// On the desktop side this resolves to:
5:   ///   C:\Users\thoma\Dropbox\My Documents\Green Stories
6:   /// On the Dropbox API side it's `/My Documents/Green Stories` because the
7:   /// Dropbox app has Full Dropbox access and the user's tree starts at
8:   /// `/My Documents/...`.
9:   static const String greenStoriesRoot = '/My Documents/Green Stories';
10: 
11:   /// Path to the canonical 10-folder template that gets recursively copied
12:   /// into every new project folder on creation.
13:   static const String sampleFolderStructure =
14:       '$greenStoriesRoot/Architecture/Sample Folder Structure';
15: 
16:   /// Where Lakou stores its own files inside Dropbox.
17:   static const String lakouRoot = '/Programs/Lakou';
18:   static const String dbPath = '$lakouRoot/data/tpm.db';
19:   static const String inboxPath = '$lakouRoot/inbox';
20: 
21:   /// Subfolder names within each project (must match Sample Folder Structure
22:   /// exactly — verified against the canonical template on 2026-04-08).
23:   ///
24:   /// Real structure:
25:   ///   01 Accounting / 02 Marketing / 03 Correspondence /
26:   ///   04 Meeting Agendas and Reports / 05 Memos and List /
27:   ///   06 Project Data / 07 Drawings / 08 Specifications /
28:   ///   09 Construction Phase / XX Other
29:   static const String meetingsFolder = '04 Meeting Agendas and Reports';
30:   static const String expensesFolder = '01 Accounting/03 Expenses';
31:   static const String projectDirectoryFolder = '06 Project Data/02 Project Directory';
32: 
33:   /// Anthropic model used for text extraction.
34:   static const String claudeModel = 'claude-sonnet-4-6';
35: 
36:   /// Default Pomodoro intervals (minutes).

38:   static const int breakMinutes = 10;
39: 
40:   /// Default weekly invoice reminder slot (Friday 15:00 local).

43:   static const int invoiceReminderMinute = 0;
44:

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
3: import 'package:path/path.dart' as p;
4: import 'package:path_provider/path_provider.dart';
5: 
6: final dbProvider = FutureProvider<LakouDb>((ref) async {
7:   return await LakouDb.open();
8: });
9: 
10: 
11: /// SQLite schema and accessor for Lakou.
12: ///
13: /// The live db file is intended to live inside the user's Dropbox folder
14: /// (`/Programs/Lakou/data/tpm.db`) so the desktop sees it automatically.
15: /// On Android the runtime file is opened from the app's documents dir and
16: /// synced up/down via [DropboxClient]; we never write to the Dropbox path
17: /// directly because Android sandboxing makes that unreliable.
18: class LakouDb {
19:   LakouDb._(this._db);
20:   final Database _db;
21:   Database get raw => _db;
22: 
23:   static Future<LakouDb> open() async {
24:     final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
25:     final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');
26:     final db = await openDatabase(
27:       path,
28:       version: 3,
29:       onCreate: (db, version) async {
30:         await _migrateV1(db);
31:         await _migrateV2(db);
32:         await _migrateV3(db);
33:       },
34:       onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {
35:         if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);
36:         if (oldV < 3) await _migrateV3(db);
37:       },
38:     );
39:     return LakouDb._(db);
40:   }
41: 
42:   /// v3: tasks get a planned_date column for the Morning Planner feature.
43:   static Future<void> _migrateV3(Database db) a
<truncated 7002 bytes>
ake_forms (
215:         id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
216:         project_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
217:         token       TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
218:         sent_to     TEXT,
219:         sent_at     INTEGER NOT NULL,
220:         received_at INTEGER,
221:         payload_json TEXT
222:       )
223:     ''');
224: 
225:     // Gamification
226:     batch.execute('''
227:       CREATE TABLE quests (
228:         id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
229:         title       TEXT NOT NULL,
230:         cadence     TEXT NOT NULL,            -- daily | weekly
231:         target      INTEGER NOT NULL,
232:         progress    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
233:         xp_reward   INTEGER NOT NULL,
234:         completed_at INTEGER,
235:         period_key  TEXT NOT NULL              -- e.g. 2026-W14 or 2026-04-06
236:       )
237:     ''');
238:     batch.execute('''
239:       CREATE TABLE xp_log (
240:         id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
241:         amount     INTEGER NOT NULL,
242:         source     TEXT NOT NULL,
243:         ref_id     INTEGER,
244:         awarded_at INTEGER NOT NULL
245:       )
246:     ''');
247: 
248:     // Email tagging (Gmail label TPM/<jobno>)
249:     batch.execute('''
250:       CREATE TABLE email_links (
251:         id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
252:         project_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
253:         message_id  TEXT NOT NULL,
254:         thread_id   TEXT,
255:         label       TEXT,
256:         tagged_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,
257:         UNIQUE(project_id, message_id)
258:       )
259:     ''');
260: 
261:     // Settings (key/value)
262:     batch.execute('''
263:       CREATE TABLE settings (
264:         key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
265:         value TEXT
266:       )
267:     ''');
268: 
269:     await batch.commit(noResult: true);
270:   }

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: package com.greenstories.lakou
2: 
3: import android.Manifest
4: import android.content.ContentResolver
5: import android.content.Context
6: import android.content.pm.PackageManager
7: import android.net.Uri
8: import android.provider.ContactsContract
9: import androidx.core.content.ContextCompat
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Resolves a phone number to a contact display name via ContactsContract.PhoneLookup.
13:  *
14:  * Returns null if:
15:  *   - READ_CONTACTS is not granted
16:  *   - the number is null/blank
17:  *   - no contact matches
18:  *
19:  * PhoneLookup handles number normalization (country code, formatting) for us, so a
20:  * raw "+1 973 555 0123" from an SMS will match a contact saved as "(973) 555-0123".
21:  */
22: object ContactResolver {
23:     fun displayNameFor(context: Context, number: String?): String? {
24:         if (number.isNullOrBlank()) return null
25:         if (ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(context, Manifest.permission.READ_CONTACTS)
26:             != PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED
27:         ) return null
28: 
29:         val uri = Uri.withAppendedPath(
30:             ContactsContract.PhoneLookup.CONTENT_FILTER_URI,
31:             Uri.encode(number)
32:         )
33:         val resolver: ContentResolver = context.contentResolver
34:         return runCatching {
35:             resolver.query(
36:                 uri,
37:                 arrayOf(ContactsContract.PhoneLookup.DISPLAY_NAME),
38:                 null, null, null
39:             )?.use { c ->
40:                 if (c.moveToFirst()) c.getString(0) else null
41:             }
42:         }.getOrNull()
43:     }
44: }
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart';
2: import 'package:app_links/app_links.dart';
3: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
4: import 'package:flutter_secure_storage/flutter_secure_storage.dart';
5: import 'package:dio/dio.dart';
6: import '../../core/secrets.dart';
7: import '../../core/dropbox_client.dart';
8: import 'dropbox_auth_provider.dart';
9: 
10: final appLinksHandlerProvider = Provider((ref) => AppLinksHandler(ref));
11: 
12: class AppLinksHandler {
13:   AppLinksHandler(this.ref);
14:   final Ref ref;
15:   late final _appLinks = AppLinks();
16:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
17:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
18:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
19:       resetOnError: true,
20:     ),
21:   );
22:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
23: 
24:   void init() {
25:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Initializing...');
26:     
27:     // Handle deep links when the app is already running
28:     _appLinks.uriLinkStream.listen((uri) {
29:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Stream URI: $uri');
30:       _processUri(uri);
31:     });
32: 
33:     // Handle deep link that launched the app
34:     _appLinks.getInitialLink().then((uri) {
35:       if (uri != null) {
36:         debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Initial URI: $uri');
37:         _processUri(uri);
38:       }
39:     });
40:   }
41: 
42:   void _processUri(Uri uri) {
43:     if (uri.scheme == 'lakou' && uri.host == 'oauth') {
44:       final code = uri.queryParameters['code'];
45:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code found: ${code != null}');
46:       if (code != null) {
47:         _handleDropboxCode(code);
48:       }
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> _handleDropboxCode(String code) async {
53:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Exchanging code for token...');
54:     try {
55:       final codeVerifier = await _storage.read(key: _verifierKey);
56:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code Verifier found: ${codeVerifier != null}');
57:       final dio = Dio();
58:       final res = await dio.post(
59:         'https://api.dropbox.com/oauth2/token',
60:         options: Options(contentType: Headers.formUrlEncodedContentType),
61:         data: {
62:           'code': code,
63:           'grant_type': 'authorization_code',
64:           'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
65:           'client_secret': Secrets.dropboxAppSecret,
66:           'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
67:           'code_verifier': codeVerifier,
68:         },
69:       );
70: 
71:       final refreshToken = res.data['refresh_token'] as String;
72:       final client = ref.read(dropboxClientProvider);
73:       await client.saveRefreshToken(refreshToken);
74:       
75:       // Invalidate the auth state so the UI updates
76:       ref.invalidate(dropboxAuthStateProvider);
77:       
78:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Success: Access Token Acquired');
79:     } on DioException catch (e) {
80:       final responseData = e.response?.data;
81:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error (DioException): ${e.message}\nResponse: $responseData');
82:     } catch (e) {
83:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error: $e');
84:     }
85:   }
86: }
87:

1: import 'dart:convert';
2: import 'dart:math';
3: import 'package:crypto/crypto.dart';
4: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
5: import 'package:flutter_secure_storage/flutter_secure_storage.dart';
6: import 'package:url_launcher/url_launcher.dart';
7: import '../../core/secrets.dart';
8: import '../../core/dropbox_client.dart';
9: 
10: class DropboxAuthNotifier extends StateNotifier<AsyncValue<void>> {
11:   DropboxAuthNotifier(this.client) : super(const AsyncValue.data(null));
12: 
13:   final DropboxClient client;
14:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
15:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
16:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
17:       resetOnError: true,
18:     ),
19:   );
20:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
21: 
22:   Future<void> signIn() async {
23:     state = const AsyncValue.loading();
24:     try {
25:       final codeVerifier = _generateCodeVerifier();
26:       await _storage.write(key: _verifierKey, value: codeVerifier);
27: 
28:       final codeChallenge = _generateCodeChallenge(codeVerifier);
29: 
30:       final url = Uri.https('www.dropbox.com', '/oauth2/authorize', {
31:         'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
32:         'response_type': 'code',
33:         'code_challenge': codeChallenge,
34:         'code_challenge_method': 'S256',
35:         'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
36:         'token_access_type': 'offline',
37:       });
38: 
39:       if (await canLaunchUrl(url)) {
40:         await launchUrl(url, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);
41:       } else {
42:         throw Exception('Could not launch Dropbox auth URL');
43:       }
44:       
45:       // Note: Token exchange happens via deep link handler (to be implemented)
46:       state = const AsyncValue.data(null);
47:     } catch (e, st) {
48:       state = AsyncValue.error(e, st);
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   String _generateCodeVerifier() {
53:     final random = Random.secure();
54:     final values = List<int>.generate(32, (i) => random.nextInt(256));
55:     return base64UrlEncode(values).replaceAll('=', '');
56:   }
57: 
58:   String _generateCodeChallenge(String verifier) {
59:     final bytes = utf8.encode(verifier);
60:     final digest = sha256.convert(bytes);
61:     return base64UrlEncode(digest.bytes).replaceAll('=', '');
62:   }
63: }
64: 
65: final dropboxAuthProvider = StateNotifierProvider<DropboxAuthNotifier, AsyncValue<void>>((ref) {
66:   return DropboxAuthNotifier(ref.watch(dropboxClientProvider));
67: });
68: 
69: final dropboxAuthStateProvider = FutureProvider<bool>((ref) async {
70:   final client = ref.watch(dropboxClientProvider);
71:   return client.hasRefreshToken();
72: });
73:

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 16:06:00 UTC)

Created At: 2026-06-12T16:06:00Z
Completed At: 2026-06-12T16:06:03Z
The search for "Fieldy AI API documentation developer oauth token" returned the following summary:
To access Fieldy AI's developer resources and integrate with its services, you should refer to the official API documentation at **[api.fieldy.ai/docs](https://api.fieldy.ai/docs)**.

### Key Integration Options
Fieldy AI currently provides two primary methods for external integrations:

*   **Public API (REST):** Designed for developers building custom automations, dashboards, or CRM synchronizations. This is the standard route if you are writing code to interact with your conversation data.
*   **MCP (Model Context Protocol):** Intended for connecting Fieldy to AI chat tools like Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor. This allows those AI agents to query your conversation history directly.

### Authentication & Security
*   **API Keys:** For the Public API, you generally manage access via settings within your Fieldy account. Security best practices include keeping these keys private, revoking unused keys, and fetching only the specific data needed for your workflows.
*   **Webhooks:** If you are configuring webhooks (e.g., for automation platforms like Zapier or custom endpoints), you can typically generate a "hooks token" or use an endpoint URL that includes a token parameter for security.
*   **Note on "Fieldly":** Be aware that search results may sometimes conflate "Fieldy AI" (a wearable AI note-taker) with "Fieldly" (a construction management software). Fieldly uses a standard OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow for its API, whereas Fieldy AI focuses on direct integration through API keys and MCP for its wearable data.

### Next Steps for Developers
1.  Visit **[api.fieldy.ai/docs](https://api.fieldy.ai/docs)** for the full API reference and authentication details specific to your needs.
2.  Check the **Developer Settings** in your Fieldy mobile app to manage your API keys or configure webhook endpoints.
3.  If you are building an AI agent, review the MCP integration guide provided in the official documentation to enable context-aware access to your conversations.

For the **Fieldy Public API**, all requests require your API key to be included in the `Authorization` header[1].

### Authentication Details
*   **Header Name:** `Authorization`
*   **Key Management:** You can create and manage your API keys by navigating to the **Developer Settings** section within the Fieldy app[1].
*   **Base URL:** `https://api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2`[1]

If you are building custom integrations or scripts, ensure that your API key is kept secure and not hardcoded in public repositories[2][3].

***

*Note: Be careful not to confuse "Fieldy" with "Fieldly," which is a separate platform that uses OAuth 2.0 (Bearer tokens) for authentication[4]. Always verify you are following the documentation for the correct service.*

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 16:06:08 UTC)

Created At: 2026-06-12T16:06:08Z
Completed At: 2026-06-12T16:06:11Z
The search for ""api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2" documentation OR endpoints OR swagger" returned the following summary:
Fieldy provides a Public API for developers to programmatically access conversation data, including transcripts, summaries, and tasks[1][2].

### Accessing the Public API
The Fieldy Public API is designed for developers building custom integrations, CRM syncs, or automation dashboards[3][4].

*   **Base URL:** `https://api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2`[5][2]
*   **Authentication:** The API uses Bearer token authentication (`Authorization: Bearer sk-fieldy-...`)[5][2]. You can manage your API keys within the **Developer Settings** in the Fieldy app[5][6].

### Example Endpoints
While a formal Swagger/OpenAPI documentation page is not publicly indexed, documentation provided by Fieldy confirms the following types of endpoints[5][2]:

*   **Conversations:** `GET /api/public/v2/conversations`
    *   *Parameters:* Supports filtering by `startTime`, `endTime`, and `pageSize`[5].
*   **Transcriptions:** `GET /api/public/v2/transcriptions`
    *   *Usage:* Used to fetch raw transcript segments[5][2].

### Development Resources & Alternatives
*   **Fieldy MCP (Model Context Protocol):** If your goal is to connect Fieldy to AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor to query your conversations in natural language, Fieldy recommends using their **MCP endpoint** (`https://api.fieldy.ai/mcp`) rather than the REST API[7][3][2]. 
*   **Security Best Practices:** 
    *   Keep your API keys private and never commit them to source control[5][8].
    *   Always use narrow date ranges and specific filters in your API calls to minimize data exposure[5][8].
    *   Revoke any API keys that are no longer in use via your Developer Settings[5][8].

If you are looking for a complete reference, check your **Fieldy Developer Settings** or the official **Fieldy Help Center** integrated within the app, as they provide the most up-to-date documentation for your speci

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 16:06:13 UTC)

Created At: 2026-06-12T16:06:13Z
Completed At: 2026-06-12T16:06:15Z
The search for ""fieldy" "public" "api" OR "v2" OR "conversations" OR "transcriptions" OR "transcripts"" returned the following summary:
Fieldy provides a **Public API** that allows developers to programmatically access and interact with data captured by the device[1][2].

### Key Details:
*   **API Access:** The Fieldy Public API enables developers to build custom automations, dashboards, and CRM integrations[1]. It provides REST access to various data points, including **conversations, transcripts, transcript segments, tasks, speaker profiles, and memory templates**[2][3].
*   **API Version:** The base URL for the API is `https://api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2`[3].
*   **Additional Integrations:** In addition to the REST API, Fieldy supports **Webhooks** to trigger events in real-time and provides **MCP (Model Context Protocol) access**, allowing compatible AI clients (such as Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor) to work with Fieldy data directly[1][2].
*   **Important Distinction:** Note that there are two distinct entities commonly referred to as "Fieldy":
    *   **Fieldy (AI Wearable):** The AI-powered wearable note-taker that captures and transcribes conversations[4][5]. This is the entity associated with the conversation/transcription API described above[2][3].
    *   **Fieldy (Field Service Management):** A separate software platform designed for field service operations (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, or landscaping businesses)[6][7][8]. Ensure you are referencing the correct service when reviewing documentation[7][9].

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 16:06:17 UTC)

Created At: 2026-06-12T16:06:17Z
Completed At: 2026-06-12T16:06:19Z
The search for "site:intercom.help/ "api.fieldy.ai" OR "/api/public/v2"" returned the following summary:
The information regarding Fieldy's integration options can be found in their documentation[1][2]. Fieldy provides two primary ways to connect your data to external tools[1][2]:

### 1. Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Designed for AI chat tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor, allowing you to query your Fieldy conversations using natural language[1][2].
*   **Endpoint:** `https://api.fieldy.ai/mcp`[1][3]
*   **Usage:** Configure your AI tool's MCP settings to use this endpoint, then authorize via your browser[1][3].

### 2. Public API (v2)
Designed for developers to build custom integrations, automations, and dashboards using REST[1][2].
*   **Base URL:** `https://api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2`[1]
*   **Authentication:** Requires an API key (created in Fieldy Developer Settings) included in the Authorization header (`Authorization: Bearer sk-fieldy-...`)[1].

**Security Recommendations:**
*   Keep your API keys private and never commit them to source control[4].
*   Revoke any unused keys via Developer Settings[4].
*   Only grant MCP access to AI tools you trust and verify their requested permissions[4].

Sources:
[1] [intercom.help](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQH88XQWk7o213NN6QZjknYfOO8olt8DHP-xF0GQwfW0BRIUo_iI9X0JpKJPVvkAzn88QU2mTs8kLBmzSc_9G50dYFIXuaCrZ3nsegN8GvGsP3GOkSy3FhqQwPDZ8dEx6fugx88B6AfwVLLBSWD1eYobdIencEq7WuLW7Skbq1zwHxnMPx3C3dMx6TU32Z8bSackn_ZhDDhXYui-k8CM)

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 16:06:20 UTC)

Created At: 2026-06-12T16:06:20Z
Completed At: 2026-06-12T16:06:23Z
The search for "site:intercom.help "fieldy" public api v2" returned the following summary:
The "Fieldy" Public API v2 is not an Intercom API; it is a separate service for **Fieldy**, an AI-powered Field Service Management (FSM) platform[1][2].

While search results appear in an Intercom-branded help center (likely due to documentation hosting or integration partnerships)[1], the API itself is specific to Fieldy's own infrastructure[1].

### Key Details for Fieldy Public API v2
*   **Base URL:** `https://api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2`[1][3]
*   **Purpose:** Provides REST access to your Fieldy data, including conversations, transcripts, tasks, and speakers[1][4].
*   **Authentication:** Requires an API key in the `Authorization` header using the format `Bearer sk-fieldy-...`[1][3]. You can manage these keys in the **Developer Settings** section of the Fieldy app[1][3].
*   **Usage Tip:** Fieldy also offers an **MCP (Model Context Protocol)** endpoint (`https://api.fieldy.ai/mcp`) if you are looking to connect your conversations to AI chat tools like Claude or ChatGPT rather than building a custom integration[1][5].

If you were looking for Intercom's own API documentation, it is hosted at [developers.intercom.com](https://developers.intercom.com), and the latest versions follow a `v2.x` numbering scheme (e.g., v2.15)[6][7].
