1: /// Builds canonical filenames per the firm's standard.
2: /// See `docs/filename-spec.md` for the source of truth.
3: class FilenameBuilder {
4:   /// Strips characters illegal in NTFS/FAT filenames but keeps spaces, commas,
5:   /// and hyphens (Tom's existing folder names depend on those).
6:   static String _safe(String s) =>
7:       s.replaceAll(RegExp(r'[\\/:*?"<>|]'), '').trim();
8: 
9:   static String _yyyymmdd(DateTime d) {
10:     final y = d.year.toString().padLeft(4, '0');
11:     final m = d.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
12:     final dd = d.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
13:     return '$y-$m$dd';
14:   }
15: 
16:   /// Helper for folder listing prefix
17:   static String yyyymmddForPrefix(DateTime d) => _yyyymmdd(d);
18: 
19:   /// Returns the SMS capture filename, e.g.
20:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`.
21:   ///
22:   /// [projectName] is whatever short label the user wants between job number and
23:   /// the suffix — typically the project's display name without the job number.
24:   /// [iteration] is 1-based; pass the next available NN after listing the folder.
25:   static String textCapture({
26:     required DateTime captureDate,
27:     required String jobNo,
28:     required String projectName,
29:     required int iteration,
30:   }) {
31:     final n = iteration.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
32:     return '${_yyyymmdd(captureDate)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_${_safe(projectName)}_Text-Projectplan-$n.txt';
33:   }
34: 
35:   /// Returns the receipt filename, e.g.
36:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg`.
37:   static String expenseReceipt({
38:     required DateTime spentAt,
39:     required String jobNo,
40:     required String vendor,
41:     required double amount,
42:     String extension = 'jpg',
43:   }) {
44:     final amt = amount.toStringAsFixed(2);
45:     final v = _safe(vendor).replaceAll(' ', '');
46:     return '${_yyyymmdd(spentAt)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_Expense_${v}_$amt.$extension';
47:   }
48: }

28:   final m = d.minute.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
29:   return '$h:$m';
30: }
31: 
32: String _humanDuration(int seconds) {
33:   final h = seconds ~/ 3600;
34:   final m = (seconds % 3600) ~/ 60;
35:   if (h == 0) return '${m}m';
36:   if (m == 0) return '${h}h';
37:   return '${h}h ${m}m';
38: }
39: 
40: /// Captures + time entries logged today, newest first. Used by both the
41: /// Today screen and the Dashboard's "today strip".
42: final todayItemsProvider = FutureProvider<List<TodayItem>>((ref) async {
43:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
44:   final now = DateTime.now();
45:   final dayStart = DateTime(now.year, now.month, now.day).millisecondsSinceEpoch;
46: 
47:   final captures = await db.raw.rawQuery('''
48:     SELECT c.captured_at, c.filename, c.project_id, p.name as project_name, p.job_no
49:     FROM captures c
50:     LEFT JOIN
<truncated 353 bytes>
   WHERE t.started_at >= ? AND t.ended_at IS NOT NULL
60:     ORDER BY t.started_at DESC
61:   ''', [dayStart]);
62: 
63:   final items = <TodayItem>[];
64: 
65:   for (final c in captures) {
66:     final ts = c['captured_at'] as int;
67:     final project = (c['project_name'] as String?) ?? 'Unfiled';
68:     final job = (c['job_no'] as String?) ?? '';
69:     items.add(TodayItem(
70:       icon: Icons.edit_note,
71:       title: 'Captured to $project',
72:       subtitle: job.isEmpty ? null : job,
73:       timeLabel: _hhmm(ts),
74:       timestampMs: ts,
75:       projectId: c['project_id'] as int?,
76:     ));
77:   }
78: 
79:   for (final t in entries) {
80:     final ts = t['started_at'] as int;
81:     final secs = t['seconds'] as int;
82:     final project = (t['project_name'] as String?) ?? 'Unfiled';
83:     final job = (t['job_no'] as String?) ?? '';
84:     items.add(TodayItem(
85:       icon: Icons.timer,
86:       title: '${_humanDuration(secs)} on $project',
87:       subtitle: job.isEmpty ? null : job,
88:       timeLabel: _hhmm(ts),
89:       timestampMs: ts,
90:       projectId: t['project_id'] as int?,
91:     ));
92:   }
93: 
94:   // Tasks completed today
95:   final tasks = await db.raw.rawQuery('''
96:     SELECT t.title, t.completed_at, t.project_id, p.name as project_name, p.job_no
97:     FROM tasks t
98:     LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = t.project_id
99:     WHERE t.completed = 1 AND t.completed_at >= ?
100:     ORDER BY t.completed_at DESC
101:   ''', [dayStart]);
102:   for (final t in tasks) {
103:     final ts = t['completed_at'] as int;
104:     final job = (t['job_no'] as String?) ?? '';
105:     items.add(TodayItem(
106:       icon: Icons.check_box,
107:       title: t['title'] as String,
108:       subtitle: job.isEmpty ? null : job,
109:       timeLabel: _hhmm(ts),

  "ArtifactMetadata": "{\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Complete codebase audit of Task F (Fieldy Integration) and the capture archiving bug fix, outlining all parts, verifying them against build plans, and noting the resolution of issues.\",\"UserFacing\":true}",
  "CodeContent": "\"# Codebase Audit Report: Fieldy Integration (Task F) & Capture Archival Fix\\n\\nThis audit outlines all the implemented components of the Task F feature (Fieldy Ambient Wearable Capture) and the capture review race condition fixes, mapping them back to the original design intentions.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. Database Schema & Migrations\\n\\n### Code Implementation: [db.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/core/db.dart)\\n\\n- **Audit Findings:**\\n  - Migrated SQLite database to version `4`.\\n  - Added table `dismissed_fieldy_conversations` to track conversations the user dismisses.\\n  - Added table `meetings` to map assigned recordings to their respective project IDs.\\n  - Added table `meeting_attendees` linking `meetings` to `consultants` with specific roles.\\n- **Plan & Intent Alignment:**\\n  - **100% Aligned.** The schema supports cascade deletes and references `projects` and `consultants` correctly. Reuse of the `consultants` table for meeting attendees matches the requirement to prevent profile duplication and consolidate the project directory.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. Fieldy REST API Client\\n\\n### Code Implementation: [fieldy_client.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/core/fieldy_client.dart)\\n\\n- **Audit Findings:**\\n  - Implements Bearer token lookup from `flutter_secure_storage` under the key `fieldy.api_key`.\\n  - Configures `Dio` clients with connection retry parameters and exception filters.\\n  - Correctly paginates raw transcription streams through automatic cursor recursion.\\n- **Plan & Intent Alignment:**\\n  - **100% Aligned.** API endpoints utilize the secure `https://api.fieldy.ai/api/public/v2` namespace. Credentials are kept safe in encrypted SharedPreferences/Keychain.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Triage & Inbox UI\\n\\n### Code Implementation: [fieldy_inbox_screen.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/fieldy/fieldy_inbox_screen.dart)\\n\\n- **Audit Findings:**\\n  - Connect view handles API Key input.\\n \n<truncated 2917 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Create audit report artifact for Task F and capture screen fixes\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",
  "TargetFile": "\"C:\\\\Users\\\\thoma\\\\.gemini\\\\antigravity\\\\brain\\\\b8ee263d-5d80-47c7-b33e-d8fde3b41dd5\\\\audit_report.md\"",
  "toolAction": "\"Writing audit report artifact\"",

}

1: # Walkthrough: Project Time & Dropbox Drawings Browser
2: 
3: This walkthrough summarizes the implementation of project tracked hours on project cards, direct navigation to the project's `07 Drawings/Plots` directory, recent-first sorting for drawings, and opening drawing PDFs directly from the Dropbox browser.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## Verification Results
8: 
9: | Check | Result | Details |
10: |---|---|---|
11: | `flutter analyze` | ✅ **0 issues found** | Verified clean build with no static analysis warnings or errors. |
12: | `flutter test` | ✅ **5/5 tests passed** | Run widget boot tests and filename builders. All passed. |
13: | Build Release APK | ✅ **Build Succeeded** | Run `.\scripts\build-apk.ps1` to output `lakou-20260612-1202.apk`. |
14: | Deploy to Device | ✅ **Successfully Installed** | Installed APK onto Pixel 10 Pro XL (`192.168.50.67:42483`) via wireless ADB. |
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## Changes Implemented
19: 
20: ### 1. Project Time Tracking & Display
21: * **SQLite Query Integration:** Modified [projects_provider.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/projects_provider.dart) to include a `secondsTracked` field on the `Project` model and populated it via a `LEFT JOIN` on `time_entries`.
22: * **Projects Card Subtitle:** Modified [projects_list_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/projects_list_screen.dart) to show the job number and the total tracked hours on each project card (e.g. `23AC-08 · 12.5h
<truncated 675 bytes>
: * **Temporary Download Links:** Implemented `getTemporaryLink(path)` in `DropboxClient` to fetch a viewer URL for specific drawings.
28: * **Routing Integration:** Updated `/projects/browse` in [app.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/app.dart) to parse the `sort` query parameter and pass it to `DropboxBrowserScreen` as `sortByRecent`.
29: * **Recent-First Sorting:** Modified [dropbox_browser_screen.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/projects/dropbox_browser_screen.dart) to sort files by modification date descending (newest first) when `sortByRecent` is enabled, while keeping folders grouped alphabetically at the top.
30: * **PDF File Launcher:** Handled tapping on files inside the browser to fetch a temporary download URL and launch it directly in the system's default PDF viewer or browser using `url_launcher`.
31: * **Fallback Navigation:** Handled non-existent folders gracefully. If `07 Drawings/Plots` does not exist on Dropbox yet, the browser automatically falls back to the project root directory.
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## Instructions for Manual Testing on Phone
36: 
37: 1. **Open Projects List:** Open the Lakou app on your phone. You should see the total tracked time displayed next to the job number on each project card.
38: 2. **Access Project Drawings:** Open a project and tap the **Browse Drawings (Plots)** action button.
39:    * If the `07 Drawings/Plots` directory exists under the project's Dropbox folder, it will open directly.
40:    * If it doesn't exist, it will gracefully fall back to the project root folder.
41: 3. **Check Sorting:** Inside the directory, the drawings (PDFs) will be sorted with the most recently modified ones at the top.
42: 4. **Open Drawings:** Tap on a drawing file. It will fetch a temporary link and prompt you to open the file in the system's default PDF viewer (e.g., Drive PDF Viewer or Chrome).
43:

Task F: Fieldy Ambient Capture Integration\\n* **SQLite Tables (V4 Schema):** Implemented schema migration creating `dismissed_fieldy_conversations`, `meetings`, and `meeting_attendees` tables in [db.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/core/db.dart).\\n* **Fieldy API Client:** Created [fieldy_client.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/core/fieldy_client.dart) supporting token lookup, listing, conversation fetching, and paginated transcription retrieval.\\n* **Triage / Inbox Screen:** Created [fieldy_inbox_screen.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/fieldy/fieldy_inbox_screen.dart) to connect/disconnect Fieldy, triage ambient recordings, dismiss conversations, or map them to projects using the vocab match engine.\\n* **Assign Meeting Dialog:** Created [assign_meeting_dialog.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/fieldy/assign_meeting_dialog.dart) featuring:\\n  - Project selection and contact tagging.\\n  - Integration with device contacts using the `flutter_contacts` plugin.\\n  - Automatic compilation of formatted Markdown minutes including metadata, F\n<truncated 2904 bytes>",

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 22:08:09 UTC)

I have completed a thorough audit of the entire codebase and implemented the fixes to verify that all code matches the build plans and design intent. 

Here is a summary of the accomplishments:

1. **Fixed the Archival Race Condition:** 
   - Modified [capture_review_screen.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/capture/capture_review_screen.dart) to block back-swiping/popping when a save is in progress (`_isSaving == true`), preventing the pop callback from invoking `_archiveCapture()` and accidentally archiving SMS/MMS texts.

2. **Standardized Date & Filename Formatting:** 
   - Adjusted [assign_meeting_dialog.dart](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/fieldy/assign_meeting_dialog.dart) to use `FilenameBuilder.yyyymmddForPrefix` to ensure all meeting minutes saved to Dropbox strictly follow the firm standard date convention (`YYYY-MMDD` e.g., `2026-0612_23AC-08_Meeting_Title.md`).

3. **Resolved Warnings & Compilation Errors:**
   - Fixed the `maxHeight` container constraints in `assign_meeting_dialog.dart`.
   - Corrected the `actions` array type signature in `fieldy_inbox_screen.dart` (avoiding nullable widgets inside lists).
   - Removed unused imports and variables in `fieldy_client.dart`, `fieldy_inbox_screen.dart`, `assign_meeting_dialog.dart`, and `today_provider.dart`.

4. **Successful Build & Deployment:**
   - Ran `flutter clean` to release intermediate build folders locked by the Dropbox desktop application.
   - Ran `flutter analyze` and got **0 issues/warnings**.
   - Ran `flutter test` and got **5/5 tests passing**.
   - Successfully compiled the release APK (`lakou-20260612-1807.apk`) and sideloaded it directly onto your Pixel 10 Pro XL.

For a full breakdown of the codebase architecture, review the newly created [audit_report.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/.gemini/antigravity/brain/b8ee263d-5d80-47c7-b33e-d8fde3b41dd5/audit_report.md) and the updated [walkthrough.md](file:///C:/Users/thoma/.gemini/antigravity/brain/b8ee263d-5d80-47c7-b33e-d8fde3b41dd5/walkthrough.md).

# Implementation Plan v2: Sensor Leak (done) + Full Fresh-Eyes Audit Findings

Updated by Claude (Fable 5) after independently repeating the same exercise: outline every module, trace each outline against the PRD (`docs/sophiaspace-prd.md`) + build dashboard intent, then walk backwards through the code. Verified against the codebase as of **v1.4.1 (build 9)**, 56/56 JUnit tests green.

## User Review Required

> Item 1 (your original finding) is **already applied and verified** — it compiles and all tests pass, but it is **not yet released**: the APK on the phone/website (v1.4.1) predates the edit. Any further changes from this plan must ship through the release checklist at the bottom (this project versions every published build).

## Open Questions

None blocking. Items 5–6 are optional polish — Tom can veto.

## Status of the Original Plan

### ✅ [DONE — VERIFIED] [SensorSpikeScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/spike/SensorSpikeScreen.kt)
Your `DisposableEffect` → `LifecycleResumeEffect` migration is applied correctly (lines 55–58), matches the `SkyViewScreen` pattern, compiles, and the full suite passes. ---

## New Findings (same audit method, deeper pass)

### 1. [MODIFY] [OrientationProvider.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/sky/OrientationProvider.kt) + [SkyViewScreen.kt](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Solar%20System/app/app/src/main/java/com/example/sophiaspace/ui/sky/SkyViewScreen.kt) — calibration prompt never fires (PRD-intent gap)
**Outline vs intent:** the PRD risk register specifies a *"kid-friendly figure-8 calibration ('draw a lazy 8 in the air!')"* in response to sensor noise. The script line exists (`SKY_SENSOR_ON` mentions 
<truncated 5405 bytes>
 pass; add a small JUnit for the nearest-planet selection if it's extracted into a pure helper (preferred).

### Manual (on the Pixel, wireless adb available at 192.168.50.67:38001 — port may rotate)
1. Item 1: in Sky View magic-window mode, wave the phone near a magnet/metal until accuracy drops — the lazy-8 line should play once, and not again within a minute.
2. Item 2: bump `SYNTH_VERSION`, reinstall, confirm sounds still play (new files render) and old `synth_*.wav` are gone from cache.
3. Item 3: tonight Venus and Jupiter are both up — point between them; the ring should claim the closer one.
4. Item 7: dashboard renders, Phase 0 shows the new ticks, no console errors.

## 🔒 Project guardrails (non-negotiable — from the PRD + research brief)
- **Pedagogy:** never an Earth-shadow/creeping-disc moon visual; the Sun never "moves/sets/sleeps" (we spin); too-hot/too-cold stays in habitable-zone "lived/always" language — never weather/season words; no fail-states (misses are gags).
- **Privacy:** CAMERA stays the only permission; preview-only, never stored/sent; no internet/location/accounts.
- **Release checklist for ANY shipped change:** bump `versionCode` + `versionName` in `app/build.gradle.kts` → `gradlew testDebugUnitTest assembleDebug` → copy APK to `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\SophiasSpace-<version>.apk` (never overwrite a published version) → update the site card's version chip + download href in `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\index.html` → append a dated entry to the dashboard `CHANGES` array → `adb install -r` to the Pixel. Next free slot: **v1.4.2 (build 10)** — note your already-applied SensorSpike fix rides in this release.
- **Site caveat:** `Y:\...\index.html` is sometimes regenerated by an external site build — re-read before editing, and know that the download-popup bypass fix (`programmaticDownload` flag) and the Sophia's Space card must survive any regeneration.

11: import kotlin.math.atan2

13: 
14: /** Where the back of the phone is pointing, in sky coordinates. */
15: data class Pointing(
16:     /** Degrees clockwise from magnetic north, 0..360. */
17:     val azimuthDeg: Float,
18:     /** Degrees above the horizon, -90..90. */
19:     val altitudeDeg: Float
20: )
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Rotation-vector sensor → "magic window" look direction.
24:  *
25:  * The look vector is the device's -Z axis (out the back of the phone) expressed
26:  * in world ENU coordinates: hold the phone up like a window and the reported
27:  * azimuth/altitude is the patch of sky behind it. The raw vector is smoothed
28:  * with an exponential moving average and re-normalized — smoothing Euler angles
29:  * directly causes the classic 359°→0° snap.
30:  *
31:  * Register in onResume / unregister in onPause; the sensor must never run
32:  * while the screen is off (battery).
33:  */
34: class OrientationProvider(context: Context) : SensorEventListener {
35: 
36:     private val sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE) as SensorManager
37:     private val rotationSensor
<truncated 1089 bytes>
5: 
66:     fun stop() {
67:         sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
68:         primed = false
69:         _pointing.value = null
70:     }
71: 
72:     override fun onSensorChanged(event: SensorEvent) {
73:         if (event.sensor.type != Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR) return
74:         SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector(rotationMatrix, event.values)
75: 
76:         // World-frame direction of the device -Z axis (out the back of the phone).
77:         // getRotationMatrixFromVector returns R with world = R * device, row-major.
78:         val lookX = -rotationMatrix[2]
79:         val lookY = -rotationMatrix[5]
80:         val lookZ = -rotationMatrix[8]
81: 
82:         if (!primed) {
83:             smoothX = lookX; smoothY = lookY; smoothZ = lookZ
84:             primed = true
85:         } else {
86:             val k = smoothing
87:             smoothX += k * (lookX - smoothX)
88:             smoothY += k * (lookY - smoothY)
89:             smoothZ += k * (lookZ - smoothZ)
90:         }
91:         val len = sqrt(smoothX * smoothX + smoothY * smoothY + smoothZ * smoothZ)
92:         if (len < 1e-6f) return
93:         val nx = smoothX / len
94:         val ny = smoothY / len
95:         val nz = smoothZ / len
96: 
97:         // World frame: X = east, Y = magnetic north, Z = up.
98:         val azimuth = Math.toDegrees(atan2(nx.toDouble(), ny.toDouble())).toFloat().let {
99:             if (it < 0f) it + 360f else it
100:         }
101:         val altitude = Math.toDegrees(asin(nz.toDouble().coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0))).toFloat()
102: 
103:         _pointing.value = Pointing(azimuth, altitude)
104:     }
105: 
106:     override fun onAccuracyChanged(sensor: Sensor?, accuracy: Int) = Unit
107: 
108:     companion object {
109:         const val DEFAULT_SMOOTHING = 0.18f
110:         /** Snappier tracking for camera AR — the overlay must keep up. */
111:         const val AR_SMOOTHING = 0.45f
112:     }
113: }

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.audio
2: 
3: import android.content.Context

5: import android.media.SoundPool
6: import java.io.BufferedOutputStream
7: import java.io.DataOutputStream
8: import java.io.File
9: import java.io.FileOutputStream
10: import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap
11: import kotlin.math.PI
12: import kotlin.math.sin
13: 
14: /**
15:  * Tiny 8-bit-style synthesizer for interaction juice (SophiaQuest lineage).
16:  *
17:  * The six effects are rendered to small WAV files in the cache dir once, then
18:  * played through a single shared [SoundPool] — which owns the thread/voice
19:  * pool, enforces a concurrency cap, and applies volume live. (The previous
20:  * thread-per-sound AudioTrack scheme could exhaust the device's AudioTrack
21:  * limit under a five-year-old's tap rate, and leaked tracks on error paths.)
22:  *
23:  * The NarrationManager ducks this while speaking via [volumeScale].
24:  */
25: object SoundSynth {
26:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
27: 
28:     @Volatile
29:     var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
30: 
31:     private enum class Effect { POP, WHOOSH, SIZZLE, DRIFT, CHIME, FANFARE }
32: 
33:     @Volatile
34:     private var pool: SoundPool? = null
35:     private val soundIds = ConcurrentHashMap<Effect, Int>()
36:     private val readySamples = ConcurrentHashMap<Int, Boolean>()
37: 
38:     /** Call once from MainActivity.onCreate. Idempotent; renders off-thread. */
39:     fun init(context: Context) {
40:
<truncated 5644 bytes>
t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
176:         }
177:         return buffer
178:     }
179: 
180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
200: 
201:     /** Minimal 16-bit mono PCM WAV writer. */
202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }
219: }
220:

1: package com.example.sophiaspace.ui.components
2: 
3: import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4: import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
5: import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
6: import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
7: import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableFloatStateOf
8: import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
9: import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
10: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
11: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
12: import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
13: import kotlin.math.sin
14: import kotlin.random.Random
15: 
16: private class TwinkleStar(val fx: Float, val fy: Float, val size: Float, val phase: Float, val speed: Float)
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Gently twinkling star backdrop drawn behind every screen.
20:  * Stars are generated once per composition; only their alpha animates.
21:  */
22: @Composable
23: fun SpaceBackground(modifier: Modifier = Modifier, starCount: Int = 90) {
24:     val stars = remember {
25:         val rng = Random(42)
26:         List(starCount) {
27:             TwinkleStar(
28:                 fx = rng.nextFloat(),
29:                 fy = rng.nextFloat(),
30:                 size = 1.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 2.2f,
31:                 phase = rng.nextFloat() * 6.2832f,
32:                 speed = 0.4f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.9f
33:             )
34:         }
35:     }
36: 
37:     var time by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
38:     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
39:         val start = System.nanoTime()
40:         while (true) {
41:             androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos { now ->
42:                 time = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f
43:             }
44:         }
45:     }
46: 
47:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
48:         for (s in stars) {
49:             val alpha = 0.25f + 0.55f * (0.5f + 0.5f * sin(time * s.speed + s.phase))
50:             drawCircle(
51:                 color = Color.White.copy(alpha = alpha),
52:                 radius = s.size,
53:                 center = Offset(s.fx * size.width, s.fy * size.height)
54:             )
55:         }
56:     }
57: }

clamp bug before it shipped). Design spec saved to docs/sophiaspace-place-to-orbit-design.md. APK built and published

allowBackup=false). Core engines: GravitySim (symplectic Euler, natural units, softening/clamps/bounds), 
ConsequenceDetector (escape/crash/hot/cold/wobbly/restored with debounce), single shared NarrationManager (fixes 
SophiaQuest L6), SoundSynth port, DataStore settings. Screens: Home space map, Sandbox with Earth-Moon + Sun 
playgrounds (drag/fling, trails, Earth moods, temperature tint, Put It Back tween, confetti + sticker on 
hand-restore), Grown-up Corner (gate, voice sliders, reset), sensor spike screen. 18 JUnit tests green; assembleDebug 
builds. NEXT: install on the Pixel, check TTS latency + sensor math, then GATE 1 with Sophia.'},

23:         assertTrue(g0 in 0.0..360.0)
24:         val advance = ((g1 - g0) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0
25:         assertEquals(0.9856, advance, 0.01) // sidereal gain ≈ 0.9856°/day
26:     }
27: 
28:     @Test
29:     fun `polaris altitude is about the observer latitude, azimuth about north`() {
30:         // Polaris: RA 37.95°, Dec +89.26°. Observer at Denver-ish (39.7N, -105.0E).
31:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
32:         val (alt, az) = AstroMath.altAz(37.95, 89.26, 39.7, -105.0, jd)
33:         assertEquals(39.7, alt, 1.5)
34:         assertTrue("azimuth should be near north: $az", az < 3.0 || az > 357.0)
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     fun `a star on the celestial equator culminates at 90 minus latitude`() {
39:         var maxAlt = -90.0
40:         val jd0 = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
41:         for (m in 0 until 1440) {
42:             val (alt, _) = AstroMath.altAz(180.0, 0.0, 40.0, 0.0, jd0 + m / 1440.0)
43:             if (alt > maxAlt) maxAlt = alt
44:         }
45:         assertEquals(50.0, maxAlt, 0.5)
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     fun `projection - the look-center lands at the origin and behind-camera culls`() {
50:         val center = AstroMath.project(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)
51:         assertEquals(0.0, center!!.first, 1e-9)
52:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
53:         assertNull(AstroMath.project(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
54:     }
55: 
56:     @Test
57:     fun `projection - a star above the look-center projects upward`() {
58:         val p = AstroMath.project(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
59:         assertTrue("higher star should have +y: ${p.second}", p.second > 0.0)
60:         assertEquals(0.0, p.first, 1e-6)
61:     }
62: 
63:     @Test
64:     fun `camera projection - center at origin, offsets are exact tangents, behind culls`() {
65:         val center = AstroMath.projectCamera(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
66:         assertEquals(0.0, center.first, 1e-9)
67:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
68:         // 10° above the look-center must land at exactly tan(10°) —
69:         // that's what makes the overlay pan at the camera image's rate.
70:         val above = AstroMath.projectCamera(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
71:         assertEquals(Math.tan(Math.toRadians(10.0)), above.second, 1e-6)
72:         assertEquals(0.0, above.first, 1e-6)
73:         assertNull(AstroMath.projectCamera(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
74:     }
75: }

11: /**
12:  * Time + coordinate math for the sky view. Pure Kotlin (JVM-testable).
13:  * Conventions: angles in degrees at the API surface, radians inside;
14:  * azimuth clockwise from north; altitude up from the horizon.
15:  */
16: object AstroMath {
17: 
18:     /** Unix epoch millis → Julian day. */
19:     fun julianDay(epochMillis: Long): Double = epochMillis / 86400000.0 + 2440587.5
20: 
21:     /** Greenwich mean sidereal time, degrees 0..360. */
22:     fun gmstDeg(jd: Double): Double {
23:         val d = jd - 2451545.0
24:         val t = d / 36525.0
25:         var g = 280.46061837 + 360.98564736629 * d + 0.000387933 * t * t - t * t * t / 38710000.0
26:         g %= 360.0
27:         return if (g < 0) g + 360.0 else g
28:     }
29: 
30:     /** RA/Dec (deg) → (altitudeDeg, azimuthDeg-from-north) for an observer. */
31:     fun altAz(raDeg: Double, decDeg: Double, latDeg: Double, lonDeg: Double, jd: Double): Pair<Double, Double> {
32:         val lstDeg = gmstDeg(jd) + lonDeg
33:         val h = Math.toRadians(((lstDeg - raDeg) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0)
34:         val dec = Math.toRadians(decDeg)
35:         val lat = Math.toRadians(latDeg)
36:         val sinAlt = sin(dec) * sin(lat) + cos(dec) * cos(lat) * cos(h)
37:         val alt = asin(sinAlt.coerceIn(-1
<truncated 1806 bytes>

73:         val forward = s.first * f.first + s.second * f.second + s.third * f.third
74:         return Triple(right, up, forward)
75:     }
76: 
77:     /**
78:      * Stereographic projection of (alt, az) about a look-center, returning
79:      * tangent-plane coordinates (x grows toward the right of the view,
80:      * y up), or null when the point is behind the camera (cull). Used for
81:      * the drawn-sky modes — pleasing wide-angle look.
82:      */
83:     fun project(altDeg: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
84:         val (right, up, forward) = cameraFrame(altDeg, azDeg, centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg)
85:         if (forward <= 0.05) return null // behind / >87° away
86:         val k = 2.0 / (1.0 + forward)
87:         return (k * right) to (k * up)
88:     }
89: 
90:     /**
91:      * Rectilinear (gnomonic) projection — what a camera LENS does. Returns
92:      * tan-angle offsets; multiply by the focal length in pixels and the
93:      * overlay pans at exactly the camera image's rate. AR mode only.
94:      */
95:     fun projectCamera(altDeg: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
96:         val (right, up, forward) = cameraFrame(altDeg, azDeg, centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg)
97:         if (forward <= 0.15) return null // rectilinear blows up near 90° off-axis
98:         return (right / forward) to (up / forward)
99:     }
100: 
101:     /** Great-circle separation between two (alt, az) directions, degrees. */
102:     fun angularSeparationDeg(alt1: Double, az1: Double, alt2: Double, az2: Double): Double {
103:         val a1 = Math.toRadians(alt1)
104:         val a2 = Math.toRadians(alt2)
105:         val dAz = Math.toRadians(az1 - az2)
106:         val cosSep = sin(a1) * sin(a2) + cos(a1) * cos(a2) * cos(dAz)
107:         return Math.toDegrees(acos(cosSep.coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0)))
108:     }
109: }

132:     fun moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd: Double): Double {
133:         val t = centuries(jd)
134:         val lp = norm360(218.3164477 + 481267.88123421 * t)                 // mean longitude
135:         val mp = Math.toRadians(norm360(134.9633964 + 477198.8675055 * t)) // moon anomaly
136:         val m = Math.toRadians(norm360(357.5291092 + 35999.0502909 * t))   // sun anomaly
137:         val d = Math.toRadians(norm360(297.8501921 + 445267.1114034 * t))  // elongation
138:         val f = Math.toRadians(norm360(93.2720950 + 483202.0175233 * t))   // arg of latitude
139:         return norm360(
140:             lp + 6.289 * sin(mp) + 1.274 * sin(2 * d - mp) + 0.658 * sin(2 * d) -
141:                 0.186 * sin(m) - 0.214 * sin(2 * mp) - 0.114 * sin(2 * f)
142:         )
143:     }

150:         val mp = Math.toRadians(norm360(134.9633964 + 477198.8675055 * t))
151:         val beta = Math.toRadians(5.128 * sin(f) + 0.281 * sin(mp + f) - 0.280 * sin(f - mp))
152:         val x = cos(beta) * cos(lambda)
153:         val y = cos(beta) * sin(lambda)
154:         val z = sin(beta)
155:         return eclipticToRaDec(x, y, z)
156:     }

161: }

### Manual (on the Pixel, wireless adb available at 192.168.50.67:38001 — port may rotate)

## 🔒 Project guardrails (non-negotiable — from the PRD + research brief)
- **Pedagogy:** never an Earth-shadow/creeping-disc moon visual; the Sun never "moves/sets/sleeps" (we spin); too-hot/too-cold stays in habitable-zone "lived/always" language — never weather/season words; no fail-states (misses are gags).
- **Privacy:** CAMERA stays the only permission; preview-only, never stored/sent; no internet/location/accounts.
- **Release checklist for ANY shipped change:** bump `versionCode` + `versionName` in `app/build.gradle.kts` → `gradlew testDebugUnitTest assembleDebug` → copy APK to `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\SophiasSpace-<version>.apk` (never overwrite a published version) → update the site card's version chip + download href in `Y:\ReynoldsFamily\builds\index.html` → append a dated entry to the dashboard `CHANGES` array → `adb install -r` to the Pixel. Next free slot: **v1.4.2 (build 10)** — note your already-applied SensorSpike fix rides in this release.
- **Site caveat:** `Y:\...\index.html` is sometimes regenerated by an external site build — re-read before editing, and know that the download-popup bypass fix (`programmaticDownload` flag) and the Sophia's Space card must survive any regeneration.

14: /** Where the back of the phone is pointing, in sky coordinates. */
15: data class Pointing(
16:     /** Degrees clockwise from magnetic north, 0..360. */
17:     val azimuthDeg: Float,
18:     /** Degrees above the horizon, -90..90. */
19:     val altitudeDeg: Float
20: )
21: 
22: /**
23:  * Rotation-vector sensor → "magic window" look direction.
24:  *
25:  * The look vector is the device's -Z axis (out the back of the phone) expressed
26:  * in world ENU coordinates: hold the phone up like a window and the reported
27:  * azimuth/altitude is the patch of sky behind it. The raw vector is smoothed
28:  * with an exponential moving average and re-normalized — smoothing Euler angles
29:  * directly causes the classic 359°→0° snap.
30:  *
31:  * Register in onResume / unregister in onPause; the sensor must never run
32:  * while the screen is off (battery).
33:  */
34: class OrientationProvider(context: Context) : SensorEventListener {
35: 
36:     private val sensorManager = context.getSystemService(Context.SENSOR_SERVICE) as SensorManager
37:     private val rotationSensor
<truncated 1089 bytes>
5: 
66:     fun stop() {
67:         sensorManager.unregisterListener(this)
68:         primed = false
69:         _pointing.value = null
70:     }
71: 
72:     override fun onSensorChanged(event: SensorEvent) {
73:         if (event.sensor.type != Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR) return
74:         SensorManager.getRotationMatrixFromVector(rotationMatrix, event.values)
75: 
76:         // World-frame direction of the device -Z axis (out the back of the phone).
77:         // getRotationMatrixFromVector returns R with world = R * device, row-major.
78:         val lookX = -rotationMatrix[2]
79:         val lookY = -rotationMatrix[5]
80:         val lookZ = -rotationMatrix[8]
81: 
82:         if (!primed) {
83:             smoothX = lookX; smoothY = lookY; smoothZ = lookZ
84:             primed = true
85:         } else {
86:             val k = smoothing
87:             smoothX += k * (lookX - smoothX)
88:             smoothY += k * (lookY - smoothY)
89:             smoothZ += k * (lookZ - smoothZ)
90:         }
91:         val len = sqrt(smoothX * smoothX + smoothY * smoothY + smoothZ * smoothZ)
92:         if (len < 1e-6f) return
93:         val nx = smoothX / len
94:         val ny = smoothY / len
95:         val nz = smoothZ / len
96: 
97:         // World frame: X = east, Y = magnetic north, Z = up.
98:         val azimuth = Math.toDegrees(atan2(nx.toDouble(), ny.toDouble())).toFloat().let {
99:             if (it < 0f) it + 360f else it
100:         }
101:         val altitude = Math.toDegrees(asin(nz.toDouble().coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0))).toFloat()
102: 
103:         _pointing.value = Pointing(azimuth, altitude)
104:     }
105: 
106:     override fun onAccuracyChanged(sensor: Sensor?, accuracy: Int) = Unit
107:

110:         /** Snappier tracking for camera AR — the overlay must keep up. */
111:         const val AR_SMOOTHING = 0.45f
112:     }

14: /**
15:  * Tiny 8-bit-style synthesizer for interaction juice (SophiaQuest lineage).
16:  *
17:  * The six effects are rendered to small WAV files in the cache dir once, then
18:  * played through a single shared [SoundPool] — which owns the thread/voice
19:  * pool, enforces a concurrency cap, and applies volume live. (The previous
20:  * thread-per-sound AudioTrack scheme could exhaust the device's AudioTrack
21:  * limit under a five-year-old's tap rate, and leaked tracks on error paths.)
22:  *
23:  * The NarrationManager ducks this while speaking via [volumeScale].
24:  */
25: object SoundSynth {
26:     private const val SAMPLE_RATE = 22050
27: 
28:     @Volatile
29:     var volumeScale: Float = 1.0f
30: 
31:     private enum class Effect { POP, WHOOSH, SIZZLE, DRIFT, CHIME, FANFARE }
32: 
33:     @Volatile
34:     private var pool: SoundPool? = null
35:     private val soundIds = ConcurrentHashMap<Effect, Int>()
36:     private val readySamples = ConcurrentHashMap<Int, Boolean>()
37: 
38:     /** Call once from MainActivity.onCreate. Idempotent; renders off-thread. */
39:     fun init(context: Context) {
40:
<truncated 5644 bytes>
t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
176:         }
177:         return buffer
178:     }
179: 
180:     private fun renderFanfare(): ShortArray {
181:         val numSamples = samples(600)
182:         val buffer = ShortArray(numSamples)
183:         for (i in 0 until numSamples) {
184:             val t = i.toDouble() / SAMPLE_RATE
185:             val noteIdx = i / (numSamples / 4)
186:             val freq = when (noteIdx) {
187:                 0 -> 523.25
188:                 1 -> 783.99
189:                 2 -> 659.25
190:                 else -> 1046.50
191:             }
192:             val vibrato = if (noteIdx == 3) sin(2.0 * PI * 12.0 * t) * 8.0 else 0.0
193:             val envelope = 1.0 - (i.toDouble() / numSamples)
194:             buffer[i] = (sin(2.0 * PI * (freq + vibrato) * t) * 12000.0 * envelope).toInt().toShort()
195:         }
196:         return buffer
197:     }
198: 
199:     private fun samples(durationMs: Int) = (SAMPLE_RATE * (durationMs / 1000.0)).toInt()
200: 
201:     /** Minimal 16-bit mono PCM WAV writer. */
202:     private fun writeWav(file: File, pcm: ShortArray) {
203:         DataOutputStream(BufferedOutputStream(FileOutputStream(file))).use { o ->
204:             fun le32(v: Int) {
205:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
206:                 o.write((v shr 16) and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 24) and 0xFF)
207:             }
208:             fun le16(v: Int) {
209:                 o.write(v and 0xFF); o.write((v shr 8) and 0xFF)
210:             }
211:             val dataSize = pcm.size * 2
212:             o.writeBytes("RIFF"); le32(36 + dataSize); o.writeBytes("WAVE")
213:             o.writeBytes("fmt "); le32(16); le16(1); le16(1)
214:             le32(SAMPLE_RATE); le32(SAMPLE_RATE * 2); le16(2); le16(16)
215:             o.writeBytes("data"); le32(dataSize)
216:             for (s in pcm) le16(s.toInt() and 0xFFFF)
217:         }
218:     }
219: }

use drag mode only.",
87:                 style = MaterialTheme.typography.titleMedium,
88:                 color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error
89:             )

91:         }
92: 
93:         val p = pointing
94:         Text(
95:             text = if (p == null) "Waiting for sensor…"
96:             else "Azimuth  %.1f°\nAltitude %.1f°".format(p.azimuthDeg, p.altitudeDeg),
97:             fontSize = 34.sp,
98:             fontWeight = FontWeight.ExtraBold,
99:             color = StarGold
100:         )
101:         Text(
102:             "Hold the phone up like a window.\nAzimuth: 0 = north, 90 = east.\nAltitude: 0 = horizon, 90 = straight up.",
103:             style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium,
104:             color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.onSurfaceVariant
105:         )
106: 
107:         // Simple dial: needle = azimuth, ring fill = altitude.
108:         Canvas(
109:             modifier = Modifier
110:                 .fillMaxWidth()
111:                 .height(280.dp)
112:         ) {
113:             val c = Offset(size.width / 2f, size.height / 2f)
114:             val r = min(size.width, size.height) / 2.4f
115:             drawCircle(SpaceSurface, r, c)
116:             drawCircle(IceBlue.copy(alpha = 0.5f), r, c, style = Stroke(width = 4f))
117:             if (p != null) {
118:                 val azRad = Math.toRadians(p.azimuthDeg.toDouble())
119:                 val needle = Offset(
120:                     c.x + (sin(azRad) * r * 0.9).toFloat(),
121:                     c.y - (cos(azRad) * r * 0.9).toFloat()
122:                 )
123:                 drawLine(StarGold, c, needle, strokeWidth = 8f)
124:                 val altFrac = ((p.altitudeDeg + 90f) / 180f).coerceIn(0f, 1f)
125:                 drawCircle(StarGold.copy(alpha = 0.7f), r * 0.12f + r * 0.5f * altFrac, c, style = Stroke(width = 6f))
126:             }
127:         }
128:     }
129: }

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: package com.example.sophiaspace.ui.components
2: 
3: import androidx.compose.foundation.Canvas
4: import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
5: import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
6: import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
7: import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableFloatStateOf
8: import androidx.compose.runtime.remember
9: import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
10: import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
11: import androidx.compose.ui.geometry.Offset
12: import androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color
13: import kotlin.math.sin
14: import kotlin.random.Random
15: 
16: private class TwinkleStar(val fx: Float, val fy: Float, val size: Float, val phase: Float, val speed: Float)
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Gently twinkling star backdrop drawn behind every screen.
20:  * Stars are generated once per composition; only their alpha animates.
21:  */
22: @Composable
23: fun SpaceBackground(modifier: Modifier = Modifier, starCount: Int = 90) {
24:     val stars = remember {
25:         val rng = Random(42)
26:         List(starCount) {
27:             TwinkleStar(
28:                 fx = rng.nextFloat(),
29:                 fy = rng.nextFloat(),
30:                 size = 1.2f + rng.nextFloat() * 2.2f,
31:                 phase = rng.nextFloat() * 6.2832f,
32:                 speed = 0.4f + rng.nextFloat() * 0.9f
33:             )
34:         }
35:     }
36: 
37:     var time by remember { mutableFloatStateOf(0f) }
38:     LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
39:         val start = System.nanoTime()
40:         while (true) {
41:             androidx.compose.runtime.withFrameNanos { now ->
42:                 time = (now - start) / 1_000_000_000f
43:             }
44:         }
45:     }
46: 
47:     Canvas(modifier = modifier) {
48:         for (s in stars) {
49:             val alpha = 0.25f + 0.55f * (0.5f + 0.5f * sin(time * s.speed + s.phase))
50:             drawCircle(
51:                 color = Color.White.copy(alpha = alpha),
52:                 radius = s.size,
53:                 center = Offset(s.fx * size.width, s.fy * size.height)
54:             )
55:         }
56:     }
57: }

fixed in the Earth-Moon scene), habitable-zone reframe of TOO_HOT/TOO_COLD, and a new once-per-playground "closer 
means faster" narration beat. 7 new PlacementTest JUnit tests - 25/25 green (the dead-center-drop test caught a real 
clamp bug before it shipped). Design spec saved to docs/sophiaspace-place-to-orbit-design.md. APK built and published 
as SophiasSpace-1.0.0.apk on the family builds site. NEXT: install on the Pixel - TTS latency + sensor spike checks, 
then GATE 1 with Sophia.'},
docs\sophiaspace-build-dashboard.html:392:  {date:'2026-06-09', title:'Phase 0 + Phase 1 built - sandbox ready for 
GATE 1', body:'Full Android scaffold (Kotlin/Compose, SDK 36/24, portrait, zero-permission manifest, 
allowBackup=false). Core engines: GravitySim (symplectic Euler, natural units, softening/clamps/bounds), 
ConsequenceDetector (escape/crash/hot/cold/wobbly/restored with debounce), single shared NarrationManager (fixes 
SophiaQuest L6), SoundSynth port, DataStore settings. Screens: Home space map, Sandbox with Earth-Moon + Sun 
playgrounds (drag/fling, trails, Earth moods, temperature tint, Put It Back tween, confetti + sticker on 
hand-restore), Grown-up Corner (gate, voice sliders, reset), sensor spike screen. 18 JUnit tests green; assembleDebug 
builds. NEXT: install on the Pixel, check TTS latency + sensor math, then GATE 1 with Sophia.'},

8: class AstroMathTest {
9: 
10:     // 2026-06-10 00:00:00 UTC in epoch millis.
11:     private val nowMillis = 1781049600000L
12: 
13:     @Test
14:     fun `julian day of the unix epoch is 2440587_5`() {
15:         assertEquals(2440587.5, AstroMath.julianDay(0L), 1e-9)
16:     }
17: 
18:     @Test
19:     fun `gmst is in 0-360 and gains a sidereal degree per day`() {
20:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
21:         val g0 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd)
22:         val g1 = AstroMath.gmstDeg(jd + 1.0)
23:         assertTrue(g0 in 0.0..360.0)
24:         val advance = ((g1 - g0) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0
25:         assertEquals(0.9856, advance, 0.01) // sidereal gain ≈ 0.9856°/day
26:     }
27: 
28:     @Test
29:     fun `polaris altitude is about the observer latitude, azimuth about north`() {
30:         // Polaris: RA 37.95°, Dec +89.26°. Observer at Denver-ish (39.7N, -105.0E).
31:         val jd = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
32:         val (alt, az) = AstroMath.altAz(37.95, 89.26, 39.7, -105.0, jd)
33:         assertEquals(39.7, alt, 1.5)
34:         assertTrue("azimuth should be near north: $az", az < 3.0 || az > 357.0)
35:     }
36: 
37:     @Test
38:     fun `a star on the celestial equator culminates at 90 minus latitude`() {
39:         var maxAlt = -90.0
40:         val jd0 = AstroMath.julianDay(nowMillis)
41:         for (m in 0 until 1440) {
42:             val (alt, _) = AstroMath.altAz(180.0, 0.0, 40.0, 0.0, jd0 + m / 1440.0)
43:             if (alt > maxAlt) maxAlt = alt
44:         }
45:         assertEquals(50.0, maxAlt, 0.5)
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     fun `projection - the look-center lands at the origin and behind-camera culls`() {
50:         val center = AstroMath.project(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)
51:         assertEquals(0.0, center!!.first, 1e-9)
52:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
53:         assertNull(AstroMath.project(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
54:     }
55: 
56:     @Test
57:     fun `projection - a star above the look-center projects upward`() {
58:         val p = AstroMath.project(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
59:         assertTrue("higher star should have +y: ${p.second}", p.second > 0.0)
60:         assertEquals(0.0, p.first, 1e-6)
61:     }
62: 
63:     @Test
64:     fun `camera projection - center at origin, offsets are exact tangents, behind culls`() {
65:         val center = AstroMath.projectCamera(45.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
66:         assertEquals(0.0, center.first, 1e-9)
67:         assertEquals(0.0, center.second, 1e-9)
68:         // 10° above the look-center must land at exactly tan(10°) —
69:         // that's what makes the overlay pan at the camera image's rate.
70:         val above = AstroMath.projectCamera(55.0, 180.0, 45.0, 180.0)!!
71:         assertEquals(Math.tan(Math.toRadians(10.0)), above.second, 1e-6)
72:         assertEquals(0.0, above.first, 1e-6)
73:         assertNull(AstroMath.projectCamera(-45.0, 0.0, 45.0, 180.0))
74:     }

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: package com.example.sophiaspace.sky
2: 
3: import kotlin.math.acos
4: import kotlin.math.asin
5: import kotlin.math.atan2
6: import kotlin.math.cos
7: import kotlin.math.sin
8: import kotlin.math.sqrt
9: import kotlin.math.tan
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Time + coordinate math for the sky view. Pure Kotlin (JVM-testable).
13:  * Conventions: angles in degrees at the API surface, radians inside;
14:  * azimuth clockwise from north; altitude up from the horizon.
15:  */
16: object AstroMath {
17: 
18:     /** Unix epoch millis → Julian day. */
19:     fun julianDay(epochMillis: Long): Double = epochMillis / 86400000.0 + 2440587.5
20: 
21:     /** Greenwich mean sidereal time, degrees 0..360. */
22:     fun gmstDeg(jd: Double): Double {
23:         val d = jd - 2451545.0
24:         val t = d / 36525.0

30:     /** RA/Dec (deg) → (altitudeDeg, azimuthDeg-from-north) for an observer. */
31:     fun altAz(raDeg: Double, decDeg: Double, latDeg: Double, lonDeg: Double, jd: Double): Pair<Double, Double> {
32:         val lstDeg = gmstDeg(jd) + lonDeg
33:         val h = Math.toRadians(((lstDeg - raDeg) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0)
34:         val dec = Math.toRadians(decDeg)
35:         val lat = Math.toRadians(latDeg)
36:         val sinAlt = sin(dec) * sin(lat) + cos(dec) * cos(lat) * cos(h)
37:         val alt = asin(sinAlt.coerceIn(-1
<truncated 1806 bytes>

73:         val forward = s.first * f.first + s.second * f.second + s.third * f.third
74:         return Triple(right, up, forward)
75:     }
76: 
77:     /**
78:      * Stereographic projection of (alt, az) about a look-center, returning
79:      * tangent-plane coordinates (x grows toward the right of the view,
80:      * y up), or null when the point is behind the camera (cull). Used for
81:      * the drawn-sky modes — pleasing wide-angle look.
82:      */
83:     fun project(altDeg: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
84:         val (right, up, forward) = cameraFrame(altDeg, azDeg, centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg)
85:         if (forward <= 0.05) return null // behind / >87° away
86:         val k = 2.0 / (1.0 + forward)
87:         return (k * right) to (k * up)
88:     }
89: 
90:     /**
91:      * Rectilinear (gnomonic) projection — what a camera LENS does. Returns
92:      * tan-angle offsets; multiply by the focal length in pixels and the
93:      * overlay pans at exactly the camera image's rate. AR mode only.
94:      */
95:     fun projectCamera(altDeg: Double, azDeg: Double, centerAltDeg: Double, centerAzDeg: Double): Pair<Double, Double>? {
96:         val (right, up, forward) = cameraFrame(altDeg, azDeg, centerAltDeg, centerAzDeg)
97:         if (forward <= 0.15) return null // rectilinear blows up near 90° off-axis
98:         return (right / forward) to (up / forward)
99:     }
100: 
101:     /** Great-circle separation between two (alt, az) directions, degrees. */
102:     fun angularSeparationDeg(alt1: Double, az1: Double, alt2: Double, az2: Double): Double {
103:         val a1 = Math.toRadians(alt1)
104:         val a2 = Math.toRadians(alt2)
105:         val dAz = Math.toRadians(az1 - az2)
106:         val cosSep = sin(a1) * sin(a2) + cos(a1) * cos(a2) * cos(dAz)
107:         return Math.toDegrees(acos(cosSep.coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0)))
108:     }
109: }

31:         ),

128:         return Math.toDegrees(acos(cosSep.coerceIn(-1.0, 1.0)))
129:     }

131:     /** Truncated lunar theory: geocentric ecliptic longitude, degrees. */
132:     fun moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd: Double): Double {
133:         val t = centuries(jd)
134:         val lp = norm360(218.3164477 + 481267.88123421 * t)                 // mean longitude
135:         val mp = Math.toRadians(norm360(134.9633964 + 477198.8675055 * t)) // moon anomaly
136:         val m = Math.toRadians(norm360(357.5291092 + 35999.0502909 * t))   // sun anomaly
137:         val d = Math.toRadians(norm360(297.8501921 + 445267.1114034 * t))  // elongation
138:         val f = Math.toRadians(norm360(93.2720950 + 483202.0175233 * t))   // arg of latitude
139:         return norm360(
140:             lp + 6.289 * sin(mp) + 1.274 * sin(2 * d - mp) + 0.658 * sin(2 * d) -
141:                 0.186 * sin(m) - 0.214 * sin(2 * mp) - 0.114 * sin(2 * f)
142:         )
143:     }
144: 
145:     /** Geocentric RA/Dec of the Moon (latitude included). */
146:     fun moonRaDec(jd: Double): Pair<Double, Double> {
147:         val t = centuries(jd)
148:         val lambda = Math.toRadians(moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd))
149:         val f = Math.toRadians(norm360(93.2720950 + 483202.0175233 * t))
150:         val mp = Math.toRadians(norm360(134.9633964 + 477198.8675055 * t))
151:         val beta = Math.toRadians(5.128 * sin(f) + 0.281 * sin(mp + f) - 0.280 * sin(f - mp))
152:         val x = cos(beta) * cos(lambda)
153:         val y = cos(beta) * sin(lambda)
154:         val z = sin(beta)
155:         return eclipticToRaDec(x, y, z)
156:     }
157: 
158:     /** Tonight's-real-Moon elongation for the phase glyph (radians). */
159:     fun moonElongationRad(jd: Double): Double =
160:         Math.toRadians(norm360(moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd) - sunEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd)))
161: }

7: class EphemerisTest {

9:     private fun jdOf(year: Int, dayOfYear: Int): Double =
10:         2451544.5 + (year - 2000) * 365.25 + dayOfYear
11: 
12:     @Test
13:     fun `mercury never strays more than ~28 degrees from the sun`() {
14:         for (d in 0 until 720 step 10) {
15:             val jd = jdOf(2026, d)
16:             val sep = Ephemeris.sunSeparationDeg(Ephemeris.Planet.MERCURY, jd)
17:             assertTrue("mercury elongation $sep on day $d", sep < 29.5)
18:         }
19:     }

24:             val jd = jdOf(2026, d)
25:             val sep = Ephemeris.sunSeparationDeg(Ephemeris.Planet.VENUS, jd)
26:             assertTrue("venus elongation $sep on day $d", sep < 48.5)
27:         }
28:     }
29: 
30:     @Test
31:     fun `sun ecliptic longitude is ~0 at march equinox and ~90 at june solstice`() {
32:         // 2026-03-20 ≈ day 79; 2026-06-21 ≈ day 172.
33:         val equinox = Ephemeris.sunEclipticLongitudeDeg(jdOf(2026, 79))
34:         val solstice = Ephemeris.sunEclipticLongitudeDeg(jdOf(2026, 172))
35:         assertEquals(0.0, ((equinox + 180.0) % 360.0) - 180.0, 2.5)
36:         assertEquals(90.0, solstice, 2.5)
37:     }
38: 
39:     @Test
40:     fun `moon longitude moves ~13 degrees per day`() {
41:         val jd = jdOf(2026, 161)
42:         val l0 = Ephemeris.moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd)
43:         val l1 = Ephemeris.moonEclipticLongitudeDeg(jd + 1.0)
44:         val move = ((l1 - l0) % 360.0 + 360.0) % 360.0
45:         assertEquals(13.2, move, 2.5)
46:     }
47: 
48:     @Test
49:     fun `all bodies produce finite RA Dec for a year of dates`() {
50:         for (d in 0 until 365 step 7) {
51:             val jd = jdOf(2026, d)
52:             // Geocentric Earth is the zero vector — skip it, as the UI does.
53:             for (p in Ephemeris.Planet.entries.filter { it != Ephemeris.Planet.EARTH }) {

## 👤 User Request (2026-06-12 22:27:38 UTC)

ok - i let claudecode look at the codebase and assume what it does as well - read what it sent me and let me know whats missing to let claude code know or what is misunderstood "I'll read the Lakou codebase from scratch and audit it. Let me start by exploring the project structure.

Here's what Lakou is and does:

---

## Lakou — What It Is

Lakou (Haitian Kreyòl for "communal courtyard") is a **sideloaded Android app** built for your one-person architecture firm, Green Stories. It's a project management tool designed specifically around how *you* work — AuDHD-friendly, SMS-first, Dropbox-backed.

## What It Does (Feature Outline)

### Core Loop: Capture → Organize → Track → Bill
1. **SMS Capture** — Intercepts incoming texts, runs them through Claude AI (or a local regex fallback) to extract job numbers, action items, and summaries, then saves the parsed note as a markdown file into the correct Dropbox project folder.

2. **Project Management** — Imports projects from your existing Dropbox folder structure (recognizes job number patterns like `23AC-08` or `26001`), maintains a local SQLite database as a metadata cache, and can clone a 10-folder template for new projects.

3. **Time Tracking** — Pomodoro-style timer with categories (design/meeting/site visit), background notifications, and interrupted-timer recovery across reboots. Tracks hours against contract fees to warn about fee burn.

4. **Task Planner** — Morning Planner for assigning tasks to specific days, with backlog roll-forward. Tasks link to projects with due dates.

5. **Expense Capture** — Camera capture with OCR/Claude extraction for vendor and amount, saved to the project's accounting folder with standardized filenames.

6. **Billing & Fee Burn** — Tracks hours × rate against contract fee. Warns when you're approaching or exceeding budget. Milestone-based billing phases. Invoice reminder triggers.

7. **Gmail Sync** — Searches emails by job number, saves correspondence to Dropbox,
<truncated 177 bytes>
th attendees.

9. **Daily Summary ("Today")** — Timeline view aggregating captures, time entries, completed tasks, and meetings for the current day.

### Supporting Features
- **Dock Mode** — Auto-activates when charging in landscape (docked tablet), shows an optimized dashboard
- **Intake Forms** — Token-based forms for client onboarding
- **Gamification** — Daily/weekly quests with XP rewards (capture N texts, log N hours)
- **Watched Senders** — Filter which SMS senders trigger the capture flow
- **File Sharing** — Android share-intent routing (shared PDFs, images, etc. get routed to the right project)

### Design Philosophy
- **Gentle UX** — Rotating phrases with no exclamation marks, warm-shifting background tint by time of day, soft animations, confetti on rewards
- **AuDHD-optimized** — Predictable patterns, no urgency language, fee burn threshold set at 100% (not 80%) to reduce pre-emptive anxiety
- **Dropbox is the source of truth** — The app is a smart interface on top of your existing folder structure, not a replacement for it
- **Architecture-firm conventions** — Filenames follow drafting standards, projects organized by year/developer, 4-letter "sheet codes" (HOME, CAPT, PROJ) echo architectural drawing conventions

### Tech Stack
- **Flutter/Dart** with Riverpod state management and GoRouter
- **SQLite** local database (v4 schema, 15+ tables)
- **Dropbox API** via OAuth2 PKCE for file storage
- **Claude API** (claude-sonnet-4-6) for SMS parsing
- **Native Android** MethodChannels for SMS, timer notifications, and RCS
- **Material 3** theme with custom warm palette (cream canvas, sage green accents)

---

**In short:** Lakou turns your phone into a project management hub that catches SMS from contractors/clients, files everything into the right Dropbox folder, tracks your time and money, and does it all in a way that doesn't stress you out."

7: class SmsListener {
8:   static const _channel = MethodChannel('lakou/sms');
9:   final Ref ref;
10: 
11:   SmsListener(this.ref);

14:     Trace.log('sms', 'listener init');
15:     // Ask the host for SMS + contacts permissions on first launch.
16:     // No-op if already granted.
17:     _channel.invokeMethod('requestPermissions').catchError((e) {

23:       Trace.log('sms', 'channel call ${call.method}');
24:       if (call.method == 'onSmsReceived') {
25:         final Map<dynamic, dynamic> args = call.arguments;
26:         final String sender = args['sender'] ?? 'Unknown';
27:         final String? displayName = args['displayName'] as String?;
28:         final String body = args['body'] ?? '';
29:         final int timestamp = args['timestamp'] ?? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch;
30: 
31:         Trace.log('sms', 'onSmsReceived',
32:             data: 'from=${displayName ?? sender} len=${body.length}');
33: 
34:         // Watched-senders gate. Empty list = capture all (default during
35:         // shake-out). Once Tom curates a list, only matching senders flow
36:         // into the capture screen — others stay logged in the inbox.
37:         final watched = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
38:         if (!senderMatches(watched: watched, sender: sender, displayName: displayName)) {
39:           Trace.log('sms', 'filtered (not watched)', data: displayName ?? sender);

41:         }
42: 
43:         ref.read(incomingCaptureProvider.notifier).state = IncomingCapture(
44:           rawText: body,
45:           sender: sender,

48:         );
49:       }
50:     });

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: import 'dart:convert';
2: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
3: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
4: import '../../core/db.dart';
5: 
6: 
7: 
8: /// Watched senders. Stored in `settings` table at key `watched_senders` as a
9: /// JSON list of strings. Each entry is matched case-insensitively against the
10: /// resolved contact name AND the raw sender number for an incoming SMS.
11: ///
12: /// Empty list = no filter; every SMS is captured (the current default during
13: /// shake-out). Once Tom has a curated list, only matching senders trigger the
14: /// capture flow.
15: const _key = 'watched_senders';
16: 
17: final watchedSendersProvider = FutureProvider<List<String>>((ref) async {
18:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
19:   final rows = await db.raw.query('settings', where: 'key = ?', whereArgs: [_key], limit: 1);
20:   if (rows.isEmpty) return const [];
21:   final raw = rows.first['value'] as String?;
22:   if (raw == null || raw.isEmpty) return const [];
23:   try {
24:     return (jsonDecode(raw) as List).cast<String>();
25:   } catch (_) {
26:     return const [];
27:   }
28: });
29: 
30: class WatchedSendersActions {
31:   WatchedSendersActions(this.ref);
32:   final Ref ref;
33: 
34:   Future<void> _write(List<String> list) async {
35:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
36:     await db.raw.insert(
37:       'settings',
38:       {'key': _key, 'value': jsonEncode(list)},
39:       conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace,
40:     );
41:     ref.invalidate(watchedSendersProvider);
42:   }
43: 
44:   Future<void> add(String value) async {
45:     final v = value.trim();
46:     if (v.isEmpty) return;
47:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
48:     if (current.any((e) => e.toLowerCase() == v.toLowerCase())) return;
49:     await _write([...current, v]);
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> remove(String value) async {
53:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
54:     await _write(current.where((e) => e != value).toList());
55:   }
56: }
57: 
58: final watchedSendersActionsProvider =
59:     Provider((ref) => WatchedSendersActions(ref));
60: 
61: /// Synchronous filter helper. The receiver isolate can't await Riverpod, but
62: /// the SmsListener (in the main isolate) can read this once and call.
63: bool senderMatches({
64:   required List<String> watched,
65:   required String sender,
66:   String? displayName,
67: }) {
68:   if (watched.isEmpty) return true; // empty filter = capture all
69:   final s = sender.toLowerCase();
70:   final d = displayName?.toLowerCase();
71:   for (final w in watched) {
72:     final wl = w.toLowerCase();
73:     if (s.contains(wl)) return true;
74:     if (d != null && d.contains(wl)) return true;
75:   }
76:   return false;
77: }
78:

## 🤖 Assistant (2026-06-12 22:27:49 UTC)

Here is what is misunderstood or missing in that summary so you can update Claude Code:

### 1. The Misunderstanding: Time Tracking
* **Not a Pomodoro Timer:** The PM tracker is **not** a Pomodoro-style timer (which implies forced 25-minute work intervals and 5-minute break rotations). 
* **What it actually is:** It is a professional, continuous **Time Tracker** with categories (`design`, `meeting`, `site visit`) that runs continuously in the foreground/background (recovering across phone reboots). It also features a **Manual Time Entry Dialog** specifically to allow logging hours worked in the **past** (back-logging).

---

### 2. What is Missing (Core Features & Recent Updates)
* **Direct Access to Drawings (Recent Plots First):** On the project card, there is a dedicated action button that jumps directly to the project's `07 Drawings/Plots` Dropbox directory, sorted with the **most recent drawing first** so you can pull up the latest PDFs instantly.
* **Offline-First Core Directive:** The app is completely offline-first. The local SQLite database caches all metadata (projects, times, meetings, metrics), meaning the app is fully readable and functional offline, with Dropbox/API sync happening gracefully when connected.
* **Attendee Contact Tagging Integration:** Triage of Fieldy ambient meetings allows tagging attendees by drawing on the phone's native address book (via `flutter_contacts`) or custom roles (e.g. `GC Superintendent`, `Owner Rep`), automatically building/updating the unified `consultants` database.
* **Fieldy File Structure Spec:** Ambient recording minutes are saved under `04 Meeting Agendas and Reports/` with the firm's strict `YYYY-MMDD` filename specification (using `FilenameBuilder.yyyymmddForPrefix`) containing structured metadata, summaries, tagged participants, and raw transcripts.

---

## Antigravity Prompt: Lakou Codebase Gap Report

Here's what the full audit found — organized by severity. The app is remarkably complete (no stubs, no TODOs, no placeholder screens), but there are real gaps in three categories: **things that will crash**, **things that are half-wired**, and **things the app claims to do but doesn't actually do yet**.

---

### WILL CRASH (Fix Before Next Build)

**1. Missing Calendar Permissions — BLOCKER**
- `CalendarHelper.kt` calls `READ_CALENDAR` and `WRITE_CALENDAR` operations but **neither permission is declared in AndroidManifest.xml**. The planner screen's calendar integration (`getTodayEvents()`, `insertEvent()`) will throw `SecurityException` on every device.
- Fix: Add both permissions to manifest + add runtime permission checks in `CalendarHelper.kt` and `MainActivity.kt` before calling calendar methods.

**2. Claude API Response Parsing is Fragile**
- `claude_extractor.dart` assumes `response['content'][0]['text']` exists. If Claude returns a tool_use block, an error response, or an empty content array, this crashes. Needs defensive parsing.

**3. Meetings Table Schema Assumption**
- `today_provider.dart` queries the `meetings` table without checking if the V4 migration has run. On a dev device with an old database, this SQL query crashes. Needs a migration-version guard or a try-catch.

---

### HALF-WIRED (Features That Exist But Don't Fully Work)

**4. Auth State Management Gap**
- `dropbox_auth_provider.dart`: `signIn()` sets state to `.data(null)` immediately after launching the browser URL. The UI has no way to know the token exchange is still pending. Should stay `.loading()` until the de
<truncated 3214 bytes>
mats: `04/06/2026`, `2026-04-06`, `April 6`, `next Tuesday`. When offline, date extraction is nearly useless.

**16. Quests/Gamification is Retired But Tables Remain**
- `quests_screen.dart` redirects to `/today`. The `quests` and `xp_log` tables still exist in the schema but nothing writes to them. Either remove the tables in a V5 migration or rebuild the gamification feature.

---

### CODE QUALITY (Not Blocking But Worth Knowing)

**17. Static Mutable MethodChannel** — `SmsReceiver.methodChannel` is a companion object `var` assigned from `MainActivity` with no synchronization. Low-risk but architecturally weak.

**18. `ambientProjectProvider` Returns Generic Map** — Should return a typed object instead of `Map<String, dynamic>?`.

**19. `projectByJobNoProvider` Uncached** — Refetches from DB on every access. Should cache or use `.family` with autoDispose.

**20. Path Traversal in Share Routing** — `capture_observer.dart` passes shared file paths to URL query params without validation. Low risk on a sideloaded app but still a hygiene issue.

**21. Watched Senders Parse Error is Silent** — `watched_senders_provider.dart` returns `[]` on JSON parse error with no log or warning.

**22. Stale Comment** — `dropbox_auth_provider.dart` says "Token exchange happens via deep link handler (to be implemented)" but it IS implemented in `app_links_handler.dart`. Comment is misleading.

---

### Recommended Priority Order

1. Calendar permissions (blocker — crashes now)
2. Claude API response parsing (crash on edge case)
3. Task editing (daily usability gap)
4. Auth state management (user confusion on first setup)
5. Time entry category reporting (data exists, value locked)
6. Expense list/edit screen (captured data is invisible)
7. Consultant management UI (accumulated data has no surface)
8. Fieldy pagination (works until you have many meetings)
9. Everything else

1: # Implementation Plan: Task F — Fieldy Meeting Capture Integration
2: 
3: This plan details the implementation of Task F, which integrates Tom's Fieldy wearable note-taker with Lakou. It enables Tom to review ambient conversations, dismiss irrelevant recordings, assign conversations to projects with attendee tagging (phone contacts + custom roles), pull transcript and summary data, save them as markdown files in Dropbox, and persist metadata in SQLite.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## User Review Required
8: 
9: > [!IMPORTANT]
10: > **1. API Key Authentication & Storage:**
11: > The Fieldy Public API requires an API key (`Bearer sk-fieldy-...`). We will add a **Connect Fieldy** view on the Fieldy Inbox page if the key is missing. This key will be saved securely in `flutter_secure_storage` under the key `fieldy.api_key`.
12: > 
13: > **2. Reusing the Consultants Table for Attendees:**
14: > We will reuse the existing `consultants` table to store attendee profiles. This avoids duplicating names/details and automatically builds a consolidated project contact directory. Tapping a participant adds them to `consultants` with custom roles (e.g. "GC Superintendent", "Owner Representative").
15: > 
16: > **3. Markdown File Format & Location:**
17: > The meetings will be saved as markdown files under:
18: > `<project_dropbox_path>/04 Meeting Agendas and Reports/`
19: > **Filename convention:** `YYYY-MMDD_jobno_Meeting_<safe_title>.md`
20: > **File Structure:**
21: > - Metadata header: date, duration, project, attendees with roles.
22: > - Summary &

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';

9: 
10: 
11: /// SQLite schema and accessor for Lakou.
12: ///
13: /// The live db file is intended to live inside the user's Dropbox folder
14: /// (`/Programs/Lakou/data/tpm.db`) so the desktop sees it automatically.
15: /// On Android the runtime file is opened from the app's documents dir and
16: /// synced up/down via [DropboxClient]; we never write to the Dropbox path
17: /// directly because Android sandboxing makes that unreliable.
18: class LakouDb {
19:   LakouDb._(this._db);
20:   final Database _db;
21:   Database get raw => _db;
22: 
23:   static Future<LakouDb> open() async {
24:     final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
25:     final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');
26:     final db = await openDatabase(
27:       path,
28:       version: 4,
29:       onCreate: (db, version) async {
30:         await _migrateV1(db);
31:         await _migrateV2(db);
32:         await _migrateV3(db);
33:         await _migrateV4(db);
34:       },
35:       onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {
36:         if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);
37:         if (oldV < 3) await _migrateV3(db);
38:         if (oldV < 4) await _migrateV4(db);
39:       },
40:     );
41:     return LakouDb._(db);
42:   }
43: 
44:   /// v4: Fieldy meeting capture integratio

259:         received_at INTEGER,

9: 
10: class DropboxAuthNotifier extends StateNotifier<AsyncValue<void>> {
11:   DropboxAuthNotifier(this.client) : super(const AsyncValue.data(null));
12: 
13:   final DropboxClient client;
14:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
15:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
16:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
17:       resetOnError: true,
18:     ),
19:   );
20:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
21: 
22:   Future<void> signIn() async {
23:     state = const AsyncValue.loading();
24:     try {
25:       final codeVerifier = _generateCodeVerifier();
26:       await _storage.write(key: _verifierKey, value: codeVerifier);
27: 
28:       final codeChallenge = _generateCodeChallenge(codeVerifier);
29: 
30:       final url = Uri.https('www.dropbox.com', '/oauth2/authorize', {
31:         'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
32:         'response_type': 'code',
33:         'code_challenge': codeChallenge,
34:         'code_challenge_method': 'S256',
35:         'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
36:         'token_access_type': 'offline',
37:       });
38: 
39:       if (await canLaunchUrl(url)) {
40:         await launchUrl(url, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);
41:       } else {
42:         throw Exception('Could not launch Dropbox auth URL');
43:       }

53:     final random = Random.secure();

58:   String _generateCodeChallenge(String verifier) {

61:     return base64UrlEncode(digest.bytes).replaceAll('=', '');
62:   }
