10: final appLinksHandlerProvider = Provider((ref) => AppLinksHandler(ref));
11: 
12: class AppLinksHandler {
13:   AppLinksHandler(this.ref);
14:   final Ref ref;
15:   late final _appLinks = AppLinks();
16:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
17:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
18:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
19:       resetOnError: true,
20:     ),
21:   );
22:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
23: 
24:   void init() {
25:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Initializing...');
26:     
27:     // Handle deep links when the app is already running
28:     _appLinks.uriLinkStream.listen((uri) {
29:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Stream URI: $uri');
30:       _processUri(uri);
31:     });
32: 
33:     // Handle deep link that launched the app
34:     _appLinks.getInitialLink().then((uri) {
35:       if (uri != null) {
36:         debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Initial URI: $uri');
37:         _processUri(uri);
38:       }
39:     });
40:   }
41: 
42:   void _processUri(Uri uri) {
43:     if (uri.scheme == 'lakou' && uri.host == 'oauth') {
44:       final code = uri.queryParameters['code'];
45:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code found: ${code != null}');
46:       if (code != null) {
47:         _handleDropboxCode(code);
48:       }
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> _handleDropboxCode(String code) async {
53:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Exchanging code for token...');
54:     try {
55:       final codeVerifier = await _storage.read(key: _verifierKey);
56:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code Verifier found: ${codeVerifier != null}');
57:       final dio = Dio();
58:       final res = await dio.post(
59:         'https://api.dropbox.com/oauth2/token',
60:         options: Options(contentType: Headers.formUrlEncodedContentType),
61:         data: {
62:           'code': code,
63:           'grant_type': 'authorization_code',
64:           'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
65:           'client_secret': Secrets.dropboxAppSecret,
66:           'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
67:           'code_verifier': codeVerifier,
68:         },
69:       );
70: 
71:       final refreshToken = res.data['refresh_token'] as String;
72:       final client = ref.read(dropboxClientProvider);
73:       await client.saveRefreshToken(refreshToken);
74:       
75:       // Invalidate the auth state so the UI updates
76:       ref.invalidate(dropboxAuthStateProvider);
77:       
78:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Success: Access Token Acquired');
79:     } on DioException catch (e) {
80:       final responseData = e.response?.data;
81:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error (DioException): ${e.message}\nResponse: $responseData');
82:     } catch (e) {
83:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error: $e');
84:     }
85:   }
86: }

24: class TaskRepo {
25:   TaskRepo(this._db);
26:   final LakouDb _db;
27: 
28:   Future<int> create({required String title, int? projectId, DateTime? due, String? plannedDate}) {
29:     return _db.raw.insert('tasks', {
30:       'project_id': projectId,
31:       'title': title,
32:       'due_date': due?.millisecondsSinceEpoch,
33:       'completed': 0,
34:       'created_at': DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch,
35:       'planned_date': plannedDate,
36:     });
37:   }
38: 
39:   Future<void> setCompleted(int id, bool done) async {
40:     await _db.raw.update(
41:       'tasks',
42:       {
43:         'completed': done ? 1 : 0,
44:         'completed_at': done ? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch : null,
45:       },
46:       where: 'id = ?',
47:       whereArgs: [id],
48:     );
49:   }
50: 
51:   Future<void> planTask(int id, String? dateStr) async {
52:     await _db.raw.update(
53:       'tasks',
54:       {
55:         'planned_date': dateStr,
56:       },
57:       where: 'id = ?',
58:       whereArgs: [id],
59:     );
60:   }
61: 
62:   Future<void> delete(int id) async {
63:     await _db.raw.delete('tasks', where: 'id = ?', whereArgs: [id]);
64:   }
65: }

77:     FROM tasks t

11: /// Billing — flat-fee + milestones, fee burn ratio, reimbursables.
12: ///
13: /// Pulls real data from the local DB rather than the prior wireframe:
14: ///  - "Worth a look" lists projects whose burn_ratio > 1.0 (already provided

16: ///  - "Reimbursables" lists unbilled expenses with reimbursable=1.
17: final _reimbursablesProvider =
18:     FutureProvider<List<Map<String, dynamic>>>((ref) async {
19:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);

21:     SELECT e.id, e.amount, e.vendor, e.spent_at, e.project_id,
22:            p.job_no, p.name AS project_name
23:     FROM expenses e
24:     LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = e.project_id
25:     WHERE e.reimbursable = 1 AND e.billed_back = 0
26:     ORDER BY e.spent_at DESC
27:   ''');
28: });

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: /// Builds canonical filenames per the firm's standard.
2: /// See `docs/filename-spec.md` for the source of truth.
3: class FilenameBuilder {
4:   /// Strips characters illegal in NTFS/FAT filenames but keeps spaces, commas,
5:   /// and hyphens (Tom's existing folder names depend on those).
6:   static String _safe(String s) =>
7:       s.replaceAll(RegExp(r'[\\/:*?"<>|]'), '').trim();
8: 
9:   static String _yyyymmdd(DateTime d) {
10:     final y = d.year.toString().padLeft(4, '0');
11:     final m = d.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
12:     final dd = d.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
13:     return '$y-$m$dd';
14:   }
15: 
16:   /// Helper for folder listing prefix
17:   static String yyyymmddForPrefix(DateTime d) => _yyyymmdd(d);
18: 
19:   /// Returns the SMS capture filename, e.g.
20:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`.
21:   ///
22:   /// [projectName] is whatever short label the user wants between job number and
23:   /// the suffix — typically the project's display name without the job number.
24:   /// [iteration] is 1-based; pass the next available NN after listing the folder.
25:   static String textCapture({
26:     required DateTime captureDate,
27:     required String jobNo,
28:     required String projectName,
29:     required int iteration,
30:   }) {
31:     final n = iteration.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
32:     return '${_yyyymmdd(captureDate)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_${_safe(projectName)}_Text-Projectplan-$n.txt';
33:   }
34: 
35:   /// Returns the receipt filename, e.g.
36:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg`.
37:   static String expenseReceipt({
38:     required DateTime spentAt,
39:     required String jobNo,
40:     required String vendor,
41:     required double amount,
42:     String extension = 'jpg',
43:   }) {
44:     final amt = amount.toStringAsFixed(2);
45:     final v = _safe(vendor).replaceAll(' ', '');
46:     return '${_yyyymmdd(spentAt)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_Expense_${v}_$amt.$extension';
47:   }

151:   String _extractBody(MessagePart? part) {
152:     if (part == null) return '';
153:     
154:     if (part.mimeType == 'text/plain' && part.body?.data != null) {
155:       final data = part.body!.data!;
156:       final normalized = data.replaceAll('-', '+').replaceAll('_', '/');
157:       try {
158:         final decoded = utf8.decode(base64Url.decode(normalized));
159:         return decoded;
160:       } catch (_) {
161:         try {
162:           final decoded = utf8.decode(base64.decode(normalized));
163:           return decoded;
164:         } catch (_) {}
165:       }
166:     }
167: 
168:     if (part.parts != null) {
169:       final buffer = StringBuffer();
170:       for (final p in part.parts!) {
171:         final content = _extractBody(p);
172:         if (content.isNotEmpty) {
173:           buffer.writeln(content);
174:         }
175:       }
176:       return buffer.toString();
177:     }
178: 
179:     return '';
180:   }
181: }

1: import 'package:flutter/services.dart';
2: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
3: import '../../core/trace.dart';
4: import '../settings/watched_senders_provider.dart';
5: import 'capture_provider.dart';
6: 
7: class SmsListener {
8:   static const _channel = MethodChannel('lakou/sms');
9:   final Ref ref;
10: 
11:   SmsListener(this.ref);
12: 
13:   void init() {
14:     Trace.log('sms', 'listener init');
15:     // Ask the host for SMS + contacts permissions on first launch.
16:     // No-op if already granted.
17:     _channel.invokeMethod('requestPermissions').catchError((e) {
18:       Trace.log('sms', 'requestPermissions error', data: e);
19:       return null;
20:     });
21: 
22:     _channel.setMethodCallHandler((call) async {
23:       Trace.log('sms', 'channel call ${call.method}');
24:       if (call.method == 'onSmsReceived') {
25:         final Map<dynamic, dynamic> args = call.arguments;
26:         final String sender = args['sender'] ?? 'Unknown';
27:         final String? displayName = args['displayName'] as String?;
28:         final String body = args['body'] ?? '';
29:         final int timestamp = args['timestamp'] ?? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch;
30: 
31:         Trace.log('sms', 'onSmsReceived',
32:             data: 'from=${displayName ?? sender} len=${body.length}');
33: 
34:         // Watched-senders gate. Empty list = capture all (default during
35:         // shake-out). Once Tom curates a list, only matching senders flow
36:         // into the capture screen — others stay logged in the inbox.
37:         final watched = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
38:         if (!senderMatches(watched: watched, sender: sender, displayName: displayName)) {
39:           Trace.log('sms', 'filtered (not watched)', data: displayName ?? sender);
40:           return;
41:         }
42: 
43:         ref.read(incomingCaptureProvider.notifier).state = IncomingCapture(
44:           rawText: body,
45:           sender: sender,
46:           displayName: displayName,
47:           capturedAt: DateTime.fromMillisecondsSinceEpoch(timestamp),
48:         );
49:       }
50:     });
51:   }
52: }
53: 
54: final smsListenerProvider = Provider((ref) => SmsListener(ref));
55:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: import 'dart:convert';
2: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
3: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
4: import '../../core/db.dart';
5: 
6: 
7: 
8: /// Watched senders. Stored in `settings` table at key `watched_senders` as a
9: /// JSON list of strings. Each entry is matched case-insensitively against the
10: /// resolved contact name AND the raw sender number for an incoming SMS.
11: ///
12: /// Empty list = no filter; every SMS is captured (the current default during
13: /// shake-out). Once Tom has a curated list, only matching senders trigger the
14: /// capture flow.
15: const _key = 'watched_senders';
16: 
17: final watchedSendersProvider = FutureProvider<List<String>>((ref) async {
18:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
19:   final rows = await db.raw.query('settings', where: 'key = ?', whereArgs: [_key], limit: 1);
20:   if (rows.isEmpty) return const [];
21:   final raw = rows.first['value'] as String?;
22:   if (raw == null || raw.isEmpty) return const [];
23:   try {
24:     return (jsonDecode(raw) as List).cast<String>();
25:   } catch (_) {
26:     return const [];
27:   }
28: });
29: 
30: class WatchedSendersActions {
31:   WatchedSendersActions(this.ref);
32:   final Ref ref;
33: 
34:   Future<void> _write(List<String> list) async {
35:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
36:     await db.raw.insert(
37:       'settings',
38:       {'key': _key, 'value': jsonEncode(list)},
39:       conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace,
40:     );
41:     ref.invalidate(watchedSendersProvider);
42:   }
43: 
44:   Future<void> add(String value) async {
45:     final v = value.trim();
46:     if (v.isEmpty) return;
47:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
48:     if (current.any((e) => e.toLowerCase() == v.toLowerCase())) return;
49:     await _write([...current, v]);
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> remove(String value) async {
53:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
54:     await _write(current.where((e) => e != value).toList());
55:   }
56: }
57: 
58: final watchedSendersActionsProvider =
59:     Provider((ref) => WatchedSendersActions(ref));
60: 
61: /// Synchronous filter helper. The receiver isolate can't await Riverpod, but
62: /// the SmsListener (in the main isolate) can read this once and call.
63: bool senderMatches({
64:   required List<String> watched,
65:   required String sender,
66:   String? displayName,
67: }) {
68:   if (watched.isEmpty) return true; // empty filter = capture all
69:   final s = sender.toLowerCase();
70:   final d = displayName?.toLowerCase();
71:   for (final w in watched) {
72:     final wl = w.toLowerCase();
73:     if (s.contains(wl)) return true;
74:     if (d != null && d.contains(wl)) return true;
75:   }
76:   return false;
77: }
78:

201:   String _formatDuration(DateTime start, DateTime end) {
202:     final diff = end.difference(start);
203:     final h = diff.inHours;
204:     final m = diff.inMinutes % 60;
205:     if (h == 0) return '${m}m';
206:     if (m == 0) return '${h}h';
207:     return '${h}h ${m}m';
208:   }
209: 
210:   String _formatDate(DateTime dt) {
211:     final local = dt.toLocal();
212:     final y = local.year.toString();
213:     final m = local.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
214:     final d = local.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
215:     final hr = local.hour.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
216:     final min = local.minute.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
217:     return '$y-$m-$d $hr:$min';
218:   }

28:     super.initState();
29:     _initSharingListener();

33:   void dispose() {
34:     _intentSub?.cancel();

38:   void _initSharingListener() {
39:     // Listen to shared media files (images, pdfs, etc.)
40:     _intentSub = ReceiveSharingIntent.instance.getMediaStream().listen((value) {
41:       _handleSharedMedia(value);
42:     }, onError: (err) {
43:       Trace.log('sharing', 'getMediaStream error', data: err);
44:     });
45: 
46:     // Handle initial shared media files (when app is launched via sharing)
47:     ReceiveSharingIntent.instance.getInitialMedia().then((value) {
48:       _handleSharedMedia(value);
49:     }).catchError((err) {
50:       Trace.log('sharing', 'getInitialMedia error', data: err);
51:     });
52:   }
53: 
54:   void _handleSharedMedia(List<SharedMediaFile> media) {
55:     if (media.isEmpty) return;
56:     
57:     final path = media.first.path;
58:     Trace.log('sharing', 'received shared media', data: path);
59:     
60:     // Clear shared media after handling it to prevent duplicate triggers
61:     ReceiveSharingIntent.instance.reset();
62: 
63:     final navContext = widget.navigatorKey.currentContext;
64:     if (navContext != null) {
65:       WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback((_) {
66:         navContext.push('/share-routing?path=${Uri.encodeComponent(path)}');
67:       });
68:     }
69:   }
70:

72:   Widget build(BuildContext context) {
73:     ref.listen(incomingCaptureProvider, (previous, next) {
74:       Trace.log('capture',
75:           'incomingCaptureProvider ${previous == null ? "null" : "set"} -> ${next == null ? "null" : "set"}',
76:           data: next?.senderLabel);
77:       if (next != null) {
78:         final navContext = widget.navigatorKey.currentContext;
79:         if (navContext != null) {
80:           Trace.log('capture', 'observer push /capture');
81:           navContext.push('/capture');
82:         } else {
83:           Trace.log('capture', 'observer skipped: no nav context');
84:         }
85:       }
86:     });

1: package com.greenstories.lakou
2: 
3: import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
4: import android.content.Context
5: import android.content.Intent
6: import android.provider.Telephony
7: import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodChannel
8: 
9: class SmsReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
10:     companion object {
11:         var methodChannel: MethodChannel? = null
12:     }
13: 
14:     override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
15:         if (intent.action != Telephony.Sms.Intents.SMS_RECEIVED_ACTION) return
16: 
17:         // Multi-part SMS arrive as multiple PDUs from the same sender; concatenate
18:         // their bodies so the Dart side sees one capture instead of N fragments.
19:         val messages = Telephony.Sms.Intents.getMessagesFromIntent(intent) ?: return
20:         if (messages.isEmpty()) return
21: 
22:         val sender = messages.first().displayOriginatingAddress
23:         val body = messages.joinToString(separator = "") { it.displayMessageBody ?: "" }
24:         // Use the message's own timestamp (UTC ms), not wall-clock now — broadcasts
25:         // can be delivered late after a doze wake-up.
26:         val timestamp = messages.first().timestampMillis
27:         val displayName = ContactResolver.displayNameFor(context, sender)
28: 
29:         methodChannel?.invokeMethod(
30:             "onSmsReceived",
31:             mapOf(
32:                 "sender" to sender,
33:                 "displayName" to displayName,
34:                 "body" to body,
35:                 "timestamp" to timestamp,
36:                 "type" to "sms"
37:             )
38:         )
39:     }
40: }

## Antigravity Prompt: Lakou Codebase Gap Report

Here's what the full audit found — organized by severity. The app is remarkably complete (no stubs, no TODOs, no placeholder screens), but there are real gaps in three categories: **things that will crash**, **things that are half-wired**, and **things the app claims to do but doesn't actually do yet**.

---

### WILL CRASH (Fix Before Next Build)

**1. Missing Calendar Permissions — BLOCKER**
- `CalendarHelper.kt` calls `READ_CALENDAR` and `WRITE_CALENDAR` operations but **neither permission is declared in AndroidManifest.xml**. The planner screen's calendar integration (`getTodayEvents()`, `insertEvent()`) will throw `SecurityException` on every device.
- Fix: Add both permissions to manifest + add runtime permission checks in `CalendarHelper.kt` and `MainActivity.kt` before calling calendar methods.

**2. Claude API Response Parsing is Fragile**
- `claude_extractor.dart` assumes `response['content'][0]['text']` exists. If Claude returns a tool_use block, an error response, or an empty content array, this crashes. Needs defensive parsing.

**3. Meetings Table Schema Assumption**
- `today_provider.dart` queries the `meetings` table without checking if the V4 migration has run. On a dev device with an old database, this SQL query crashes. Needs a migration-version guard or a try-catch.

---

### HALF-WIRED (Features That Exist But Don't Fully Work)

**4. Auth State Management Gap**
- `dropbox_auth_provider.dart`: `signIn()` sets state to `.data(null)` immediately after launching the browser URL. The UI has no way to know the token exchange is still pending. Should stay `.loading()` until the de
<truncated 3214 bytes>
mats: `04/06/2026`, `2026-04-06`, `April 6`, `next Tuesday`. When offline, date extraction is nearly useless.

**16. Quests/Gamification is Retired But Tables Remain**
- `quests_screen.dart` redirects to `/today`. The `quests` and `xp_log` tables still exist in the schema but nothing writes to them. Either remove the tables in a V5 migration or rebuild the gamification feature.

---

### CODE QUALITY (Not Blocking But Worth Knowing)

**17. Static Mutable MethodChannel** — `SmsReceiver.methodChannel` is a companion object `var` assigned from `MainActivity` with no synchronization. Low-risk but architecturally weak.

**18. `ambientProjectProvider` Returns Generic Map** — Should return a typed object instead of `Map<String, dynamic>?`.

**19. `projectByJobNoProvider` Uncached** — Refetches from DB on every access. Should cache or use `.family` with autoDispose.

**20. Path Traversal in Share Routing** — `capture_observer.dart` passes shared file paths to URL query params without validation. Low risk on a sideloaded app but still a hygiene issue.

**21. Watched Senders Parse Error is Silent** — `watched_senders_provider.dart` returns `[]` on JSON parse error with no log or warning.

**22. Stale Comment** — `dropbox_auth_provider.dart` says "Token exchange happens via deep link handler (to be implemented)" but it IS implemented in `app_links_handler.dart`. Comment is misleading.

---

### Recommended Priority Order

1. Calendar permissions (blocker — crashes now)
2. Claude API response parsing (crash on edge case)
3. Task editing (daily usability gap)
4. Auth state management (user confusion on first setup)
5. Time entry category reporting (data exists, value locked)
6. Expense list/edit screen (captured data is invisible)
7. Consultant management UI (accumulated data has no surface)
8. Fieldy pagination (works until you have many meetings)
9. Everything else

1: # Implementation Plan: Task F — Fieldy Meeting Capture Integration
2: 
3: This plan details the implementation of Task F, which integrates Tom's Fieldy wearable note-taker with Lakou. It enables Tom to review ambient conversations, dismiss irrelevant recordings, assign conversations to projects with attendee tagging (phone contacts + custom roles), pull transcript and summary data, save them as markdown files in Dropbox, and persist metadata in SQLite.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## User Review Required
8: 
9: > [!IMPORTANT]
10: > **1. API Key Authentication & Storage:**
11: > The Fieldy Public API requires an API key (`Bearer sk-fieldy-...`). We will add a **Connect Fieldy** view on the Fieldy Inbox page if the key is missing. This key will be saved securely in `flutter_secure_storage` under the key `fieldy.api_key`.
12: > 
13: > **2. Reusing the Consultants Table for Attendees:**
14: > We will reuse the existing `consultants` table to store attendee profiles. This avoids duplicating names/details and automatically builds a consolidated project contact directory. Tapping a participant adds them to `consultants` with custom roles (e.g. "GC Superintendent", "Owner Representative").
15: > 
16: > **3. Markdown File Format & Location:**
17: > The meetings will be saved as markdown files under:
18: > `<project_dropbox_path>/04 Meeting Agendas and Reports/`
19: > **Filename convention:** `YYYY-MMDD_jobno_Meeting_<safe_title>.md`
20: > **File Structure:**
21: > - Metadata header: date, duration, project, attendees with roles.
22: > - Summary & 
<truncated 3698 bytes>
s/counts.
90: - Display the last meeting date in the **Project Glance** card's "LAST ACTIVE" label.
91: - Add a meetings list section showing all assigned meetings with click-to-open Dropbox viewer integration.
92: 
93: #### [MODIFY] [today_provider.dart](file:///C:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/today/today_provider.dart)
94: - Query meetings created today and include them in the timeline ledger list with the icon `Icons.record_voice_over` (Code: `MTG`).
95: 
96: ---
97: 
98: ## Verification Plan
99: 
100: ### Automated Tests
101: - Run `flutter analyze` to verify no warnings or errors.
102: - Run `flutter test` to ensure all tests execute successfully.
103: 
104: ### Manual Verification
105: 1. **Connect Fieldy:** Navigate to the Fieldy Inbox, enter a mock/real API Key, and save. Verify it is persisted.
106: 2. **List & Suggest:** View the list of conversations. Verify that dates and durations are formatted properly, and projects are suggested when a job number (e.g. `23AC-08`) is found.
107: 3. **Dismiss Triage:** Tap dismiss on a conversation. Verify it disappears from the list and doesn't reappear on refresh.
108: 4. **Assign & Tag Attendees:** Tap assign. Choose a project, select a phone contact, and add a custom person (e.g., "GC Superintendent"). Save.
109: 5. **Dropbox & File Naming Verification:** Navigate to the project's Dropbox folder under `04 Meeting Agendas and Reports/`. Confirm the file exists with the name `YYYY-MMDD_jobno_Meeting_<title>.md` and contains the metadata header, summary, and raw transcript at the end.
110: 6. **Glance & Timeline Updates:** Verify the meeting is listed on the project detail page, updates the "LAST ACTIVE" glance field, and appears on the Today screen timeline.
111: 7. **Offline Mode:** Turn off network connectivity. Confirm that previously saved meetings can be listed/viewed fully offline, and the inbox fails gracefully with a retry prompt.

(final line in lines) {
147:       final lower = line.toLowerCase();

150:         actionItems.add(line.replaceFirst(RegExp(r'^[-*\s]+'), '').trim());

153:       // Basic date keyword scanner

158:       }
159:     }
160:     
161:     // If no explicit action items found, treat lines after summary as notes/actions
162:     if (actionItems.isEmpty && lines.length > 1) {

168:       actionItems.add(summary);

9: 
10: 
11: /// SQLite schema and accessor for Lakou.
12: ///
13: /// The live db file is intended to live inside the user's Dropbox folder
14: /// (`/Programs/Lakou/data/tpm.db`) so the desktop sees it automatically.
15: /// On Android the runtime file is opened from the app's documents dir and
16: /// synced up/down via [DropboxClient]; we never write to the Dropbox path
17: /// directly because Android sandboxing makes that unreliable.
18: class LakouDb {
19:   LakouDb._(this._db);
20:   final Database _db;
21:   Database get raw => _db;
22: 
23:   static Future<LakouDb> open() async {
24:     final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
25:     final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');
26:     final db = await openDatabase(
27:       path,
28:       version: 4,
29:       onCreate: (db, version) async {
30:         await _migrateV1(db);
31:         await _migrateV2(db);
32:         await _migrateV3(db);
33:         await _migrateV4(db);
34:       },
35:       onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {
36:         if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);
37:         if (oldV < 3) await _migrateV3(db);
38:         if (oldV < 4) await _migrateV4(db);
39:       },
40:     );
41:     return LakouDb._(db);
42:   }
43: 
44:   /// v4: Fieldy meeting capture integratio

2: import 'dart:math';
3: import 'package:crypto/crypto.dart';
4: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
5: import 'package:flutter_secure_storage/flutter_secure_storage.dart';
6: import 'package:url_launcher/url_launcher.dart';
7: import '../../core/secrets.dart';
8: import '../../core/dropbox_client.dart';
9: 
10: class DropboxAuthNotifier extends StateNotifier<AsyncValue<void>> {
11:   DropboxAuthNotifier(this.client) : super(const AsyncValue.data(null));
12: 
13:   final DropboxClient client;
14:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
15:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
16:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
17:       resetOnError: true,
18:     ),
19:   );
20:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
21: 
22:   Future<void> signIn() async {
23:     state = const AsyncValue.loading();
24:     try {
25:       final codeVerifier = _generateCodeVerifier();
26:       await _storage.write(key: _verifierKey, value: codeVerifier);
27: 
28:       final codeChallenge = _generateCodeChallenge(codeVerifier);
29: 
30:       final url = Uri.https('www.dropbox.com', '/oauth2/authorize', {
31:         'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
32:         'response_type': 'code',
33:         'code_challenge': codeChallenge,
34:         'code_challenge_method': 'S256',
35:         'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
36:         'token_access_type': 'offline',
37:       });
38: 
39:       if (await canLaunchUrl(url)) {
40:         await launchUrl(url, mode: LaunchMode.externalApplication);
41:       } else {
42:         throw Exception('Could not launch Dropbox auth URL');
43:       }
44:       
45:       // Note: Token exchange happens via deep link handler (to be implemented)
46:       state = const AsyncValue.data(null);
47:     } catch (e, st) {
48:       state = AsyncValue.error(e, st);
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   String _generateCodeVerifier() {
53:     final random = Random.secure();
54:     final values = List<int>.generate(32, (i) => random.nextInt(256));
55:     return base64UrlEncode(values).replaceAll('=', '');
56:   }
57: 
58:   String _generateCodeChallenge(String verifier) {
59:     final bytes = utf8.encode(verifier);
60:     final digest = sha256.convert(bytes);
61:     return base64UrlEncode(digest.bytes).replaceAll('=', '');
62:   }
63: }
64: 
65: final dropboxAuthProvider = StateNotifierProvider<DropboxAuthNotifier, AsyncValue<void>>((ref) {
66:   return DropboxAuthNotifier(ref.watch(dropboxClientProvider));
67: });
68: 
69: final dropboxAuthStateProvider = FutureProvider<bool>((ref) async {
70:   final client = ref.watch(dropboxClientProvider);
71:   return client.hasRefreshToken();
72: });

1: import 'package:flutter/foundation.dart';
2: import 'package:app_links/app_links.dart';
3: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';

5: import 'package:dio/dio.dart';
6: import '../../core/secrets.dart';
7: import '../../core/dropbox_client.dart';
8: import 'dropbox_auth_provider.dart';
9: 
10: final appLinksHandlerProvider = Provider((ref) => AppLinksHandler(ref));
11: 
12: class AppLinksHandler {
13:   AppLinksHandler(this.ref);
14:   final Ref ref;
15:   late final _appLinks = AppLinks();
16:   static const _storage = FlutterSecureStorage(
17:     aOptions: AndroidOptions(
18:       encryptedSharedPreferences: true,
19:       resetOnError: true,
20:     ),
21:   );
22:   static const _verifierKey = 'dropbox.code_verifier';
23: 
24:   void init() {
25:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Initializing...');
26:     
27:     // Handle deep links when the app is already running
28:     _appLinks.uriLinkStream.listen((uri) {
29:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Stream URI: $uri');
30:       _processUri(uri);
31:     });
32: 
33:     // Handle deep link that launched the app
34:     _appLinks.getInitialLink().then((uri) {
35:       if (uri != null) {
36:         debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Received Initial URI: $uri');
37:         _processUri(uri);
38:       }
39:     });
40:   }
41: 
42:   void _processUri(Uri uri) {
43:     if (uri.scheme == 'lakou' && uri.host == 'oauth') {
44:       final code = uri.queryParameters['code'];
45:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code found: ${code != null}');
46:       if (code != null) {
47:         _handleDropboxCode(code);
48:       }
49:     }
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> _handleDropboxCode(String code) async {
53:     debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Exchanging code for token...');
54:     try {
55:       final codeVerifier = await _storage.read(key: _verifierKey);
56:       debugPrint('AppLinksHandler: Code Verifier found: ${codeVerifier != null}');
57:       final dio = Dio();
58:       final res = await dio.post(
59:         'https://api.dropbox.com/oauth2/token',
60:         options: Options(contentType: Headers.formUrlEncodedContentType),
61:         data: {
62:           'code': code,
63:           'grant_type': 'authorization_code',
64:           'client_id': Secrets.dropboxAppKey,
65:           'client_secret': Secrets.dropboxAppSecret,
66:           'redirect_uri': 'lakou://oauth',
67:           'code_verifier': codeVerifier,
68:         },
69:       );
70: 
71:       final refreshToken = res.data['refresh_token'] as String;
72:       final client = ref.read(dropboxClientProvider);
73:       await client.saveRefreshToken(refreshToken);
74:       
75:       // Invalidate the auth state so the UI updates
76:       ref.invalidate(dropboxAuthStateProvider);
77:       
78:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Success: Access Token Acquired');
79:     } on DioException catch (e) {
80:       final responseData = e.response?.data;
81:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error (DioException): ${e.message}\nResponse: $responseData');
82:     } catch (e) {
83:       debugPrint('Dropbox Auth Error: $e');
84:     }
85:   }
86: }

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import '../../core/db.dart';
3: 
4: 
5: class TaskRow {
6:   TaskRow({
7:     required this.id,
8:     required this.title,
9:     required this.completed,
10:     this.projectId,
11:     this.projectLabel,
12:     this.dueDate,
13:     this.plannedDate,
14:   });
15:   final int id;
16:   final String title;
17:   final bool completed;
18:   final int? projectId;
19:   final String? projectLabel;
20:   final DateTime? dueDate;
21:   final String? plannedDate; // Format: YYYY-MM-DD
22: }
23: 
24: class TaskRepo {
25:   TaskRepo(this._db);
26:   final LakouDb _db;
27: 
28:   Future<int> create({required String title, int? projectId, DateTime? due, String? plannedDate}) {
29:     return _db.raw.insert('tasks', {
30:       'project_id': projectId,
31:       'title': title,
32:       'due_date': due?.millisecondsSinceEpoch,
33:       'completed': 0,
34:       'created_at': DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch,
35:       'planned_date': plannedDate,
36:     });
37:   }
38: 
39:   Future<void> setCompleted(int id, bool done) async {
40:     await _db.raw.update(
41:       'tasks',
42:       {
43:         'completed': done ? 1 : 0,
44:         'completed_at': done ? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch : null,
45:       },
46:       where: 'id = ?',
47:       whereArgs: [id],
48:     );
49:   }
50: 
51:   Future<void> planTask(int id, String? dateStr) async {
52:     await _db.raw.update(
53:       'tasks',
54:       {
55:         'planned_date': dateStr,
56:       },
57:       where: 'id = ?',
58:       whereArgs: [id],
59:     );
60:   }
61: 
62:   Future<void> delete(int id) async {
63:     await _db.raw.delete('tasks', where: 'id = ?', whereArgs: [id]);
64:   }
65: }
66: 
67: final taskRepoProvider = FutureProvider<TaskRepo>((ref) async {
68:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
69:   return TaskRepo(db);
70: });
71: 
72: final tasksProvider = FutureProvider<List<TaskRow>>((ref) async {
73:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
74:   final rows = await db.raw.rawQuery('''
75:     SELECT t.id, t.title, t.completed, t.due_date, t.project_id, t.planned_date,
76:            p.job_no, p.name AS project_name
77:     FROM tasks t
78:     LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = t.project_id
79:     ORDER BY t.completed ASC, COALESCE(t.due_date, t.created_at) ASC
80:   ''');
81:   return rows.map((r) {
82:     final job = r['job_no'] as String?;
83:     final pname = r['project_name'] as String?;
84:     return TaskRow(
85:       id: r['id'] as int,
86:       title: r['title'] as String,
87:       completed: (r['completed'] as int) == 1,
88:       projectId: r['project_id'] as int?,
89:       projectLabel: (job == null && pname == null) ? null : '[$job] $pname',
90:       dueDate: r['due_date'] == null ? null : DateTime.fromMillisecondsSinceEpoch(r['due_date'] as int),
91:       plannedDate: r['planned_date'] as String?,
92:     );
93:   }).toList();
94: });

11: /// Billing — flat-fee + milestones, fee burn ratio, reimbursables.
12: ///
13: /// Pulls real data from the local DB rather than the prior wireframe:
14: ///  - "Worth a look" lists projects whose burn_ratio > 1.0 (already provided
15: ///    by overFeeProjectsProvider; clicking a row jumps to project detail).
16: ///  - "Reimbursables" lists unbilled expenses with reimbursable=1.
17: final _reimbursablesProvider =
18:     FutureProvider<List<Map<String, dynamic>>>((ref) async {
19:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
20:   return db.raw.rawQuery('''
21:     SELECT e.id, e.amount, e.vendor, e.spent_at, e.project_id,
22:            p.job_no, p.name AS project_name
23:     FROM expenses e
24:     LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.id = e.project_id
25:     WHERE e.reimbursable = 1 AND e.billed_back = 0
26:     ORDER BY e.spent_at DESC
27:   ''');
28: });

34:   Widget build(BuildContext context, WidgetRef ref) {
35:     final overFee = ref.watch(overFeeProjectsProvider);
36:     final reimbursables = ref.watch(_reimbursablesProvider);

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: /// Builds canonical filenames per the firm's standard.
2: /// See `docs/filename-spec.md` for the source of truth.
3: class FilenameBuilder {
4:   /// Strips characters illegal in NTFS/FAT filenames but keeps spaces, commas,
5:   /// and hyphens (Tom's existing folder names depend on those).
6:   static String _safe(String s) =>
7:       s.replaceAll(RegExp(r'[\\/:*?"<>|]'), '').trim();
8: 
9:   static String _yyyymmdd(DateTime d) {
10:     final y = d.year.toString().padLeft(4, '0');
11:     final m = d.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
12:     final dd = d.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
13:     return '$y-$m$dd';
14:   }
15: 
16:   /// Helper for folder listing prefix
17:   static String yyyymmddForPrefix(DateTime d) => _yyyymmdd(d);
18: 
19:   /// Returns the SMS capture filename, e.g.
20:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_521 Springfield Ave, Newark_Text-Projectplan-01.txt`.
21:   ///
22:   /// [projectName] is whatever short label the user wants between job number and
23:   /// the suffix — typically the project's display name without the job number.
24:   /// [iteration] is 1-based; pass the next available NN after listing the folder.
25:   static String textCapture({
26:     required DateTime captureDate,
27:     required String jobNo,
28:     required String projectName,
29:     required int iteration,
30:   }) {
31:     final n = iteration.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
32:     return '${_yyyymmdd(captureDate)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_${_safe(projectName)}_Text-Projectplan-$n.txt';
33:   }
34: 
35:   /// Returns the receipt filename, e.g.
36:   /// `2026-0406_23AC-08_Expense_HomeDepot_47.83.jpg`.
37:   static String expenseReceipt({
38:     required DateTime spentAt,
39:     required String jobNo,
40:     required String vendor,
41:     required double amount,
42:     String extension = 'jpg',
43:   }) {
44:     final amt = amount.toStringAsFixed(2);
45:     final v = _safe(vendor).replaceAll(' ', '');
46:     return '${_yyyymmdd(spentAt)}_${_safe(jobNo)}_Expense_${v}_$amt.$extension';
47:   }
48: }
49:

9: /// Plain-English Dropbox error wrapper. Catch this in UI code and show
10: /// `e.message` directly — it's already human-readable.
11: class DropboxException implements Exception {
12:   DropboxException(this.message);

33: /// Minimal Dropbox v2 REST wrapper.
34: ///
35: /// Auth uses OAuth 2 PKCE; refresh token is stored in secure storage.
36: /// All paths are absolute Dropbox paths starting with `/` (e.g. `/Green Stories/2026/...`).
37: ///
38: /// Only the methods Lakou actually needs are implemented:
39: ///   - [upload] / [download] for files
40: ///   - [listFolder] for filename collision checking
41: ///   - [createFolder] / [copyFolder] for project scaffolding
42: class DropboxClient {
43:   DropboxClient({r

86:           'client_secret': appSecret,
87:         },
88:       );
89:       final token = res.data['access_token'] as String;
90:       final ttl = (res.data['expires_in'] as int? ?? 14400);
91:       await _storage.write(key: _accessKey, value: token);
92:       await _storage.write(
93:         key: _accessExpiresKey,
94:         value: DateTime.now().add(Duration(seconds: ttl)).millisecondsSinceEpoch.toString(),
95:       );

97:     } on DioException catch (e) {
98:       final body = e.response?.data;

100:       // invalid_grant = the refresh token is no longer valid (revoked, expired,
101:       // or the app's secret was rotated). Wipe it so the next sign-in starts clean.
102:       if (errCode == 'invalid_grant' || e.response?.statusCode == 400) {
103:         await _storage.delete(key: _refreshKey);
104:         await _storage.delete(key: _accessKey);

106:         throw DropboxException(

113:         );
114:       }
115:       throw DropboxException('Dropbox auth failed: ${errCode ?? e.message}');

130:   Future<void> upload(String path, Uint8List bytes) async {
131:     Trace.log('dropbox', 'upload start', data: '$path (${bytes.length}B)');

151:   String _extractBody(MessagePart? part) {
152:     if (part == null) return '';
153:     
154:     if (part.mimeType == 'text/plain' && part.body?.data != null) {
155:       final data = part.body!.data!;
156:       final normalized = data.replaceAll('-', '+').replaceAll('_', '/');
157:       try {
158:         final decoded = utf8.decode(base64Url.decode(normalized));
159:         return decoded;
160:       } catch (_) {
161:         try {
162:           final decoded = utf8.decode(base64.decode(normalized));
163:           return decoded;
164:         } catch (_) {}
165:       }
166:     }
167: 
168:     if (part.parts != null) {
169:       final buffer = StringBuffer();
170:       for (final p in part.parts!) {
171:         final content = _extractBody(p);
172:         if (content.isNotEmpty) {
173:           buffer.writeln(content);
174:         }
175:       }
176:       return buffer.toString();
177:     }
178: 
179:     return '';
180:   }
181: }
182:

1: import 'package:flutter/services.dart';
2: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
3: import '../../core/trace.dart';
4: import '../settings/watched_senders_provider.dart';
5: import 'capture_provider.dart';
6: 
7: class SmsListener {
8:   static const _channel = MethodChannel('lakou/sms');
9:   final Ref ref;
10: 
11:   SmsListener(this.ref);
12: 
13:   void init() {
14:     Trace.log('sms', 'listener init');
15:     // Ask the host for SMS + contacts permissions on first launch.
16:     // No-op if already granted.
17:     _channel.invokeMethod('requestPermissions').catchError((e) {
18:       Trace.log('sms', 'requestPermissions error', data: e);
19:       return null;
20:     });
21: 
22:     _channel.setMethodCallHandler((call) async {
23:       Trace.log('sms', 'channel call ${call.method}');
24:       if (call.method == 'onSmsReceived') {
25:         final Map<dynamic, dynamic> args = call.arguments;
26:         final String sender = args['sender'] ?? 'Unknown';
27:         final String? displayName = args['displayName'] as String?;
28:         final String body = args['body'] ?? '';
29:         final int timestamp = args['timestamp'] ?? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch;
30: 
31:         Trace.log('sms', 'onSmsReceived',
32:             data: 'from=${displayName ?? sender} len=${body.length}');
33: 
34:         // Watched-senders gate. Empty list = capture all (default during
35:         // shake-out). Once Tom curates a list, only matching senders flow
36:         // into the capture screen — others stay logged in the inbox.
37:         final watched = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
38:         if (!senderMatches(watched: watched, sender: sender, displayName: displayName)) {
39:           Trace.log('sms', 'filtered (not watched)', data: displayName ?? sender);
40:           return;
41:         }
42: 
43:         ref.read(incomingCaptureProvider.notifier).state = IncomingCapture(
44:           rawText: body,
45:           sender: sender,
46:           displayName: displayName,
47:           capturedAt: DateTime.fromMillisecondsSinceEpoch(timestamp),
48:         );
49:       }
50:     });
51:   }
52: }
53: 
54: final smsListenerProvider = Provider((ref) => SmsListener(ref));
55:

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: import 'dart:convert';
2: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
3: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
4: import '../../core/db.dart';
5: 
6: 
7: 
8: /// Watched senders. Stored in `settings` table at key `watched_senders` as a
9: /// JSON list of strings. Each entry is matched case-insensitively against the
10: /// resolved contact name AND the raw sender number for an incoming SMS.
11: ///
12: /// Empty list = no filter; every SMS is captured (the current default during
13: /// shake-out). Once Tom has a curated list, only matching senders trigger the
14: /// capture flow.
15: const _key = 'watched_senders';
16: 
17: final watchedSendersProvider = FutureProvider<List<String>>((ref) async {
18:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
19:   final rows = await db.raw.query('settings', where: 'key = ?', whereArgs: [_key], limit: 1);
20:   if (rows.isEmpty) return const [];
21:   final raw = rows.first['value'] as String?;
22:   if (raw == null || raw.isEmpty) return const [];
23:   try {
24:     return (jsonDecode(raw) as List).cast<String>();
25:   } catch (_) {
26:     return const [];
27:   }
28: });
29: 
30: class WatchedSendersActions {
31:   WatchedSendersActions(this.ref);
32:   final Ref ref;
33: 
34:   Future<void> _write(List<String> list) async {
35:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
36:     await db.raw.insert(
37:       'settings',
38:       {'key': _key, 'value': jsonEncode(list)},
39:       conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace,
40:     );
41:     ref.invalidate(watchedSendersProvider);
42:   }
43: 
44:   Future<void> add(String value) async {
45:     final v = value.trim();
46:     if (v.isEmpty) return;
47:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
48:     if (current.any((e) => e.toLowerCase() == v.toLowerCase())) return;
49:     await _write([...current, v]);
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> remove(String value) async {
53:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
54:     await _write(current.where((e) => e != value).toList());
55:   }
56: }
57: 
58: final watchedSendersActionsProvider =
59:     Provider((ref) => WatchedSendersActions(ref));
60: 
61: /// Synchronous filter helper. The receiver isolate can't await Riverpod, but
62: /// the SmsListener (in the main isolate) can read this once and call.
63: bool senderMatches({
64:   required List<String> watched,
65:   required String sender,
66:   String? displayName,
67: }) {
68:   if (watched.isEmpty) return true; // empty filter = capture all
69:   final s = sender.toLowerCase();
70:   final d = displayName?.toLowerCase();
71:   for (final w in watched) {
72:     final wl = w.toLowerCase();
73:     if (s.contains(wl)) return true;
74:     if (d != null && d.contains(wl)) return true;
75:   }
76:   return false;
77: }
78:

200: 
201:   String _formatDuration(DateTime start, DateTime end) {
202:     final diff = end.difference(start);
203:     final h = diff.inHours;
204:     final m = diff.inMinutes % 60;
205:     if (h == 0) return '${m}m';
206:     if (m == 0) return '${h}h';
207:     return '${h}h ${m}m';
208:   }
209: 
210:   String _formatDate(DateTime dt) {
211:     final local = dt.toLocal();
212:     final y = local.year.toString();
213:     final m = local.month.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
214:     final d = local.day.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
215:     final hr = local.hour.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
216:     final min = local.minute.toString().padLeft(2, '0');
217:     return '$y-$m-$d $hr:$min';
218:   }

39:     // Listen to shared media files (images, pdfs, etc.)

41:       _handleSharedMedia(value);

55:     if (media.isEmpty) return;
56:     
57:     final path = media.first.path;
58:     Trace.log('sharing', 'received shared media', data: path);
59:     
60:     // Clear shared media after handling it to prevent duplicate triggers
61:     ReceiveSharingIntent.instance.reset();
62:

67:       });
68:     }

1: package com.greenstories.lakou
2: 
3: import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
4: import android.content.Context
5: import android.content.Intent
6: import android.provider.Telephony
7: import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodChannel
8: 
9: class SmsReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
10:     companion object {
11:         var methodChannel: MethodChannel? = null
12:     }
13: 
14:     override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
15:         if (intent.action != Telephony.Sms.Intents.SMS_RECEIVED_ACTION) return
16: 
17:         // Multi-part SMS arrive as multiple PDUs from the same sender; concatenate
18:         // their bodies so the Dart side sees one capture instead of N fragments.
19:         val messages = Telephony.Sms.Intents.getMessagesFromIntent(intent) ?: return
20:         if (messages.isEmpty()) return
21: 
22:         val sender = messages.first().displayOriginatingAddress
23:         val body = messages.joinToString(separator = "") { it.displayMessageBody ?: "" }
24:         // Use the message's own timestamp (UTC ms), not wall-clock now — broadcasts
25:         // can be delivered late after a doze wake-up.
26:         val timestamp = messages.first().timestampMillis
27:         val displayName = ContactResolver.displayNameFor(context, sender)
28: 
29:         methodChannel?.invokeMethod(
30:             "onSmsReceived",
31:             mapOf(
32:                 "sender" to sender,
33:                 "displayName" to displayName,
34:                 "body" to body,
35:                 "timestamp" to timestamp,
36:                 "type" to "sms"
37:             )
38:         )
39:     }
40: }

5: /// The old Quests screen has been retired. The Today screen replaces it.
6: /// This shim exists only so existing `/quests` links don't 404 — it

8: class QuestsScreen extends StatelessWidget {
9:   const QuestsScreen({super.key});

  "CodeContent": "\"# Lakou Gap Resolutions Checklist\\n\\n- [ ] Crash Prevention & Permissions\\n  - [ ] Add calendar permissions to AndroidManifest.xml\\n  - [ ] Request calendar permissions at runtime in MainActivity.kt\\n  - [ ] Harden ClaudeExtractor JSON parsing and update local date parser\\n  - [ ] Wrap meetings queries in try-catch blocks in today provider and project details\\n- [ ] Authentication & Triage UI\\n  - [ ] Keep dropboxAuthProvider loading during redirect flow\\n  - [ ] Add loading timeout to dropboxAuthProvider\\n  - [ ] Propagate token exchange errors to UI instead of silent debugPrint\\n  - [ ] Implement auto-pagination loop for Fieldy conversations\\n  - [ ] Implement carrier retry deduplication in SMS listener\\n- [ ] Task, Expense, and Milestone Management\\n  - [ ] Implement task updates in tasks provider\\n  - [ ] Add task editing bottom sheet to tasks screen and planner screen\\n  - [ ] Create expenses provider and expenses list screen (view, edit, delete)\\n  - [ ] Add delete file operation to DropboxClient\\n  - [ ] Create milestones provider and milestones editing section\\n  - [ ] Update project details view with client info and edit details dialog\\n  - [ ] Connect milestone invoicing status to project fee burn card\\n- [ ] Intake, Reports & Settings\\n  - [ ] Auto-parse client info on intake form poll and update clients database\\n  - [ ] Add Weekly Category Summary report card to Timer screen\\n  - [ ] Create Settings view with routes to watched senders and new consultants directory\\n  - [ ] Create consultants provider and consultants management screen\\n- [ ] Code Quality & Security\\n  - [ ] Make SmsReceiver methodChannel volatile\\n  - [ ] Validate shared file paths to prevent path traversal in share routing\\n  - [ ] Log watched senders parse errors to Trace instead of swallowing\\n  - [ ] Drop retired quests and xp_log tables in DB v5 migration\\n\"",

  "Description": "\"Implement Fieldy conversation auto-pagination loop in fetchMore.\"",
  "EndLine": "88",
  "Instruction": "\"Replace _fetchMore method with auto-paginating loop implementation.\"",
  "ReplacementContent": "\"  Future<void> _fetchMore({bool clear = false}) async {\\n    final client = ref.read(fieldyClientProvider);\\n    var cursor = clear ? null : _nextCursor;\\n    var hasMore = true;\\n    final List<FieldyConversation> filtered = [];\\n\\n    // Loop until we get some conversations to display or there are no more pages.\\n    // Limit to 5 pages per fetch request to avoid long API hangs or rate limits.\\n    int pagesFetched = 0;\\n    while (hasMore && filtered.length < 5 && pagesFetched < 5) {\\n      final result = await client.listConversations(\\n        pageSize: 20,\\n        cursor: cursor,\\n      );\\n      final List<FieldyConversation> fetched = (result['entries'] as List).cast<FieldyConversation>();\\n      cursor = result['nextCursor'] as String?;\\n      hasMore = result['hasMore'] as bool;\\n      pagesFetched++;\\n\\n      // Filter out dismissed or already assigned ones\\n      final pageFiltered = fetched.where((c) => !_dismissedIds.contains(c.id) && !_assignedIds.contains(c.id)).toList();\\n      filtered.addAll(pageFiltered);\\n      \\n      if (cursor == null) break;\\n    }\\n\\n    _nextCursor = cursor;\\n    _hasMore = hasMore;\\n\\n    setState(() {\\n      if (clear) {\\n        _conversations.clear();\\n      }\\n      _conversations.addAll(filtered);\\n    });\\n  }\"",

'Unknown';\\n        final String? displayName = args['displayName'] as String?;\\n        final String body = args['body'] ?? '';\\n        final int timestamp = args['timestamp'] ?? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch;\\n\\n        // Deduplicate incoming SMS (carrier retries within 5s)\\n        final String msgKey = '${sender}_${body}_${timestamp ~/ 5000}';\\n        if (_processedKeys.contains(msgKey)) {\\n          Trace.log('sms', 'deduped incoming message', data: displayName ?? sender);\\n          return;\\n        }\\n        _processedKeys.add(msgKey);\\n        if (_processedKeys.length > 50) {\\n          _processedKeys.removeAt(0);\\n        }\\n\\n        Trace.log('sms', 'onSmsReceived',\\n            data: 'from=${displayName ?? sender} len=${body.length}');\\n\\n        // Watched-senders gate.   "TargetContent": "\"class SmsListener {\\n  static const _channel = MethodChannel('lakou/sms');\\n  final Ref ref;\\n\\n  SmsListener(this.ref);\\n\\n  void init() {\\n    Trace.log('sms', 'listener init');\\n    // Ask the host for SMS + contacts permissions on first launch.\\n    // No-op if already granted.\\n    _channel.invokeMethod('requestPermissions').catchError((e) {\\n      Trace.log('sms', 'requestPermissions error', data: e);\\n      return null;\\n    });\\n\\n    _channel.setMethodCallHandler((call) async {\\n      Trace.log('sms', 'channel call ${call.method}');\\n      if (call.method == 'onSmsReceived') {\\n        final Map<dynamic, dynamic> args = call.arguments;\\n        final String sender = args['sender'] ?? 'Unknown';\\n        final String? displayName = args['displayName'] as String?;\\n        final String body = args['body'] ?? '';\\n        final int timestamp = args['timestamp'] ?? DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch;\\n\\n        Trace.log('sms', 'onSmsReceived',\\n            data: 'from=${displayName ?? sender} len=${body.length}');\\n\\n        // Watched-senders gate.   "ReplacementContent": "\"  static Future<LakouDb> open() async {\\n    final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();\\n    final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');\\n    final db = await openDatabase(\\n      path,\\n      version: 5,\\n      onCreate: (db, version) async {\\n        await _migrateV1(db);\\n        await _migrateV2(db);\\n        await _migrateV3(db);\\n        await _migrateV4(db);\\n        await _migrateV5(db);\\n      },\\n      onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {\\n        if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);\\n        if (oldV < 3) await _migrateV3(db);\\n        if (oldV < 4) await _migrateV4(db);\\n        if (oldV < 5) await _migrateV5(db);\\n      },\\n    );\\n    return LakouDb._(db);\\n  }\\n\\n  /// v5: Clean up quests/xp_log, add billing method/frequency columns to projects\\n  static Future<void> _migrateV5(Database db) async {\\n    final batch = db.batch();\\n    batch.execute(\\\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS quests\\\");\\n    batch.execute(\\\"DROP TABLE IF EXISTS xp_log\\\");\\n    batch.execute(\\\"ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN billing_method TEXT DEFAULT 'hourly'\\\");\\n    batch.execute(\\\"ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN billing_frequency TEXT DEFAULT 'monthly'\\\");\\n    await batch.commit(noResult: true);\\n  }\"",
  "StartLine": "23",
  "TargetContent": "\"  static Future<LakouDb> open() async {\\n    final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();\\n    final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');\\n    final db = await openDatabase(\\n      path,\\n      version: 4,\\n      onCreate: (db, version) async {\\n        await _migrateV1(db);\\n        await _migrateV2(db);\\n        await _migrateV3(db);\\n        await _migrateV4(db);\\n      },\\n      onUpgrade: (db, oldV, newV) async {\\n        if (oldV < 2) await _migrateV2(db);\\n        if (oldV < 3) await _migrateV3(db);\\n        if (oldV < 4) await _migrateV4(db);\\n      },\\n    );\\n    return LakouDb._(db);\\n  }\"",

1: # Implementation Plan: Codebase Gap Report Resolutions
2: 
3: This plan details the implementation steps to resolve the 22 gaps and edge cases identified in the Lakou codebase audit. These changes are organized by priority to resolve crashes first, followed by usability gaps, missing features, and code quality improvements.
4: 
5: ---
6: 
7: ## User Review Required
8: 
9: > [!IMPORTANT]
10: > **1. Database Schema Update (v5 Migration):**
11: > We will upgrade the SQLite database version to `5`. This migration will:
12: > - Drop the retired `quests` and `xp_log` tables to prevent database bloat.
13: > - Add `billing_method` (`'hourly' | 'flat-fee' | 'milestone'`) and `billing_frequency` (`'weekly' | 'monthly' | 'bi-weekly'`) to the `projects` table.
14: > 
15: > **2. Calendar Integration Permissions:**
16: > We will declare the `READ_CALENDAR` and `WRITE_CALENDAR` permissions in the `AndroidManifest.xml` and include them in the runtime permission request dialog on startup or when the Planner screen calendar block is triggered.
17: > 
18: > **3. Client Intake Automation:**
19: > When the intake form sync succeeds, we will automatically parse the client details (Name, Email, Phone, Company, Billing Address) from the JSON payload, insert/update the `clients` table, and link the project to the client in SQLite.
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## Proposed Changes
24: 
25: ### 1. Crash Prevention & Permissions (Priority 1)
26: 
27: #### [MODIFY] [AndroidManifest.xml](file:///c:/Users/thoma/Dropbox/My%20Documents/Programs/Lakou/Lakou/android/app/
<truncated 9965 bytes>
ams/Lakou/Lakou/lib/features/settings/watched_senders_provider.dart)
148: - In `watchedSendersProvider`, log the exact error and stack trace using `Trace.log` instead of silently catching and ignoring parsing failures.
149: 
150: ---
151: 
152: ## Verification Plan
153: 
154: ### Automated Tests
155: - Run `flutter test` to verify that all existing tests pass and we introduce no regressions.
156: - Add test assertions to verify local date extraction heuristics in `filename_builder_test.dart` and `claude_extractor` unit tests.
157: 
158: ### Manual Verification
159: 1. **Calendar Permissions**: Launch Planner, verify calendar is queried without exception, and the dialog requests permissions if missing.
160: 2. **Task Editing**: Long-press a task in Backlog or Today, change its title and project, save and verify it updates.
161: 3. **Expense Listing & Editing**: Capture a mock receipt. Navigate to the Expenses list screen. Verify details are displayed. Edit the receipt vendor and check that the old file is deleted and the new file uploaded on Dropbox.
162: 4. **Milestone Tracking & Detail Edits**: Go to a project detail page. Verify the client info is displayed. Set the billing method to Milestone, add two milestones, invoice one, and verify the burn card updates to show milestone completion progress.
163: 5. **Fieldy Pagination**: Connect a mock Fieldy API key. Verify that if multiple recordings are assigned/dismissed, the page auto-paginates until unassigned records are loaded.
164: 6. **Intake Processing**: Trigger an intake check. Confirm the submitted form automatically populates the `clients` table and links it to the project.
165: 7. **Time Categories**: Track 5 minutes on "site visit" and 5 minutes on "design". Verify the weekly summary card on the Timer screen reflects the proportions.
166: 8. **Settings & Consultants**: Search for a contact in the new consultants directory. Pin the contact, verify it stays at the top.
167:

1: # Lakou Gap Resolutions Checklist
2: 
3: - [ ] Crash Prevention & Permissions
4:   - [ ] Add calendar permissions to AndroidManifest.xml
5:   - [ ] Request calendar permissions at runtime in MainActivity.kt
6:   - [ ] Harden ClaudeExtractor JSON parsing and update local date parser
7:   - [ ] Wrap meetings queries in try-catch blocks in today provider and project details
8: - [ ] Authentication & Triage UI
9:   - [ ] Keep dropboxAuthProvider loading during redirect flow
10:   - [ ] Add loading timeout to dropboxAuthProvider
11:   - [ ] Propagate token exchange errors to UI instead of silent debugPrint
12:   - [ ] Implement auto-pagination loop for Fieldy conversations
13:   - [ ] Implement carrier retry deduplication in SMS listener
14: - [ ] Task, Expense, and Milestone Management
15:   - [ ] Implement task updates in tasks provider
16:   - [ ] Add task editing bottom sheet to tasks screen and planner screen
17:   - [ ] Create expenses provider and expenses list screen (view, edit, delete)
18:   - [ ] Add delete file operation to DropboxClient
19:   - [ ] Create milestones provider and milestones editing section
20:   - [ ] Update project details view with client info and edit details dialog
21:   - [ ] Connect milestone invoicing status to project fee burn card
22: - [ ] Intake, Reports & Settings
23:   - [ ] Auto-parse client info on intake form poll and update clients database
24:   - [ ] Add Weekly Category Summary report card to Timer screen
25:   - [ ] Create Settings view with routes to watched senders and new consultants directory
26:   - [ ] Create consultants provider and consultants management screen
27: - [ ] Code Quality & Security
28:   - [ ] Make SmsReceiver methodChannel volatile
29:   - [ ] Validate shared file paths to prevent path traversal in share routing
30:   - [ ] Log watched senders parse errors to Trace instead of swallowing
31:   - [ ] Drop retired quests and xp_log tables in DB v5 migration

1: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
2: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
3: import 'package:path/path.dart' as p;
4: import 'package:path_provider/path_provider.dart';
5: 
6: final dbProvider = FutureProvider<LakouDb>((ref) async {
7:   return await LakouDb.open();
8: });
9: 
10: 
11: /// SQLite schema and accessor for Lakou.
12: ///
13: /// The live db file is intended to live inside the user's Dropbox folder
14: /// (`/Programs/Lakou/data/tpm.db`) so the desktop sees it automatically.
15: /// On Android the runtime file is opened from the app's documents dir and
16: /// synced up/down via [DropboxClient]; we never write to the Dropbox path
17: /// directly because Android sandboxing makes that unreliable.
18: class LakouDb {
19:   LakouDb._(this._db);
20:   final Database _db;
21:   Database get raw => _db;
22: 
23:   static Future<LakouDb> open() async {
24:     final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
25:     final path = p.join(dir.path, 'tpm.db');

4: /// A drafting-style dimension line — thin rule with tick terminators and a
5: /// floating mono label above. Replaces every LinearProgressIndicator in Lakou.
6: ///
7: ///   ├──────────●──────────────┤
8: ///              1h 23m
9: ///
10: /// Set [progress] from 0..1 to position the marker. [label] is rendered in
11: /// JetBrains Mono above the marker.
12: class DimensionLine extends StatelessWidget {
13:   const DimensionLine({
14:     super.key,
15:     required this.progress,
16:     this.label,
17:     this.color = LakouTheme.inkPrimary,
18:     this.thickness = 1.0,
19:     this.height = 32,
20:     this.markerVisible = true,
21:   });
22:

25:   final Color color;
26:   final double thickness;
27:   final double height;
28:   final bool markerVisible;
29: 
30:   @override
31:   Widget build(BuildContext context) {
32:     return SizedBox(
33:       height: height,
34:       child: CustomPaint(
35:         painter: _DimPainter(
36:           progress: progress.clamp(0.0, 1.0),
37:           label: label,
38:           color: color,
39:           thickness: thickness,
40:           markerVisible: markerVisible,
41:         ),
42:         child: const SizedBox.expand(),
43:       ),
44:     );
45:   }
46: }

49:   _DimPainter({
50:     required this.progress,
51:     required this.
<truncated 17 bytes>
quired this.color,

57:   final double progress;
58:   final String? label;
59:   final Color color;
60:   final double thickness;
61:   final bool markerVisible;
62: 
63:   @override
64:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
65:     final lineY = size.height * 0.7;
66:     final paint = Paint()
67:       ..color = color
68:       ..strokeWidth = thickness
69:       ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke;
70: 
71:     // Main rule
72:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(2, lineY), Offset(size.width - 2, lineY), paint);
73: 
74:     // Tick terminators
75:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(2, lineY - 5), Offset(2, lineY + 5), paint);
76:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(size.width - 2, lineY - 5), Offset(size.width - 2, lineY + 5), paint);
77: 
78:     // Marker
79:     if (markerVisible) {
80:       final markerX = 2 + (size.width - 4) * progress;
81:       final fillPaint = Paint()..color = color;
82:       canvas.drawCircle(Offset(markerX, lineY), 3.5, fillPaint);
83:     }
84: 
85:     // Label above marker
86:     if (label != null && label!.isNotEmpty) {
87:       final tp = TextPainter(
88:         text: TextSpan(
89:           text: label,
90:           style: LakouTheme.mono(size: 11, weight: FontWeight.w500, color: color, letterSpacing: 0.5),
91:         ),
92:         textDirection: TextDirection.ltr,
93:       )..layout();
94:       final markerX = 2 + (size.width - 4) * progress;
95:       var x = markerX - tp.width / 2;
96:       if (x < 0) x = 0;
97:       if (x + tp.width > size.width) x = size.width - tp.width;
98:       tp.paint(canvas, Offset(x, lineY - tp.height - 6));
99:     }
100:   }

105:       old.label != label ||

108:       old.markerVisible != markerVisible;

+final weeklyTimeCategoryProvider = FutureProvider<Map<String, double>>((ref) async {
+  final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
+  final sevenDaysAgo = DateTime.now().subtract(const Duration(days: 7)).millisecondsSinceEpoch;
+  final rows = await db.raw.query(
+    'time_entries',
+    columns: ['category', 'SUM(seconds) as total_seconds'],
+    where: 'started_at >= ?',
+    whereArgs: [sevenDaysAgo],
+    groupBy: 'category',

+    'site visit': 0.0,
+    'coordination': 0.0,
+    'admin': 0.0,
+  };
+
+  for (final row in rows) {
+    final cat = row['category'] as String?;
+    final sec = row['total_seconds'] as num?;
+    if (cat != null && sec != null) {
+      result[cat] = (result[cat] ?? 0.0) + (sec / 3600.0);
+    }
+  }
+  return result;
+});
+

{
  "CodeContent": "\"import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';\\nimport '../../core/db.dart';\\n\\nclass ConsultantRow {\\n  final int id;\\n  final String name;\\n  final String? firm;\\n  final String? role;\\n  final String? email;\\n  final String? phone;\\n  final String? contactId;\\n  final int timesUsed;\\n  final int? lastUsed;\\n  final bool pinned;\\n\\n  ConsultantRow({\\n    required this.id,\\n    required this.name,\\n    this.firm,\\n    this.role,\\n    this.email,\\n    this.phone,\\n    this.contactId,\\n    required this.timesUsed,\\n    this.lastUsed,\\n    required this.pinned,\\n  });\\n\\n  factory ConsultantRow.fromMap(Map<String, dynamic> map) {\\n    return ConsultantRow(\\n      id: map['id'] as int,\\n      name: map['name'] as String,\\n      firm: map['firm'] as String?,\\n      role: map['role'] as String?,\\n      email: map['email'] as String?,\\n      phone: map['phone'] as String?,\\n      contactId: map['contact_id'] as String?,\\n      timesUsed: map['times_used'] as int? ?? 0,\\n      lastUsed: map['last_used'] as int?,\\n      pinned: (map['pinned'] as int? ?? 0) == 1,\\n    );\\n  }\\n}\\n\\nclass ConsultantsRepo {\\n  final LakouDb db;\\n  ConsultantsRepo(this.db);\\n\\n  Future<List<ConsultantRow>> list(String query) async {\\n    final clean = query.trim().toLowerCase();\\n    List<Map<String, dynamic>> rows;\\n    if (clean.isEmpty) {\\n      rows = await db.raw.query(\\n        'consultants',\\n        orderBy: 'pinned DESC, times_used DESC, name ASC',\\n      );\\n    } else {\\n      rows = await db.raw.query(\\n        'consultants',\\n        where: 'name LIKE ? OR firm LIKE ? OR role LIKE ?',\\n        whereArgs: ['%$clean%', '%$clean%', '%$clean%'],\\n        orderBy: 'pinned DESC, times_used DESC, name ASC',\\n      );\\n    }\\n    return rows.map((r) => ConsultantRow.fromMap(r)).toList();\\n  }\\n\\n  Future<void> togglePinned(int id, bool pinned) async {\\n    await db.raw.update(\\n      'consultants',\\n      {'pinned': pinned ? 1 : 0},\\n      where: 'id = ?',\\n      whereArgs: [id],\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  Future<void> delete(int id) \n<truncated 588 bytes>",
  "Description": "\"Create the consultants provider file exposing ConsultantRow, ConsultantsRepo, and list queries.\"",
  "Overwrite": "true",

1: package com.greenstories.lakou
2: 
3: import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
4: import android.content.Context
5: import android.content.Intent
6: import android.provider.Telephony
7: import io.flutter.plugin.common.MethodChannel
8: 
9: class SmsReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
10:     companion object {
11:         var methodChannel: MethodChannel? = null
12:     }
13: 
14:     override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
15:         if (intent.action != Telephony.Sms.Intents.SMS_RECEIVED_ACTION) return
16: 
17:         // Multi-part SMS arrive as multiple PDUs from the same sender; concatenate
18:         // their bodies so the Dart side sees one capture instead of N fragments.
19:         val messages = Telephony.Sms.Intents.getMessagesFromIntent(intent) ?: return
20:         if (messages.isEmpty()) return
21: 
22:         val sender = messages.first().displayOriginatingAddress
23:         val body = messages.joinToString(separator = "") { it.displayMessageBody ?: "" }
24:         // Use the message's own timestamp (UTC ms), not wall-clock now — broadcasts
25:         // can be delivered late after a doze wake-up.
26:         val timestamp = messages.first().timestampMillis
27:         val displayName = ContactResolver.displayNameFor(context, sender)
28: 
29:         methodChannel?.invokeMethod(
30:             "onSmsReceived",
31:             mapOf(
32:                 "sender" to sender,
33:                 "displayName" to displayName,
34:                 "body" to body,
35:                 "timestamp" to timestamp,
36:                 "type" to "sms"
37:             )
38:         )
39:     }
40: }
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

1: import 'dart:convert';
2: import 'package:flutter_riverpod/flutter_riverpod.dart';
3: import 'package:sqflite/sqflite.dart';
4: import '../../core/db.dart';
5: 
6: 
7: 
8: /// Watched senders. Stored in `settings` table at key `watched_senders` as a
9: /// JSON list of strings. Each entry is matched case-insensitively against the
10: /// resolved contact name AND the raw sender number for an incoming SMS.
11: ///
12: /// Empty list = no filter; every SMS is captured (the current default during
13: /// shake-out). Once Tom has a curated list, only matching senders trigger the
14: /// capture flow.
15: const _key = 'watched_senders';
16: 
17: final watchedSendersProvider = FutureProvider<List<String>>((ref) async {
18:   final db = await ref.watch(dbProvider.future);
19:   final rows = await db.raw.query('settings', where: 'key = ?', whereArgs: [_key], limit: 1);
20:   if (rows.isEmpty) return const [];
21:   final raw = rows.first['value'] as String?;
22:   if (raw == null || raw.isEmpty) return const [];
23:   try {
24:     return (jsonDecode(raw) as List).cast<String>();
25:   } catch (_) {
26:     return const [];
27:   }
28: });
29: 
30: class WatchedSendersActions {
31:   WatchedSendersActions(this.ref);
32:   final Ref ref;
33: 
34:   Future<void> _write(List<String> list) async {
35:     final db = await ref.read(dbProvider.future);
36:     await db.raw.insert(
37:       'settings',
38:       {'key': _key, 'value': jsonEncode(list)},
39:       conflictAlgorithm: ConflictAlgorithm.replace,
40:     );
41:     ref.invalidate(watchedSendersProvider);
42:   }
43: 
44:   Future<void> add(String value) async {
45:     final v = value.trim();
46:     if (v.isEmpty) return;
47:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
48:     if (current.any((e) => e.toLowerCase() == v.toLowerCase())) return;
49:     await _write([...current, v]);
50:   }
51: 
52:   Future<void> remove(String value) async {
53:     final current = await ref.read(watchedSendersProvider.future);
54:     await _write(current.where((e) => e != value).toList());
55:   }
56: }
57: 
58: final watchedSendersActionsProvider =
59:     Provider((ref) => WatchedSendersActions(ref));
60: 
61: /// Synchronous filter helper. The receiver isolate can't await Riverpod, but
62: /// the SmsListener (in the main isolate) can read this once and call.
63: bool senderMatches({
64:   required List<String> watched,
65:   required String sender,
66:   String? displayName,
67: }) {
68:   if (watched.isEmpty) return true; // empty filter = capture all
69:   final s = sender.toLowerCase();
70:   final d = displayName?.toLowerCase();
71:   for (final w in watched) {
72:     final wl = w.toLowerCase();
73:     if (s.contains(wl)) return true;
74:     if (d != null && d.contains(wl)) return true;
75:   }
76:   return false;
77: }
78:

9: /// Lightweight in-app tracer.
10: ///
11: /// Why this exists: while Lakou is being shaken out on a real phone, crashes
12: /// and UI loops are hard to reproduce after the fact. Trace keeps a rolling
13: /// in-memory ring of recent events plus a persisted log file in the app's
14: /// documents directory, so when something goes wrong we can read back exactly
15: /// what the app was doing in the seconds before the failure.
16: ///
17: /// Three things feed it:
18: ///   1. Manual call sites: `Trace.log('capture', 'archive tapped')`
19: ///   2. [TraceNavigatorObserver] — every route push/pop/replace.
20: ///   3. Crash hooks installed by [Trace.installCrashHandlers] in main().
21: ///
22: /// Read the buffer in-app via the `/debug/trace` route, or pull the log file
23: /// from `<app docs>/lakou-trace.log`.
24: class Trace {
25:   Trace._();
26: 
27:   static const int _ringCapacity = 500;
28:   static final Queue<TraceEvent> _ring = Queue<TraceEvent>();
29:   static File? _file;
30:   static bool _crashHandlersInstalled = false;
31: 
32:   /// Optional listeners for a live debug viewer.
33:   static final List<VoidCallback> _listeners = [];
34: 
35:   static Future<void> init() async {
36:     try {
37:       final dir = await getApplicationDocumentsDirectory();
38:       _file = File(p.join(dir.path, 'lakou-trace.log'));
39:       // Mark a session boundary so the file is easy to scan.
40:       final boundary =
41:           '\n===== session start ${DateTime.now().toIso8601String()} =====\n';
42:       await _file!.writeAsString(boundary, mode: FileMode.append, flush: true);
43:     } catch (_) {
44:       // Persistence is best-effort. The ring still works in memory.
45:     }
46:   }
47: 
48:   static void installCrashHandlers() {
49:     if (_crashHandlersInstalled) return;
50:     _crashHandlersInstalled = true;

67:     try {
68:       final ev = TraceEvent(
69:         timestamp: DateTime.now(),
70:         tag: tag,
71:         message: message,
72:         data: data?.toString(),
73:       );
74:       _ring.addLast(ev);
75:       while (_ring.length > _ringCapacity) {
76:         _ring.removeFirst();

82:         print(ev.toLine());
83:       }
84:       for (final l in _listeners) {
85:         l();
86:       }
87:     } catch (_) {
88:       // Never let logging crash the app.
